videos_analizados.hook_template: My money rules as a (insert description) working towards financial independence. Money can buy you (insert item) but it can not buy you
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina moderna con gabinetes oscuros y encimera blanca
videos_analizados.hook_texto: my MONEY RULES as a non frugal 29 year old working toward financial independence
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy luminosa, ambiente de hogar moderno
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Especificidad y Contraste (No frugal + Independencia financiera)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Lista de reglas/consejos con prueba visual.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y hogareño
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad por el estilo de vida a la seguridad de un sistema financiero sólido.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en el audio, pero implícito en la descripción del video.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: My money rules as a non frugal 29 year old working toward financial independence. Number one: buy cheap before investing in high quality items. You know the saying your eyes are bigger than your stomach? Well that's me with pretty much everything. I love getting the best of the best but nine times out of ten I buy an expensive item and then I never use it or never wear it. So now I'll buy cheap to make sure it's something that I actually value. And if it is, then I'll invest in a higher quality item. And I know it sounds counterintuitive but this has saved me thousands of dollars and the hassle of trying to resell things. Number two: overspending is okay as long as you correct yourself afterwards. Spending money for me ebbs and flows. Some months I spend way more than average and some I spend very little. I think of spending on an average across the entire year instead of zooming into a specific week or month. The more strict I am about my finances, the more likely I am to overspend. And number three: never adjust how much goes into savings or investments to pay off overspending. For me, reducing my savings or investments to cover overspending is a very slippery slope. There are many months I have so many negative categories in my budget, but I know if the buffer in my checking account is positive, I don't have to worry about paying my bills or overdrafting. I'll just dial back my spending in those categories to make up for it over the next couple of months.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Consejos prácticos y realistas que rompen con la idea de que hay que ser extremadamente frugal para ahorrar.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Crear una atmósfera relajada y aspiracional
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina estética 'lifestyle' (preparar café) con transparencia financiera real (mostrar hojas de cálculo y números exactos).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos de las reglas en fuente serif elegante y capturas de pantalla de finanzas
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Millennials y Gen Z interesados en finanzas personales y FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early).
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Reglas de Dinero para la Independencia Financiera
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Mujer en la cocina con texto grande en amarillo superpuesto

videos_analizados.hook_template: Money can buy you (insert item) but it can not buy you (insert result). Here's how to develop a (insert skill) so strong that you
videos_analizados.escenario: Sala de estar moderna con cuadros artísticos y un sofá de cuero.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Money can buy you pretty, but it cannot buy you brand.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural frontal con luces de acento en el fondo.
equipamiento: Cámara o Smartphone de alta gama
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una celebridad tendencia y una dicotomía entre dinero y éxito de marca.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Análisis de experto sobre un tema popular.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos y zooms digitales para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido, estilo estético de redes sociales.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, validación de la experta, revelación (Aha! moment) y anticipación por más contenido.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Seguir la serie para ver más análisis de iconos.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Money can buy you pretty, but it cannot buy you brand. How did a 'mistake' in branding delay Sabrina Carpenter's success for 10 years? As your marketing bestie who graduated top 1% UCLA Business Economics with a background in marketing, we are going to explore how a shift in brand finally unlocked Sabrina Carpenter's ultra success after 10 years of trying despite having the talent, personality, looks... and how YOU can take inspo from her story and apply it to your dreams. Because branding is not about putting your name on a smoothie. If Erewhon put Sabrina's name on that smoothie two years ago, good luck selling any. Branding is not about how you see yourself, but how your future college, future boss, future boyfriend sees YOU. And I'm creating this series to analyze pop culture girl icons from a marketing perspective because of the power of influence they have over a generation of young girls.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Valor educativo y relevancia cultural (Sabrina Carpenter está en su pico de popularidad).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo sin distraer del mensaje educativo.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el chisme de celebridades con educación de alto valor y credibilidad personal.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos grandes en amarillo y rojo, subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Mujeres jóvenes interesadas en marketing, negocios y cultura pop.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Análisis de Marca: El Caso de Sabrina Carpenter
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Clips rápidos de Sabrina Carpenter con texto en negrita amarillo y rojo sobre fondo negro.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Here's how to develop a (insert skill) so strong that you physically can't stop (doing skill).
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación de estudiante, escritorio y sala de estar.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: here's how to develop a study addiction so strong that you physically can't stop studying.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz cálida de lámpara de escritorio y luz natural de ventana.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Afirmación hiperbólica y contraintuitiva (adicción al estudio)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Building in public / The 'X' for 'Y' formula
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con tonos cálidos en las escenas de estudio.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, sorpresa por la edad del creador, interés técnico y deseo de probar la herramienta.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow if you don't want to miss it.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: here's how to develop a study addiction so strong that you physically can't stop studying. I'm a 15-year-old high school student currently building Cookr. The TikTok for studying where instead of an infinite feed of brain rot, it's an infinite feed of practice questions. But in order for it to be like TikTok, it needs an algorithm. So today we built the algorithm for Cookr. So basically when the user likes or saves the question, it will be transferred to the library. And if they scroll on the question, the question will show up 10 questions later. If that made any sense. I'm also trying to apply spaced repetition into Cookr, so stay tuned for that. Cookr will finally be launched this Friday as a web app. So follow if you don't want to miss it.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad para estudiantes y curiosidad tecnológica.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ambiente de estudio y concentración.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el concepto de 'TikTok para X', que es un formato probado. La edad del creador (15 años) genera admiración y el hook promete resolver un problema universal (falta de motivación para estudiar).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos en el centro de la pantalla que resaltan palabras clave.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Estudiantes de secundaria y universidad, entusiastas de la productividad.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Building Cookr: El TikTok para Estudiar
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano del creador frente a una laptop con luz cálida de escritorio en un ambiente oscuro.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y contrastado, estilo cinematográfico moderno.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'LEAN'
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I woke up 30lbs overweight tomorrow and wanted to lose all, here's exactly what I would do. The 12-10-8-4-1 fat loss method. 12 is for your calories: bodyweight times 12. 10 is for your steps: 10,000 steps a day. 8 is for your sleep: 8 hours of sleep. 4 is for your workouts: 4 strength sessions a week. 1 is for your protein: 1 gram of protein per pound of body weight. Comment LEAN.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La utilidad del método; la gente guarda o comparte listas de pasos claros.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza una estructura numérica (12-10-8-4-1) que promete un sistema simple y fácil de seguir para un problema complejo.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're a young and you want to become financially independent by making money online then listen because because you have such a big advantage and I'll tell you exactly. You have to start by learning the most basic and essential skills are required. So things like sales, marketing, personal branding, team management, advertising, human resources and finances. These are all high income and if you just dedicate yourself to mastering these. Once you start applying them to different businesses that you'll wanna start or certain you'll have such a big head start. The first step is to build a foundation. So dedicate 2, 3, 4 hours each day or three times a week to actually learning these skills. Because these are the skills that will set you apart no matter what business model you want to pursue later and by learning these skills you'll build such a strong foundation by the time that you actually start getting into the work and into the hustling. You'll be unstoppable.

videos_analizados.hook_template: As an (insert trait) responsible (insert age) year old with a goal to (insert goal) here are 3 things I will never regret doing. Not to flex, but I'm fretty f*cking good at (insert
videos_analizados.escenario: Entornos urbanos (Toronto/Montreal), interiores de casas y oficinas.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: As a financially responsible 25 year old with a goal to retire in my 30s, there are three things I'll never regret doing to upgrade my finances.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Pattern Interrupt (Interrupción de patrón)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Lista de consejos (Listicle) + Storytelling personal.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido y vibrante.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con curiosidad, pasa por la validación del esfuerzo/sacrificio y termina con una sensación de empoderamiento e inspiración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito, pero invita a la reflexión sobre el riesgo y el esfuerzo.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: As a financially responsible 25 year old with a goal to retire in my 30s, there are three things I'll never regret doing to upgrade my finances. Number one is living way below my means. They say saving one major expense like on a car or rent is easier than pinching pennies on small purchases. So I've always lived with roommates or lived with my parents. In this housing market, sacrificing my freedom for a couple years in my 20s will be the single greatest difference in when I retire. The second thing I'll never regret is working two full-time jobs for four years. Hear me out, I get it's not for a lot of people, but I'll never regret it because by frontloading my investments in my early 20s, I've literally made it possible to retire like 20 years earlier. And lastly, I will never regret trying and failing at a bunch of dumb small businesses and side hustles. Yes, I lost a bunch of money, but I made a lot more. And more importantly, it taught me the value of the process. It literally took me 25 years to learn that finding your passion in anything requires trying different things and seeing what sticks. So if there's anything I've learned, there's no regrets. Success comes from people that say YOLO and go for it.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Valor educativo y aspiracional; la gente lo comparte como motivación o para debatir el estilo de vida 'hustle'.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ritmo constante y una vibra aspiracional pero relajada.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un hook de autoridad fuerte ('retirarse a los 30') y ofrece consejos prácticos que mezclan realismo (vivir con padres) con esfuerzo extremo (2 trabajos), lo cual genera debate y compartidos.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos grandes para cada punto (1, 2, 3) y frases clave como 'THE VALUE OF THE PROCESS'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Gen Z y Millennials interesados en finanzas personales y el movimiento FIRE.
nichos_compatibles: Fitness (3 cosas que hice para perder peso)
Carrera profesional (3 cosas que hice para ser gerente)
Viajes (3 cosas que hice para viajar gratis)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: 3 Consejos Financieros para Retirarse a los 30
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano de la creadora limpiándose los dientes con un hilo dental/herramienta dental, una acción cotidiana y algo 'cruda' que capta la atención por ser inusual al inicio de un video de finanzas.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Not to flex, but I'm fretty f*cking good at (insert skill/niche). This is what (insert object/item) looks like when you
videos_analizados.nicho: Marketing Digital / Crecimiento en Redes Sociales
videos_analizados.escenario: Interior doméstico/oficina minimalista (sofá gris, paredes blancas)
videos_analizados.hook_tipo: Afirmación de autoridad y prueba social
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Not to flex, but I'm pretty fucking good at social media marketing. If I had to start my Instagram from scratch, here's what I would do.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural brillante, bien balanceada
equipamiento: iPad o Tablet (opcional pero recomendado para el estilo visual)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de autoridad inmediata mediante resultados visuales (millones de vistas) y una declaración audaz.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de 'Hoja de Ruta' (Roadmap) + Prueba Social
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos (jump cuts) sincronizados con el habla
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y vibrante, alta saturación en los rojos
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con admiración/escepticismo, pasa por la claridad del aprendizaje y termina en la confianza de que el espectador también puede lograrlo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'SMM' for more information.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Not to flex, but I'm pretty fucking good at social media marketing. If I had to start my Instagram from scratch, here's what I would do. Number one: set up my profile. Username: keep it simple, name or business name. Tagline: have it be your name, line, occupation/industry. For your actual bio, use this 4-line framework. Make sure you have a clear profile picture and make sure it's a business account. Number two: study niche-related viral content. Write down your niche and break it down into 5 to 10 subtopics. Type in one of those subtopics, press search, go to the Reels tab and save any video over 1 million. Any video you would actually like to film yourself, make sure you save. Repeat this with all your subtopics until you've saved over 50 plus viral videos. Add all of the video links onto a spreadsheet, write down the hooks, the video formats, the video topics, the views. Study this shit, it's so valuable. Step three: Create. I would script 30 short-form content videos with those viral hooks and video topics, and add in my own expert advice. And then I would film them with some visual elements so they're not just a boring talking form video. Then at number four: Post. I would post one video a day and watch my account grow. This is actually exactly what we do to help my clients and myself grow and sell on social media. If you're a business owner and you want someone to research, script, edit and upload all of your content, comment 'SMM' for more information.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina autoridad inmediata con un tutorial accionable y gratuito. Utiliza el sesgo de 'secreto revelado' y muestra pruebas tangibles de éxito.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave en colores (amarillo/verde) y títulos de pasos (Number one, Number two).

videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La creadora hablando a cámara rodeada de miniaturas de sus videos virales con contadores de vistas de más de 1M.

videos_analizados.hook_template: This is what (insert object/item) looks like when you are using/doing (insert product/service). Are you still (insert action)? I’ve got (insert result) in
videos_analizados.escenario: Estudio moderno con fondo oscuro y persianas
videos_analizados.hook_texto: WHAT YOU'RE TOLD ABOUT CREATINE IS...
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal y luces de acento en el fondo
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Interrupción de patrón visual con un globo de agua y una afirmación controversial.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Explicación con metáfora visual (The 'Show, Don't Just Tell' pattern)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Contraste alto, colores saturados y limpios
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Escepticismo inicial -> Revelación/Claridad -> Confianza/Seguridad.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito: deja de preocuparte y usa creatina)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: What you're told about creatine is this. Like creatine makes you bloated. Yes, cause it holds water. But let me show you. Here's subcutaneous water. Plastic is your skin. The balloon is your muscles. Water in the bag represents water under the skin, outside the muscle. This is what makes you look soft and bloated. This is not what happens with creatine. With creatine, the water goes inside the muscles, not under the skin. It's intramuscular. It makes the muscle bigger and harder. It looks like this. It helps you work harder, increases strength and recovery. So stop worrying about holding water. Creatine is one of the most researched supplements.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Educativo y desmitificador; la gente lo envía a amigos que tienen miedo de usar creatina.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y la energía del video
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza una demostración física extremadamente simple para explicar un concepto biológico que genera mucha confusión y miedo en el fitness.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave resaltadas en blanco y negrita que siguen el discurso.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas que van al gimnasio, principiantes en suplementación.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Mito de la Creatina: Retención de Agua vs. Músculo
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El presentador sostiene un globo de agua amarillo mientras se vierte agua sobre él y aparece una imagen de un suplemento de creatina.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con sorpresa/escepticismo, pasa por el descubrimiento de valor y termina con satisfacción por el recurso extra.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Check the description, share this video, and follow for more.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 3 YouTube channels that will teach you more than any business degree. Save this reel right now and watch till the end for the bonus. Number one is Greg Isenberg and he talks all about business ideas from offline to online, how to use AI tools and he invites some epic people who talk about how they've made their millions using code and media. Number two is a case study YouTube channel called GrowthX which goes deep into the numbers of India's fastest growing businesses and tells you what's helping them win and their industry helping them generate crores in revenue. Number three Simon Squibb talks everything he's learned in business, sales, marketing and more in condensed hour-long videos that can help you get started with your first profitable startup. And lastly the bonus is Modern MBA. They break down every industry to tell you what's working in that business from scratch. Where's the money? Where's the competition? Where's the opportunity? I love their videos on cruises, airlines, AI and sneakers. Check the description for more details, share this video with a friend and follow for more.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica (la gente guarda y comparte listas de recursos)

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I seriously think I found the biggest personal branding cheat code in 2024. Hear me out. This font on your screen right now, that is a font you cannot download anywhere on the internet. It's my actual handwriting. The best part about this is you can actually create your own font from your handwriting pretty easily. All you have to do is go to calligraphr.com, choose the letters and symbols you want to use, and just download that template. Now you have to either print this out or, what I did, send it to your iPad. Draw each letter or symbol in its own box, snap a picture, and upload that to the website. Once you have uploaded it to the website, it generates the font. So all you have to do is download it to your computer and it'll be there in any video you use. And nobody else on the entire planet has this font. So thank me later!

He shows you exactly how to edit like your favorite creators such as Devin Jatho and Ali Abdaal. His tutorials are straight to the point and super helpful. videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - bien iluminado, luz suave de frente
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de etiquetas polarizantes (Smart vs Dumb) para generar curiosidad inmediata sobre en qué grupo cae el espectador.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Comparación A vs B (Smart vs Dumb)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación ligeramente alta para resaltar el papel azul
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad inicial a la realización de que pequeños gastos suman grandes cantidades, terminando en una sensación de empoderamiento financiero.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Si vives de cheque en cheque, son las pequeñas cosas las que te detienen.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Smart guy versus dumb guy when it comes to eating out. This is smart guy, this is dumb guy. They make the same amount of money. Dumb guy hates grocery shopping and cooking, but he still spends $300 a month on groceries. Smart guy spends $450 and he eats healthy and makes most of his meals at home. Dumb guy buys coffee every day on the way to work, spending $20 a week or $80 per month. Smart guy brings coffee to work and spends zero. Dumb guy buys lunch twice a week, costing him $15 every time, $30 a week and $120 per month. Smart guy brings lunch every single day. Dumb guy gets DoorDash once a week, that's $30 every time and he spends $120 per month. Smart guy meal preps at the beginning of the week, spends another zero dollars. Fridays and Saturdays, dumb guy gets dinner with his friends, spending $100 a week and $400 per month. Smart guy does the same thing but he eats ahead of time on Friday so he spends half as much. At the end of the month, smart guy has been able to save $520. Smart guy invests that difference from the ages of 22 to 30, getting on average a 10% return, and at the end he has $76,000 at the age of 30 that he can use for a down payment on his home.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Artificial mixta
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Llamado de atención directo (Call-out) basado en un hábito negativo común
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Guía de supervivencia / Glow-up de rutina
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la culpa por la inactividad a la sensación de control y organización
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en voz, pero implícito en el seguimiento de la agenda
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I see you doing nothing but bed rotting after school so follow this agenda to avoid that. So right after school you're gonna do a physical activity for 30 minutes. This can be as moderate or intense as you want, just move your body like even taking a walk outside. And when you get back home, immediately remove your clothes and turn on the shower water. You wanna give yourself as little time as possible to break your momentum and settle down cause that'll lead you into rotting. So you're gonna take your shower, put on comfy lounge clothes and prepare your study slash homework area. But you're not gonna do your homework quite yet. Now you're gonna make your dinner, then after that pick a show with 45 minute episodes. That's what you're gonna watch while you eat your dinner. That'll give you time to eat while being entertained but also some time to just relax after you finish eating. Now you're gonna grab a quick small and easy study snack like apple slices and peanut butter for example and then head to your study area. You're gonna keep doing the Pomodoro method to get through your studying session or homework until it's one hour before your bedtime. Now you're gonna prep everything for tomorrow: your school clothes, your school bag, your lunch. Now you're gonna brush your teeth, floss, do all the things and then head to bed and then you're gonna read until it's time to fall asleep.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Wanna be the first millionaire in your family? Then 2025 is the year you learn how to strip. Now that I have your attention, I'm Vivian, your rich BFF and your favorite Wall Street girly who's gonna show you how. S: Savings. 3-6 months of living expenses. Put it into an FDIC insured high yield savings account. T: Total Debt. Pay it down. Rank your debt from highest to lowest interest rate and pay down in that order. R: Retirement. Are you taking advantage of tax efficient retirement accounts like these? Set them up and max out contributions. I: Income. Maximize your income because it's easier to save when you make more. Ask for a raise every year. P: Plan for the future. Start thinking bigger, set 5 to 10 year goals and tell a friend to keep you accountable.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la intriga inicial a la comprensión lógica, pasando por la sorpresa y terminando en el humor.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: you can drop me a follow
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is how many bottles of wine you'd need to drink to gain a pound of fat. It's very common on a fat loss journey to think that you need to cut out alcohol otherwise you won't lose weight. But if drinking is part of your social life and you stop doing it, as soon as you reintroduce it, you're gonna put all of the weight back on. The best and only way to get fat loss results and keep them is to incorporate the things that you love into your fat loss journey in moderation. If you'd benefit from a balanced approach to fat loss where you can enjoy yourself and still lose weight, you can drop me a follow. When you have a drink, it can decrease your levels of discipline, which means kebabs and takeaways the next day, which might take you out of your calorie deficit. But you would need to drink five bottles of wine on top of your maintenance calories to gain 1 pound of fat. Obviously I'm not saying to you go and drink 5 bottles of wine because I don't want you to end up like this. I wanna show you that you can still enjoy yourself without worrying that you're gonna be fat the next day.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Everyone tells you to invest in a Roth IRA in 2025 but no one actually shows you how to do it. Here's a 45 second step by step tutorial that you can save. 1. Go to Schwab, Fidelity or whatever you're comfortable with. Click on open an account. Click on Roth IRA. Fill out this form, it'll take 5 minutes. 2. Go to your dashboard and click on transfer. Connect your bank account and set a monthly recurring deposits. I like to contribute $583 each month. You can contribute up to $7k if you're under 50 or up to $8k if you're older. 3. Buy assets that are going to grow over time. I like S&P 500 funds like VOO or any of these alternatives. If you max out your Roth for the next 40 years, you'll have around $3M with average returns. But that's not the crazy part. You would have only put in $280k of your own money. The extra $2M is FREE money due to COMPOUND INTEREST! I'll talk more about this in my next livestream. Tag your friend and I'll send you and your friend the link. See you soon.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're in your 20s, these are the 3 things you need to do so you don't end up broke and lonely by 30. First, stop trading time for money and start building a high-ticket skill. Second, audit your circle; if your friends aren't talking about growth, you need new friends. Third, invest 10% of everything you earn before you pay a single bill. If I were starting over with no money, this is exactly how I'd win. Comment 'START' if you're ready to change your life.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Here are some slightly unethical (insert industry/niche) hacks that you should know if you're (insert target audience). Here's exactly how you're gonna lock in if you want to
videos_analizados.escenario: Interior (Cocina/Hogar)
videos_analizados.hook_texto: 3 UNETHICAL JOB SEARCH HACKS to maximize your chances
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un adjetivo provocativo ('unethical') combinado con un beneficio deseado ('maximize your chances').
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Lista de consejos con ángulo de 'secreto/hack'
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural / Sin procesar
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Intriga -> Sorpresa -> Alivio/Validación -> Empoderamiento.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, pero el video responde a un comentario previo, fomentando la interacción.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are some slightly unethical resume hacks that I feel like everyone should know whether you're looking for a job right now or you will in the future. 1. Leave out grad date. You don't want being young to be the reason that your hiring manager immediately rules you out. Let your experiences speak for themselves without any potential ageism. 2. Exaggerate the length of short term work experiences. I exclude the months and only include the year in my job timeline. There are times where I've worked somewhere for three months and I put down 2020 and the hiring managers think that I've worked there the entire year. I've literally never been called out for it. 3. Don't be afraid to exaggerate your responsibilities. Obviously don't go overboard, but literally everyone does it.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica y naturaleza controversial que invita al debate en comentarios.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el flujo sin distraer del mensaje
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el sesgo de 'información prohibida' o secreta. El término 'unethical' atrae clics por morbo y la promesa de una ventaja competitiva en un mercado laboral difícil.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos de cada hack y el hook principal en la parte superior.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Buscadores de empleo, Gen Z y Millennials.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: if you have any more questions let me know bye
videos_analizados.guion_completo: here's exactly how you gonna lock in if you wanna move out of your parents house. first of all silence. you're not gonna tell those people you're even thinking about moving out until that lease is signed. this part is so important especially if you're in a dynamic similar to mine and a lot of people's dynamics with their families. they're gonna speak fear into your heart. it's just a fact of the matter. they're gonna tell you it's impossible. they're gonna tell you no stay here. if you've already decided that you wanna move out of your parents house move accordingly but do not let them know. second your finances. if you have a job keep that job. if it doesn't pay enough we're gonna work on getting a job that does or we're gonna do side hustles. one of the ones I use a lot is Rover. I love it basically especially if you're living at home you're getting paid to get out of the house and take care of somebody's dog. you get to be in somebody else's house you don't have to be around your parents. it's great. once you have your finances in order once you have some type of income you gonna jot down everything that you spend money on on a month. you gonna look at the essentials and fixed expenses. what are those? that's like your car insurance, your gas, the things you have to pay for or you're not gonna be able to live the life that you live. and then you gonna look at the variables like what do you spend on grocery, what do you spend on fun, what do you spend on drinks. and we're gonna cut as much of that as possible so that that extra funds that you were putting into there we're gonna put into your savings. you're gonna look at what you can afford for rent. no more than 30% of what you make. so then you can decide if you need roommates or not. and then we're gonna save up to be able to move out either now in 2024 where the prices are low or 2025. if you have any more questions let me know bye.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is the exact same video but the first got 71 thousand views and the second over 2 million. And it's all because of these two strategies. Number one: in the second video, the artist showed a hint of a payoff at the end. This hooks viewers by showing the final result first, which keeps us watching and boosting retention. For example, in cooking videos, you'd see the finished dish before the preparation. And number two... well actually before I tell you, if you're enjoying this, give us a follow. Alright, so video two nailed the ideal duration. Many artists say you should make videos as long as possible to show the art process, which isn't always bad, but if you want to keep viewers engaged till the end, video length is crucial.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: What I eat in a day / Full Day of Eating (FDOE)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado para que la comida se vea apetecible
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la frustración por no saber qué comer a la satisfacción y sorpresa de ver que se puede comer pizza y pasta perdiendo grasa.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Just give me a follow so you don't miss any of the posts like this.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're trying to lose fat and you haven't got a clue what to eat on a daily basis I'm gonna show you an example of what 1,800 calories looks like that actually taste good it's gonna keep you full and you're not gonna feel like you're on a diet. For breakfast you're gonna have a yogurt and fruit bowl comes to just under 400 calories and over 40 grams of protein. For lunch you're gonna have a sandwich, my pizza stuffed sandwich which only comes to 500 calories and nearly 50 grams of protein. And then for snack you're gonna chuck anything like this in the bin because it isn't gonna keep you full, you're gonna swap it out with another meal, we're having an egg avocado and bacon wrap which you can prep if you're not at home that is gonna keep you full all the way up until dinner time which we've got a sausage pasta which comes to just over 400 calories and has nearly 40 grams of protein. And then if you want a sweet treat after that you can have a handful of strawberries and a bit of chocolate for all that food it's only 1,800 calories and 150 grams of protein. You've only got about three hours between each meal which is gonna stop you from snacking. There's no salads here, there's no weight loss shakes, it's real food and proper sized meals but we've just been tactical with the ingredients and when we're having the food so we can feel satisfied throughout the day not wanna bury your head in the fridge every single evening. Every single one of these recipes are already on my page if you are trying to lose fat and struggle and find recipes that are actually gonna keep you full actually taste good just give me a follow so you don't miss any of the posts like this. I'll see you in my next video.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I’m gonna save you 20 minutes off your next workout with 3 simple tips. If I only had 30 minutes in the gym, this is exactly what I would do to get a full body pump. First, stop doing 5 different isolation moves. Stick to two big compounds like a squat and a pull-up. Second, use antagonistic supersets. While your chest rests, hit your back. No wasted standing around. Third, take your sets to 1-2 reps shy of failure. Quality over quantity. Do this and you're out in half the time. Follow for more science-based gains.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Make sure you pop me a follow.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I only had 20 minutes in the gym, this is exactly what I would do for an effective 20 minute full body workout. Using two dumbbells, I would start by hitting the quads and glutes with 10x squats, making sure I get full depth on each rep. I would then move on to 10 shoulder press to hit some upper body, focusing on controlling the weight throughout the movement so the shoulders really burn. Back to the lower body, this time hitting the hamstrings with some RDLs, big stretch through the back of my legs there. Add 10x bent over rows to get that hourglass figure, and lastly 20x Russian twists to really fire up the core. Rest for 60s and repeat three times. And if you've got a busy schedule and want more effective workouts like this, make sure you pop me a follow.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you just turned 21 and you don’t wanna work till you’re 65 then you should do the following three things immediately. This is me on my 21st birthday and this is what I wish somebody had told me. Step 1: Open up a Roth IRA with a company like Schwab or Fidelity. Step 2: Invest in a zero cost total stock market index fund like Fidelity's FZROX. Step 3: Invest $101 a month into this account because that's probably about all you can afford. Then sit back, relax, enjoy your life and let compound interest work its magic. By the time you retire you'll be a tax-free millionaire because it's a Roth IRA. But wait, there's more. If at age 25 you started investing $219 a month because you have a little bit more money, you would have over double the amount you had in the first example. Wild, right? Because it's a Roth IRA it's still all tax-free. I would have told my 21 year old self the point of investing is not throwing every money you can at the stock market and not enjoying your life while you're young, but putting just a little bit of money in while you're young and still enjoying your life can yield truly astounding results. If you found this helpful, give me a follow and I'll try to get you one step closer to your financial goals.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Side hustle tier list for 2025. Let's go. First up, dropshipping: F tier. It's overhyped, most products are low quality imports, shipping times are way too long and the competition is insane. Not worth it. Affiliate marketing: A tier. You can make serious money but you need a big following to get started. It's tough at first but once you build your audience, it's a solid side hustle. Social media marketing agency: D tier. The startup cost is almost zero and you can charge high monthly fees, but here's the catch: clients drop off fast because of those high fees. It's good but be prepared for churn. Amazon FBA: F tier. Similar issues to dropshipping but worse. There are a ton of regulations and you're stuck dealing with Amazon's rules. Not worth the headache. Finally, hybrid agency: S tier. This is the best side hustle by far. It's low cost to start, you get monthly recurring revenue and the profit margins are over 90%. This is what made me a millionaire and is the best business model today, hands down. And you can check out the link in my bio to get a free copy of my entire marketing system so you can do the same thing.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Don't hate me but I don't really mind boiled eggs, but let me show you a way that's way better. Boil them for seven minutes, put them in a cold plunge, carefully crack your eggs and place them in a container. For the marinade, chop a bunch of green onions, half a yellow onion, mince down garlic, one jalapeno. I use this app to save recipes like this one so I don't have to go back to the video. Add some water, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, MSG, sesame seeds and it should look like this. Now add the marinade to your eggs, cover with a paper towel and put in the fridge for up to 24 hours. When you're ready, place over rice and add marinade. Be careful, these eggs are called drug eggs for a reason. Wow.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox cenital para eliminar sombras duras y resaltar texturas.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Promesa de valor exhaustivo (guía completa) y categorización inmediata para retener la atención.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Listado / Categorización (The 3 types of X).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener la energía.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado para hacer la comida más apetecible.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad por aprender a la satisfacción visual por el resultado final (food porn).

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Every way to make fried chicken. There are three general categories. The first being the flour-egg-breadcrumb process. This gives a school vibe, or use corn flakes for a fry that's appealing. For katsu or schnitzel, in comes panko, coating that fights off sogginess from sauces. Those are the standard fried chickens. On to the coatings like just seasoned flour, great for chicken wings for a nice light coating that isn't too heavy for sauce. Swap for corn flour for Japanese karaage, which is much more about fighting off moisture. The last category involves liquids like buttermilk, which tenderizes the meat and acts as a binder for a much thicker coating. Alternatively, mix liquid and flour together for a batter. These are the main fried chicken techniques to build upon.

videos_analizados.escenario: Sala de descanso o oficina médica con archivadores y carteles informativos al fondo.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: the time that you have now in your 20s and 30s is so precious
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz de oficina/hospital
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Segmentación demográfica directa y apelación al valor del tiempo
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Consejo de experto 'detrás de escena' (Behind the scenes expert advice)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente frío
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con reflexión, pasa por la preocupación/miedo al ver las consecuencias, y termina con una sensación de control y motivación.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Go do something for your health today.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: The time that you have now in your 20s and 30s is so precious, you just don't realize it. I'm lucky enough to be able to see this. I spend my first two hours at work handing out cups, full cups of medications to patients that have chosen to live a sedentary lifestyle, they've chosen to consume alcohol regularly, they've chosen to eat low quality foods, not pay attention to their diet. If you don't take action on your current circumstances right now, in your 20s, in your 30s, before you know it, you're gonna wake up one day, you're gonna feel like crap, you're gonna be extremely unhealthy and you're gonna be forced to take medications that you don't necessarily want to or need to take. This is preventable. You are in control of your diet, you are in control of your lifestyle, you are in control of whether or not you end up in the hospital for chronic conditions that were caused by poor lifestyle management for decades prior. This can be prevented if you take action now. Go do something for your health today.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Would you rather grow old or join them in their journey to save their lives? Ye thi meri mummy 3 mahine pehle jinki gall bladder ki surgery unhealthy lifestyle ki vajah se bhot serious ho gayi thi. Aur tab maine soch liya ab CHANGE lana padega. 3 mahine baad, this is her now. 12kgs lighter. Mere sath roz gym jaati hai, high protein clean diet khaati hai aur pehle se bhi zyada young dikhti hain. Hamare parents ke liye hamse zyada important hai kyunki age ke sath unki muscles aur bones weak hoti ja rahi hain aur kharab diet aur na ke barabar protein se itna damage ho raha hai jo hame realise nahi hogi. Unke peeche pado, walk pe leke jao, unki diet sahi karvao kyunki for granted leke abhi ignore kardiya toh time aane pe hospital ke chakkar kaatte bhot regret hoga.

videos_analizados.hook_template: This is the amount of (insert noun) you would lose per day in a (insert state). If your in a (insert dream result) journey, this is exactly what you need to do to (insert dream goal) in # simple
videos_analizados.escenario: Fondo gris neutro minimalista con mesa de madera
videos_analizados.hook_texto: This is the amount of body fat that you would lose per day in a 250 calorie deficit
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal para eliminar sombras duras
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Demostración visual impactante con un objeto cotidiano (mantequilla)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Explicación de 'La Verdad Oculta' con demostración física
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido para la madera
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad inicial a la comprensión de una frustración común (la báscula no baja), terminando en alivio y empoderamiento.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en el video, pero implícito en la educación del usuario
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is the amount of body fat that you would lose per day in a 250 calorie deficit. It's barely anything and it's unrecognizable, especially because in the same day your body would have retained some water. The weight of this bowl is gonna be primarily water. Then the next day if you stay consistent you would lose the same amount. You'd also have similar changes in water retention. This cycle basically continues with every single day that you're in a calorie deficit. See different changes water retention. So after a week you might weigh yourself and nothing is recognizable. The way you stay the same, you're a little bit frustrated, but you have dropped half a pound of body fat. This is why you need to understand that losing fat and losing weight are two different things. If you're in a calorie deficit, it's gonna be inevitable that you are gonna drop body fat. It's impossible not to, but the water retention is always gonna be all over the place. You don't really have much control over this. That's why when it comes to losing body fat you can't just go off a weekly scale reading because to be honest it doesn't really show you that much. Remember that you lose a minuscule amount of body fat a day. It comes out to about 0.64 grams of fat that you would lose a day. You can see changes in water up to 2 kg. That's a huge difference. So you could be losing body fat, the scale weight goes up. Sometimes that's so hard to understand because the likes of Slimming World is that you're only making successful fat loss if the scale weight is going down. That's why you need other measurements of progress in place like taking photos, doing measurements, going off how your clothes fit, generally going off how you feel. These will show you much more than what this would.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Resuelve una frustración psicológica muy común (no ver cambios en la báscula) mediante una analogía visual extremadamente simple y fácil de entender.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're getting older or if you have a loved one that's getting older, there are three incredibly important things that you need to make sure that you can do physically in order to live a long and healthy life. The first is making sure that you can get up off the ground by yourself with no assistance from any sort of furniture or anybody else. This may sound simple, but one reason that falls can be so dangerous in elderly people other than the risk of fractures is because they can't get back up. Hence the success of the life alert, I've fallen and I can't get up commercials. If you find that you have trouble getting up off the ground, two things that you can practice are kneeling push-ups and doing some supported lunges. Next is you want to be able to balance on one leg for at least 15 to 20 seconds. The better you are at balancing, the less likely you are to fall. And this is a really easy thing to practice at home. Finally, you want to make sure you have the ability to rise from a seated position without the use of pushing off your thighs or anything to pull up on. Because that is a really good sign that just the strength in your legs is enough to support your body weight without the use of anything else.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's the difference between Computer Science, Software Engineer, Product Manager, Data Scientist, and a Cybersecurity Engineer. Most people attend university and choose Computer Science as their major hoping to learn how to code and make a lot of money. Half of them fall in love with coding and become Software Engineers. They get to code, go to minimal meetings, and build apps, websites, and software. However, some of them realize they hate coding but are very interested in inspiring teams, understanding user needs, and driving product innovation through strategic thinking. They become Product Managers. Some of the students who pursue CS not only enjoy coding but also love complex math. They become Data Scientists, Data Analysts, or Data Engineers. Some of them get further specialized to become ML or AI engineers. Lastly, some students enjoy breaking things more than making things, and they become Cyber Security Engineers. Instead of writing code, they wanna hack it. And the rest, UI/UX designers, game developers, cloud engineers, etc. The options are endless.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente frío para resaltar la nieve
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con la intriga de un 'secreto' legal, pasa por la seguridad de un plan estructurado y termina con una reflexión inspiracional.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I were in my 20s or 30s, here is exactly how I would avoid paying taxes on about 42% of my income legally. I'm Tyler, I'm a former financial advisor portfolio manager and now I make financial content for free so that you don't have to pay for it. Number one: let's assume I make $90,000 a year. Number two: I put $23,000 into a 401k and try to get the employer match. No brainer, and anyone who tells you not to is selling you insurance. Number three: put $7,000 in a Roth IRA, invest it aggressively. There is an income cap to contribute to these, so do it while you can and do it while you're young and in a super low tax bracket. Number four: I would max out my health savings account with $4,300 if I had a high deductible health plan. Invest it in low cost index funds and trust me, your future sick self will say thank you. Number five: and I turn around and take the $15,000 single filer deduction, tell the government I only made $47,700 bucks. Went from the 22% to the 12% tax bracket and at this rate will most likely become a millionaire in just over 15 years. Number six: so in the words of Ferris Bueller, 'life moves pretty fast, if you don't stop to look around once in a while, you might just miss it'.

videos_analizados.escenario: Escritorio de oficina con plantas de interior (estética tech/minimalista).
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Every data structure that you actually need to know.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal que elimina sombras duras en la cara y resalta las galletas.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Promesa de valor educativo condensado utilizando un objeto cotidiano (Oreos) para generar curiosidad visual.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de analogía rápida (The 'X explained with Y' pattern).
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturación alta para resaltar el contraste entre el blanco de la crema y el negro de la galleta.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad inicial por el uso de comida, pasando por momentos de 'ahá' educativos, hasta la validación profesional para entrevistas.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: You'll be thankful that you followed me.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Every data structure that you actually need to know. This is a stack of Oreos, it's a last in first out ordering, I can only access the top element before I can access the next. This is a queue of Oreos, it's first and first out processing, I have to eat the thing at the front before I can eat anything else. This is an array, it is perfect when you need to do fast lookups based off of the index of the element, but it sucks when you have to resize them. This is a linked list where each Oreo is a node and each arrow is a pointer to the next node. And if you're in a technical interview, make sure you know how to reverse this. This is a binary tree where each parent has up to two children each, makes it really easy for searching and sorting. This is a max heap, each parent is greater than or equal to its child and at the top we have the maximum element, as you can see this is thicker than every single other cookie. This is a hashmap, it stores everything in key value pairs and if you have an item with the same exact key, well you're gonna chain it to this thing called external chaining. This is how hashmaps stay efficient. This is a doubly circular linked list where you can go next and previous, so bidirectional traversal. Data structures are super important for technical coding interviews and the next time you're in an interview, you'll be thankful that you followed me.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's 30 seconds of money advice I'd give my best friend if she were starting from zero. 1) Track your spending every day. Shut up and just do it, it takes 10 minutes. 2) Save up one month of expenses as fast as you can. Put this in a high yield savings account. 3) Start investing as soon as possible. Open a Roth IRA and buy VOO, figure out the rest later. 4) Stop using credit cards as a safety net. Sorry but you suck at them, cut them up if you need to. 5) Make a plan to pay off any debt with an interest rate over 7% and stick to that plan. 6) Stop telling yourself you're bad with money and will always be broke. It doesn't help and I hate hearing you say that. 7) Follow AlisonTalksMoney on IG before January 19th.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're a woman that weighs 150 pounds but you'd like to weigh 120, let's go over a really simple diet plan you can follow to quickly lose 30 pounds. Number one, you have to be in a calorie deficit. Multiply your body weight by 10. 150 times 10 is 1500 calories. Eat that day one. Day two, be more aggressive. Multiply your goal weight by 10. 120 times 10 is 1200. Keep on repeating that 15, 12, 15, 12 throughout the entire week. Number two, you want to eat an adequate amount of protein. Multiply your body weight 150 by 0.7. That's 105 grams of protein a day. Eat that much protein every single day. It'll keep you satisfied, it's going to help you maintain your muscle mass when you're losing weight. Number three, you want to eat 30 grams of fiber or more every single day. That's going to guarantee me that you're eating vegetables, fruit, salad, you're eating healthy. Do those three things, you're going to be on your way to losing 30 pounds.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal para el presentador y luces de gimnasio para el B-roll.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're somebody who lifts weights and your goal is to lose fat and build muscle at the same time, then here are my four best tips. Number one, if you are doing three sets of an exercise, then all three of those sets should be heavy, challenging, close to failure sets, not just the last one. Number two, if you want to tone your muscles, then stop thinking you need to do light weight for high reps. You should be lifting as heavy as you can in the 5 to 10 rep range. Number three, you probably think you need to take short rest to keep your heart rate high, but that's actually the opposite of what you should do because you should be resting for two minutes to let your heart rate come back down so you can lift as heavy as you can in your next set. And number four, do your cardio after you lift weights or on your rest days. Do not do cardio before your workout. And if you want a totally free workout program from me, just comment the word 'Lamborghini' below.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you can't do a pull up, make your way over to a lat pulldown machine. You'll notice there's a weight stack. You won't be able to do a pull-up until your one-rep max on here is greater than your body weight. So to build strength in order to be able to do a pull-up, pick a weight that you fail at eight reps. Do this for three sets twice a week until you can do over 10 reps with that same weight. Then you're going to increase the weight. Keep doing this process and test your one-rep max once a month. Once your one-rep max is equal to your body weight, then you'll be able to do a pull-up. Let me know when this helps you get to a full pull-up and follow me for no-BS approach to health and fitness.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you can do 20 wall pull ups, you can do Australian pull ups. If you can do 20 Australian pull ups, then you can hang from the bar. If you can hang from the bar for 12 seconds, then you can do scapula pull ups. If you can do 8 scapula pull ups, then you can do one jackknife pull up. If you can do 5 jackknife pull ups, then you can do assisted pull ups. If you can do 8 assisted pull ups, you can do negative pull ups. If you can do 8 negative pull ups, you can do chin ups. If you can do 4 chin ups, you can do one pull up. If you can do 8 pull ups, you can do your first weighted pull up.

Today we are making buffalo chicken stuffed pitas. Y'all begged me for this recipe. And since my first four recipes went viral and broke the internet with over 70 million views and 3.5 million likes, I thought I would bring you something even more maximalist. Introducing buffalo chicken pockets. Like pizza pockets but way better. Stuffed with organic shredded chicken, cheese and hot sauce. videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is how to never lose a game of tic tac toe for the rest of your life. If you go first, you have to start in one of the four corners and for this example let's go with this corner. From here, if your opponent goes in the middle, the game will end in either a tie or you winning, but if they go anywhere else, then you are guaranteed to win with these few steps. So if their first move is one of these, your second move will be in this corner. But if their first move is one of these, your second move will be in this corner. And if their first move is over here, you can pick either this or this corner for your second move. Let's try an example where they go here. Now your second move will be in this corner and they will naturally try to block you from getting three in a row here, right? Now your third move, no matter how the game has panned out, will be to give yourself two options to win. So you can either go here or here because either one of them will give you two options to win. And that's how you win. Now let's say instead your opponent starts first. Now you might not win, but you can always force a tie. If they start in any of these, always put your mark in the middle square and then keep blocking from there to get a tie. If they start in the middle, it can go either way and let's just hope they make a mistake.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 3 levels of hot chocolate. Level 1: Heat up a cup of milk, add some sugar, add some sifted cocoa powder, whisk that thing up and never ever forget that touch of salt. Simple, basic, super nostalgic, absolutely nothing wrong with it. Level 2: Take some dark chocolate and using a knife just shred it up. Add some of that to a pot with some sugar, some salt and a bit of cream. And now just gently melt that until it looks like this. Slowly drizzle in the milk while mixing to reach this super thick and smooth consistency. I call this the French style hot chocolate. Level 3: Take some chocolate, add some brown sugar, cinnamon stick, a couple cardamom pods and you cover all that with some oat milk, that's my preference. And now you let it steep on very low heat for about 10 minutes. All those flavors will infuse the milk. And after that you can just whisk it up and adjust the consistency with more milk based on your preference. I like it thick but not too thick. Anyways, I gotta take a nap now or something.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: I'm a professional food hacker and this is every hack at Chipotle.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en el exterior y luz fluorescente de restaurante en el interior.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de una identidad experta ('professional hacker') combinada con una promesa de valor exhaustiva ('every hack').
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Insider Secrets / Money Saving Guide
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes secos rápidos para mantener la energía.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Colores vibrantes y saturados, especialmente en la comida.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con intriga, pasa por la indignación ante la 'estafa', se transforma en satisfacción al ver los trucos y termina en una sensación de triunfo al ver el precio bajo por mucha comida.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Next time you go to Chipotle, come back to this video (implica guardar el video).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm a professional food hacker and this is every hack at Chipotle. If you're Chipotle, please do not watch this video. I'm tired of people saying Chipotle is scamming them and giving them less food. Also, if you're new to Chipotle, you've come to the right place. I'm gatekeeping nothing. 1. NEVER do a mobile order. Workers tend to give you less food when they can't see you. 2. ALWAYS get the bowl. It can hold way more food than a tortilla. 3. ALL items can be on the side. 4. Vinaigrette sauce, it changes the meal. 5. Order 1 item at a time. If you list everything, they pre-portion in their mind to make it easier to close the bowl. This is my entire order: 2 scoops white rice, 2 scoops brown rice, both beans, extra fajitas. For proteins, always ask for half and half. Most of the time they give you double scoops but you pay for one. Every salsa, extra corn, sour cream, cheese, lettuce. Guacamole is free if you get a veggie bowl. Free water cup. Total price: $13.07. Next time you go to Chipotle, come back to this video.

videos_analizados.hook_template: I have a very long list of (insert noun) that I (insert action) that I gate keep from other people. But today I feel like giving back so I am going to tell you. I am going to teach you how to identify a good (insert
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación minimalista con cuadros de fondo
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I have a very long list of clothing brands that I shop at that I gatekeep from other people.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Suave - parece luz de ventana o softbox frontal
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso del concepto de 'gatekeeping' para generar FOMO y sentido de valor exclusivo.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Ranking / Recomendaciones de 'Secretos'
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado, resaltando el rosa de la bata
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la intriga inicial por el secreto a la satisfacción de recibir recomendaciones valiosas y estéticas.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Let me know if you guys want a part two.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: So I have a very long list of clothing brands that I shop at that I gatekeep from other people. But today I'm feeling like giving back so I'm going to put you guys on to some of these brands. 1. Set Studio has the daintiest, girliest clothing ever. It kind of reminds me of Are You Am I but it's less expensive and lesser known. 2. 7th & York makes the cutest velvet sets and dresses. I feel like you can wear them in the winter or the summer, I have one and it's stunning. I have lots of brands that make going out clothes that I gatekeep. Recently my favorite is Orphe because they make really simple tops but all the tops have push-up padding in the... yeah. So the tops look so flattering on but it also looks like you didn't even try to like have this body. Another going out brand that I love is Lucy's Lies. Literally the coolest brand ever, I don't know how it hasn't gone viral yet. The stuff is so affordable considering the fact that it's handmade and so unique. Similarly, the brand DeconDuarte. They make dresses and going out clothes and gowns, they kind of make everything. But every time I have an occasion, whether it's a wedding or a birthday party or whatever it is, I go to DeconDuarte. Okay that's it, let me know if you guys want a part two. If you really want it, I will stop gatekeeping. Love you, bye.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Nombres de las marcas numerados (1. Set Studio, etc.) y subtítulos dinámicos en la parte inferior.

Iluminación de estudio o cocina bien iluminada
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Promesa de valor inmediata combinada con un 'open loop' (el misterio de los pies) para retener a la audiencia.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rápidos para mantener el dinamismo.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm going to teach you how to identify a good steak from a bad steak. It's in your eyes and your feet. I'll explain that later. The first steak is a Select steak. Technically the bottom of the barrel, but only because it's super lean. That doesn't always mean it's a shit steak. Very uniform color, not a lot of fat marbling, a little bit more tough, not very juicy, won't be super flavorful. The one thing I will say about Select steaks is that I don't know if I necessarily trust them all the time. I'm going to tell you a couple things you can do to determine if you trust a steak. Mostly avoid steaks that are flaccid. Steaks that are really dark red, almost to a purple... it looks like it would be a richer flavor, but what that actually is is something called Dark Cutter Meat. This beef has a really high pH level, makes it taste worse, tougher, drier, and retains less moisture. Steak should be a little bit more pink. Another thing, if it has a weird fibrous texture, rubbery, stay away from that. Moving down the line, we have our Choice steak. This Choice steak today surprised me. Look at it. That's a fucking nice steak. Nice and thick, nice white fat, pinkish red, doesn't look watery to me. Clearly they're sourcing steaks really well here. You have to determine stores that you trust and stores that you don't. Local ranches, smaller businesses... fuck Cargill. Did anybody hear that? Our Prime steak. This is where I'm in my fucking prime. This is a nice steak. Prime steak is the best grade of steak. Most tender, nice marbling, the most juicy. And that is because it has the most fat. Dry aged steaks will have kind of moldy looking stuff on the outside. By the time you eat it, you cut that off. It can be dark brown, it can be dark purple. Don't be afraid of it. It also smells different, it will smell funky. This goes against everything you've ever learned about meat. This is an unusual scenario. And I told you I'd tell you where you're going to use your feet. If you walk into a butcher shop and it looks dirty, it stinks, leave. If you walk into a grocery store, dusty, dirty, stinky, leave. Also, fuck the USDA.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos grandes para identificar cada corte: 'SELECT $8', 'CHOICE $16.10', 'DRY AGED PRIME $38', 'DARK CUTTER MEAT'.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial brillante (estilo cocina limpia)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Promesa de valor inmediata mediante un ranking de productos populares.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Ranking de productos / Desmitificación de etiquetas
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y brillante para resaltar los colores de las latas
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con la intriga del ranking, pasa por la decepción de marcas populares, y termina con la satisfacción de encontrar opciones saludables.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I bought all the most popular energy drinks at the store so I can rank them from worst to best. Red Bull Sugar Free, Alani Nu, and C4 are your three worst options. These all contain sucralose and acesulfame potassium, two artificial sweeteners that have been linked to health issues. Bloom and Celsius are not any better. A lot of people think these are healthy because of the marketing claims they make, but these still contain artificial sweeteners like sucralose and some pretty harmful preservatives. Then we have brands like Red Bull and Monster. This has 38 grams of added sugar and Monster has 54 grams of added sugar. Do not buy these. Now we're starting to get into some of the better options like Focusaid. This is sweetened with a little bit of agave nectar and stevia. Angry Angel is also a good option sweetened with organic monk fruit. An even better option would be something like Ardor, which actually doesn't contain any sweeteners at all and I'm very curious on how this tastes. I don't even drink energy drinks. It's good, just tastes like sparkling water. It's actually very, very good. And the best of the best is something like Juno. This is literally just spring water, lime juice, monk fruit for sweetness, green coffee bean extract for caffeine and some minerals. Comment ENERGY and I'll send you my top favorite energy drinks!

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Ranking Energy Drinks from Worst to Best!, nombres de ingredientes en rojo, cantidades de azúcar resaltadas.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is harder than getting into Harvard. Welcome to ep 1: getting a Spotify internship. With a 0.08% acceptance rate, the Spotify Summer 2025 internships are now live. I've been working here for six years, but I actually started as a Spotify intern myself in 2019 after getting rejected. Last year, 63,000 applied and only 50 were accepted. So, how did I stand out? I made my application tell a story about who I am. In fact, I got an interview with a team that didn't even post their internship; they were just curious about my background. That's why it's so important to make your application authentically you. I've shared all the details of my internship process on my YouTube videos, and stay tuned for the next Reels where I'll show you how to craft your application so you can stand out.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave como '0.08%', 'Harvard' y 'Spotify'.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Now how much does it really cost to start your own cafe? Let's talk about it. Starting my own cafe Ep. 14. The first things I needed to... my licenses, that came out to $2,897.37. Next we have the poster signage and this costs $184.97. Deep cleaning: $1,378.75. Fire inspection: $750. Single door marketplace: $1,900. Sheet pans and a rolling rack: $150. New metro for our pantry: $200. Logo design: $750 and website design: $1,500. And last month for coffee equipment loan: $2,100. Toast POS hardware: $109.11. That brings our current total to $11,920.20. So am I nervous or anxious? The money that goes into this... honestly, absolutely yes. There's always that things are... like I expect. What? Whole thing super scary. 100% absolutely. Half the time I don't even know what I'm doing. In the end it'll all be worth it. See you in the next episode.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is why no one remembers you. Imagine this: a hiring manager looking at hundreds of resumes. Education, experiences... all they see is paper. But you are more than a piece of paper. As a former intern and current employee at Spotify, let's write your story. Your portfolio is the story of your career. These titles are your keywords to guide people through your portfolio and show them that YOU are the main character. Show the challenge you faced, highlight the actions you took, and prove the impact you made. Because we all love a Hero Story. Your portfolio helps people visualize who you are. Comment the word 'portfolio' and I'll send you mine.

videos_analizados.hook_template: I'm 20 which means my teenage years are officially over, so here;s everything I learned from the 7 most weirdest years of my life. If you're a (target audience) and you want to become (insert dream result) by (insert action) then listen to
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación oscura con setup de podcasting y múltiples locaciones exteriores (playa, universidad, ciudad).
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I'm 20 which means my teenage years are officially over, so here's everything I learnt from the 7 weirdest years of my life.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz de monitor/anillo en el estudio y luz natural/ambiente en los clips de vlog.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un hito personal (cumplir 20) para establecer autoridad y generar curiosidad sobre lecciones de vida.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de transformación personal y lecciones de vida.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes al ritmo de la música, zoom-ins digitales y efectos de interferencia.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Contraste alto en el estudio; colores saturados y cálidos en los clips de estilo de vida.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con nostalgia reflexiva, pasa por una fase de cuestionamiento analógico y termina con una explosión de energía inspiradora y empoderamiento.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: I'll see you in another vlog tomorrow (fomenta la suscripción/seguimiento).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm 20 which means my teenage years are officially over. So here's everything I learnt from the 7 weirdest years of my life. Well, it was mainly just one thing. You need to stop... wait, I should probably explain this. When you watch a movie or a show, here's how it normally goes: you get a character who's going about their everyday life and then something happens. It just happens and it changes everything. It literally creates the story that you watch. When I was a teenager, I always wondered if I was ever going to get that moment where something 'happens'. And at some point around 16 or 17, I decided to stop waiting for life to happen. I made good decisions, I made bad decisions. I made sure to go out, woke up in my own sick on my 18th birthday, but still spent weeks studying so I could go to my dream uni. I went to that uni even when my best friend didn't get in, and I left to get a job even when people said it was the wrong decision. I stopped leaving my life to chance and instead I left it to me. Anyways, I'll see you in another vlog tomorrow.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: El mensaje de 'tomar las riendas de tu vida' es altamente compartible para jóvenes que se sienten estancados.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are some signs you're in functional freeze. Let's find out if you're in a dorsal vagal shutdown in 5 steps. 1. You feel like you're on autopilot. 2. You're physically present but mentally elsewhere. 3. You feel heavy or sluggish. 4. You're over-scrolling or numbing out. 5. You feel disconnected from your body. This is your nervous system trying to protect you.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Do you have strawberry legs? Don’t waste your money trying to scrub it away, it's just going to come back. If giving yourself smooth skin causes irritation, bumps, and ingrowns, listen up. Most people think they need to exfoliate harder, but that just damages the skin barrier. What you actually need is a chemical exfoliant like Salicylic Acid to unclog the pores and a moisturizer with Urea to soften the skin. Do this twice a week and you'll see the difference. Check my bio for the exact routine!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: It's okay if your mom didn't talk to you about... The reason you can't get that dream result you keep talking about is because your nervous system is wired to view that new reality as unsafe. We repeat what is familiar, even if it's not what we want. To get there, you have to teach your body that it's safe to be seen, safe to succeed, and safe to be different from where you came from.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Lose weight and transform your body with these three tips. For reference I've lost 100 pounds and have maintained this for three to four years. Before we begin, let's not overcomplicate things. By staying in a caloric deficit and exercising, the body will burn fat. If you add the correct amount of protein to your body weight, you will burn fat and build muscle, which is called toning. My number one tip for fat loss is water and here's why: by drinking half your body weight in ounces daily, you'll suppress your appetite, boost your metabolism, and exercise will be easier and more efficient. My second fat loss tip, sure enough, is cardio. Walking 10,000 steps is the easiest way to burn calories, so hop on a treadmill, grab a book, or throw on a show and get to work. My last tip is volume and proportions. Lettuce is truly unmatched because you can add in as much as you want without a bunch of calories added. This results in you feeling full and satisfied. So if you're wanting more tips like this, just let me know.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Gym Girl Eras: Episode 5. How to build a shelf part 2. If you want to build that upper glute shelf, you need to focus on three things. One: Exercises. You need 2 to 4. Divide them between shortened position like hip thrusts, hyperextensions, and kickbacks, and lengthened position like RDLs, Bulgarians, and lunges. Two: Volume. videos_analizados.hook_texto: The lack of clinical studies on tallow isn't because it doesn't work, it's because big brands don't see a profit in it.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial/Mixta - Muy limpia, estilo laboratorio/cocina profesional

videos_analizados.color_grading: Limpio, brillante, con énfasis en blancos y tonos naturales
