videos_analizados.guion_completo: The lack of clinical studies on tallow isn't because it doesn't work, it's because big brands don't see a profit in it. Most skincare studies are funded by companies looking to patent their products. But for a product to be patented, it has to be novel, non-obvious, and man-made. Tallow is none of these things. It's simply beef fat, a naturally occurring substance that hasn't been chemically altered and has been used for centuries. Because of this, it can't be patented. And if a company can't patent an ingredient, they can't own it or monopolize it. So, instead of studying tallow, the beauty industry focuses on synthetic alternatives they can trademark, mass produce, and sell as exclusive innovations. And that's a shame because tallow is one of the most skin compatible ingredients out there. Its fatty acids are already well documented for their skincare benefits in their isolated forms, and in their natural state like in tallow, they are even more bioavailable, making it one of the most effective moisturizers you can use.

I was serving tables at a restaurant before I started what I'm about to tell you. It's not OnlyFans and I'm not selling pictures of my feet and literally anybody can do this. I've always known I was meant to make a lot of money but I simply didn't know how. So one day while scrolling on social media I found someone talking about digital marketing. Essentially all I had to do was promote products with the phone that I already had. I mean I was already wasting hours of my day on social media so why not monetize it? But I realized that the people that were being successful in this industry had skills that I didn't like marketing, sales or branding. Instead of letting that stop me, I did the complete opposite. I found a training that taught me exactly how to start my business from A to Z and it also gave me a product that I could start reselling right away. I didn't have to waste any time creating my own digital product and I could simply turn around and make my investment back. That same product taught me how to start my business, made me over $90,000 in profit in just a few months and it also gave me multiple sources of incomes. Social media is a wild place to make money and no you don't need thousands of followers to do this. I personally started a brand new account and grew it to over 100,000 followers in less than a year. So trust me this is not rocket science and if I can do it you can do it too. Just comment the word info if you wanna know exactly how I did it.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: How much do I need to make to buy a $500,000 house? Let's break it down. If you make $25 an hour, that's about $4,300 a month before taxes. With a 5% down payment, you're looking at $25,000 upfront. At current interest rates, your monthly payment including taxes and insurance would be around $3,800. Since lenders want your debt-to-income ratio under 43%, you actually need to make at least $110,000 a year to qualify for this. So at $25 an hour, you're short. Follow for more money tips!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Alright, so you said you make $30 an hour. Do you work 40 hours a week full time? Yes. Okay, so at 40 hours a week, that's $1,200 per week that you make. This is your gross income so before taxes are deducted which is what we as a lender use to qualify you. Now, you work a full year I'm assuming. So, 52 weeks out of the year. So, that's going to give you $62,400 a year of income. Now, to figure out your monthly income will divide it by twelve and that's going to tell me that you make $5,200 every month. Now, for a mortgage, ideally, we want to stay at 45% of that monthly income for qualifying which works out to $2,340. Now, I assume you have other expenses right now? Yes, I have a car payment, $400 a month. And then the minimum payment for my credit card is a hundred. Okay, perfect. So, you have $500 in other expenses right now. So, we want to subtract that from the 2,340 and that's going to leave us $1,840 that can go toward a housing expense. So, now let's do the math on that. Figure out how much house you qualify for. So, just from experience, to get to 1840, I'm going to estimate you can borrow around $200,000 at current rate of 7% over 30 years, which will be $1,331 for principal and interest and then you're also going to have mortgage insurance because you're going to put down less than 20% realistically, so that's about $84 a month on top of that you're going to have property taxes we'll call that 250 and then you're also going to have homeowners insurance we'll use 175 as that number so we add this together that's actually going to give us exactly $1,840, which is the amount of money that you have available to use towards a home so assuming this was a purchase where you borrowed 200 you're probably looking at a purchase price of 210,000 with 5% down.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is 7,700 calories worth of bread. But there is also 7,700 calories in 1 kg of fat, which means you'll have to eat all of this bread on top of your maintenance calories to gain 1 kg of fat. Sounds crazy, but hold on because you're just cutting out bread to see the number on the scales decrease and you're doing it all wrong. So let me break it down: eating a few slices of toast for breakfast or a sandwich for lunch isn't going to make you gain weight. It's more likely what you're putting on it. Bread isn't the enemy, restricting yourself from it won't fast track your weight loss, consistency with a balanced diet will. So enjoy your bread in moderation, stick to your routine, but be mindful of your toppings and fillings. No single food is gonna fast track your diet when eaten. And for those wondering, yes I did lay out all this bread just to get my point across. So if you enjoy content that cuts through the diet myths, hitting that follow will be super appreciated. I did count all these calories just for you.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: There is one thing above all that sets the top YouTube creators apart from the rest. Ideas. Ideas. Ideas. Ideas. Ideas. Ideas. So here's four tips to help brainstorm better ideas. One: Use Title Frameworks. The titles for some of the best YouTube videos all fall into very simple frameworks. Find a YouTube video that catches your eye and see if you can change the title and adjust it to your channel. Second: Make sure your idea is really simple to communicate. We got this one from Mark Rober. 'If I can't explain it in like one to three sentences to you where you're like: dope, that's a cool idea, it doesn't work for my channel.' Third: Time Travel. When we spoke with Austin Kleon, he told us when you're looking for inspiration, the way to be original is to go back further than anyone's willing to go. And lastly: Make this a daily habit. It's all about consistency. If you want a framework to help brainstorm new ideas for YouTube, comment 'ideas' on this video and we'll send you a link to our five-day YouTube ideation sprint.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is how I would invest $1,000 from scratch. First, I'd invest $400 into an S&P 500 ETF like VOO or SPY and this is typically the largest holding of any portfolio that I start, and it's up 80% in the past five years. Next I would add $200 of QQQ. That's the ETF giving you exposure to tech and software companies. It's done really well in the past five years, +128%. Then I would put $300 into individual stocks. So I would choose three stocks and put $100 into each of them. You want companies that you think will still be around in 40 years. So think of your Apple's, your Googles, or your JPMorgan's. It's important to avoid any penny stocks. My last $100 is staying in a high yield savings account in cash, waiting for opportunities. This split provides a lot of diversification to stocks via ETFs as well as some concentration into stable, long term companies.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're spending a lot of money on iced coffee then you need to try this recipe because it's super cheap and tastes like you're drinking a cloud. Grab a glass and add one teaspoon of instant coffee, about two teaspoons of honey and four teaspoons of water. You'll froth this until it turns into a really nice light brown color. Then froth together milk with a bunch of cinnamon. Use a lot of cinnamon. Cinnamon's good and this drink tastes the best with a ton of it. Add ice to the coffee then pour the cinnamon infused milk right on top of that. It's basically a freddo cappuccino but the honey and the cinnamon give it a cafe miel twist. The best part though, it tastes like a $7 drink but it's way cheaper and you made it at home.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Fraternal twins: genetic. Identical twins: black magic. A little over two years ago I gave birth to identical twins and I spend a lot of my time answering the same question: Do twins run in your family? I would like to take this moment on behalf of all parents of identical twins to explain the difference... Fraternal twins are two separate eggs fertilized by two separate sperm. They are just regular siblings who happen to be born at the same time... Identical twins on the other hand are not genetic in any way... It is a magic trick your body plays on you... One sperm fertilizes one egg and then it becomes a zygote... The zygote starts delegating... and says 'you be another me'...

videos_analizados.hook_texto: So you've heard of the viral 6-to-1 grocery shopping method, right? Yeah, I created that.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y luz de tienda/estudio en interiores.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Hack de vida / Tutorial de sistema
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rítmicos sincronizados con la voz.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y vibrante para resaltar la frescura de los alimentos.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con intriga por la tendencia, pasa por la claridad del método y termina con la satisfacción e inspiración de ver los platos terminados.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito, pero el video funciona como prueba de concepto para seguir su perfil.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: So you've heard of the viral 6-to-1 grocery shopping method, right? Yeah, I created that. So you want to save money grocery shopping and time cooking, you gotta try this method. Let's get going. For this method, you're going to grab 6 veggies, 5 fruits, 4 proteins, 3 starches, 2 sauces or spreads, and 1 fun item for each person in your family. Let's go check out and it's time to cook. And just like that, I spent 80 dollars for all these groceries and I'm making 6 meals using my 6-to-1 grocery shopping method. For breakfast, I made a broccoli cheddar quiche and breakfast pasta. For lunch, I made pesto sausage tostadas and barbecue salmon quesadillas. For dinner, I made a baked ziti and a big old salmon salad. All for under 80 bucks.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica y ahorro de dinero.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y la energía del video.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un sistema mnemotécnico simple (6-5-4-3-2-1) para resolver un problema universal: el costo de la vida y la falta de tiempo.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Aparecen los números y categorías (6 veggies, 5 fruits, etc.) a medida que los menciona.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas que cocinan en casa, familias con presupuesto limitado, entusiastas del meal prep.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: El Método de Compra 6-to-1 de Chef Will Coleman
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador en un estacionamiento con capturas de pantalla de artículos de prensa (CNN, Food & Wine) y una búsqueda de Google que confirma su nombre.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: What food I'd make for you based on your favorite sport
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox profesional para resaltar colores de la comida
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una premisa relacional ('yo haría esto por ti') vinculada a una identidad ('tu deporte favorito')
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Ranking/Categorización basada en personalidades.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturación alta, tonos cálidos para hacer la comida más apetecible
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, risa o indignación por los comentarios sarcásticos, y deseo final por la comida.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito ('You're welcome' al final para cerrar el ciclo de la serie)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: What food I'd make for you based on your favorite sport. Football. This dish is for people who like to watch grown men in tights professionally hug each other on TV. I know you're supposed to be tough but there's nothing that screams masculinity more than 22 dudes run after a ball. You need to get your priorities straight if you're more concerned about your favorite football team winning than losing your beer belly. Instead of cheering for the running back, you should run back to the gym and run laps to make sure that you don't end up a big back. And for the Swifties who pretend to be interested in football because Taylor Swift is now dating Travis Kelce or whatever, I'm not surprised with you pretending to like something because I'd never imagine seeing this many people pretend to like Taylor Swift's last album. I'm happy that you at least got the halftime show to look forward to because I know that you got no clue about what's happening on the football field. Now some football fans are more loyal to their teams than they are to their spouse. You're still rooting for your team like a Drake fan who turned the TV off during the halftime show. At least you're not a bandwagon Chiefs fan who is now a proud supporter of the Eagles. So because you always need more protein, I'm making you cheesy birria sliders. You're welcome.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Los usuarios etiquetan a amigos que encajan en las descripciones (fans de fútbol o Swifties).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ritmo constante sin distraer del discurso
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el 'rage bait' (provocación) humorístico contra nichos muy apasionados (fans de la NFL y Swifties) para generar comentarios, combinado con una producción visual de cocina impecable.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos dinámicos con fuentes coloridas para palabras clave como 'Football' y 'Welcome'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes adultos, fans de la cocina, fans de los deportes, usuarios de redes sociales que disfrutan del humor ácido.
nichos_compatibles: Signos del zodiaco (comida según tu signo)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Cheesy Birria Sliders para Fans del Fútbol Americano
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador sentado en un sofá naranja con un micrófono, mirando directamente a cámara con texto superpuesto.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Here are (insert #) of (insert noun) from (insert noun) that I am going to (insert verb) in (insert time frame). How we saved (insert $) on our (insert noun), and one
videos_analizados.escenario: Sala de estar con una estantería de libros al fondo, reforzando la autoridad en el nicho literario.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Here are ten books from different decades that I'm going to read in the next 10 weeks.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial cálida - ambiente hogareño y acogedor.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una lista específica y un marco de tiempo determinado para generar compromiso inmediato.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Reto de 10 semanas / Unboxing de misterio.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con tonos cálidos.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial -> Intriga por el misterio -> Anticipación durante el desempaque -> Satisfacción/Sorpresa con la revelación -> Motivación para participar.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: If you want to join on on this tour of this century, totally join in.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are ten books from different decades that I'm going to read in the next 10 weeks. But the catch is, I don't know what any of them are. My wife did a ton of research, and she picked all ten of these books. She wrapped them for me and this is the first time I'm actually holding them. So every week, I'm going to unwrap a book, and I'm going to read the book that week and let you know what I think about it. So let's unwrap the first one. Okay, so this one's really small. I told her to try to make them under 300 pages. I think there's one exception in here, but this one is really, really small. Okay, here we go. This is the very first book: It's Sherlock Holmes, The Hound of the Baskervilles. I've never read any Sherlock. I've seen the BBC show. I've certainly heard of this book, but I've never actually gotten around to read it. It looks like it's only 220-ish pages with, like, somewhat small-ish font, so I bet I can knock this out in a couple of days, maybe three days. So whenever I'm done, I'll get back and we'll open the next one. I'm going to be doing this weekly, so if you want to join on on this tour of this century, totally join in. That'd be awesome.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La naturaleza del reto invita a otros lectores a replicarlo o a comentar si han leído el libro revelado.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Enfocar la atención totalmente en la voz y el sonido del papel.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'misterio' y 'unboxing' aplicado a la literatura (BookTok/Bookstagram). El hecho de que otra persona elija los libros añade un factor de curiosidad sobre el gusto de la esposa y la reacción del creador.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave en amarillo.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Lectores, entusiastas de los libros, personas buscando retos de productividad o lectura.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Desafío de Lectura a Ciegas: 10 Décadas, 10 Semanas
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador sostiene una pila de libros envueltos en papel kraft marrón, ocultando sus portadas.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is everything I bought at Costco today to stay fit at 25. Look at the size of this tube. Was gonna buy avocados but $10 for 6 is ridiculous. Bananas for $1.99 because that's value. Persimmons for $5 because that's also value. Grape tomatoes for $8. Blueberries to put in yogurt for $5.49. These salad kits kind of slap, I can make four meals out of them, so we'll get this one for $9. Hey could I get that like tube of ground beef? Perfect, thank you. It is $50 but $4.89 per pound (90% lean). Also gotta get some chicken thighs because we can't be eating ground beef every meal. Celsius, I swear I'm not addicted, I can quit tomorrow. This sauce for seasoning or dressing. My friend told me this is gas so we'll try it, it's $12 for nine. 42 bars of protein for $14.79. And everything in total only came out to $167.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I present a challenge. Leg Day (No weights). I may not have monster thighs, but I do know a thing or two about training for stuff like this. First exercise: The squat. Easy. Medium, remember to do both sides. Hard. Oh yeah. And hardest. Pick the variation that you can do five or more reps of and get it done. Great, so these quads are covered and now we'll move on to our hamstrings. This is the hamstring bridge. This one is easy, medium, hard, and hardest. Then of course you can rest, unless you want to get flexible too. Random scene change. Easy, medium, hard. Wait, what's after that part? The easy, medium, hard, and hardest. After three sets of these your legs will be feeling like noodles. And hey, don't overdo it. And it's okay if you only do the easy stuff. We all have to start somewhere and it might as well be here. Stay flexy.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Es una rutina útil que los usuarios guardan para entrenar luego o comparten con amigos que entrenan en casa.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'niveles de dificultad' que genera retención (la gente quiere ver cuál es el nivel más difícil) y ofrece utilidad inmediata sin necesidad de equipo.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Niveles (Easy, Medium, Hard, Hardest) y nombres de ejercicios.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is every way to make toast. The classic: I find adding a little drizzle and a pinch of salt makes it shine. Simple with PB and jelly, using the back of a spoon makes it pretty and topping with pistachios. Next is avocado toast, egg and enjoying this while remembering paying 20 dollars at a gentrified cafe for it. Egg in a hole: butter both sides before cracking an egg and cooking on high heat. You'll be making a 100 names for it by next week. French toast: starting with a simple custard, vanilla, soaking a piece of challah or any stale bread for a maximum of 10 seconds and cooking with some butter until golden brown. Custard toast: a quick custard in the center of the toast, making a crusty divot in the middle of soft bread. Fry at 350F and starting with raspberries before adding any toppings. Something fancy with zero effort: ricotta in a piping bag or a Ziploc bag will work. Lemon zest and honey combination is unbelievably good. For a dairy-free option that tastes way better, blend tofu and olive oil together with beet juice for a pink spread.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This small fish gets eaten by a bigger fish which gets eaten by an even bigger fish which gets eaten by an even bigger fish and that's how the food chain moves. But let's rewind. Small fish like sardines or anchovies which are typically filter feeders meaning they eat plankton and algae but also tiny amounts of microplastics, biotoxins, and mercury that's floating around in the water. Then a mid-size fish like a mackerel or snapper doesn't just eat one sardine. It eats around 500 of these small fish per year. And with every bite it absorbs the nutrients from the small fish but also the toxins that the small fish collected. Now a tuna, a much bigger fish eats 100 of these mid-sized fish per year. This is why a big tuna compared to a sardine can have over 50 times more mercury. This process is called bioaccumulation and that's when toxins build up over time but when a predator eats hundreds of contaminated fish, those toxins multiply and this process is called biomagnification and that's why the biggest fish like sharks, swordfish, and king mackerel have the highest mercury levels in the ocean. The bigger the fish, the bigger the risk. So when it comes to fish, I like to follow SMASH. The five fish that are low in mercury and high in omega-3s. That's Sardines, Mackerel, Anchovies, Salmon, and Herring. These fish sit lower on the food chain meaning they don't accumulate nearly as many toxins making them some of the best choices for both nutrition and sustainability.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y etiquetas de conceptos (BIOACCUMULATION, BIOMAGNIFICATION).
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en salud, nutrición, fitness y sostenibilidad.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Explicación de Bioacumulación y Biomagnificación de Mercurio en Peces
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Manos moviendo recortes de peces que se superponen simulando que uno come al otro sobre un fondo de cartón.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: In this video see if you can tell what’s real and what’s AI. Thanks Marissa. Okay. This bouncy house? It’s AI unfortunately. Looks fun though. Which lamp post is real? That one’s real, that one’s AI. The bench, it’s real. It’s everyone’s favorite game: purse or potato. Which one’s real? This one’s real. This one’s actually not a potato, it’s a yam. Are these flowers AI or real? They’re real. Is this gazebo real? No, it’s actually this gazebo thing. What about this easel? The easel is real, so if anyone was looking for a beautiful piece of art to hang in their home, you can’t hang this piece because the art was AI generated. Sorry. There is one AI banana in this bunch. Can you find it? It’s this one. It’s actually a potato.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I avoid all toxic food. Here's how to clean up your protein. Get rid of the cage-free eggs. These birds have very limited space to move. Switch to organic pasture-raised. Roaming the pastures, eating the bugs, the worms, the dirt, living that natural life. Look at that color difference. We're going wild-caught over farm-raised. Less contaminants and way more nutrients. Even though you're getting organic chicken, these birds are often fed that standard diet of organic grains, whereas those pasture-raised chickens, they're on the pastures getting their own food. The quality of your food fully depends on what that animal is eating. This beef is not for me. I'm going with 100% grass-fed beef. More Omega-3s, antioxidants, and free of those hormones and antibiotics. This may sound nuts, but a lot of these trail mixes have seed oils. You gotta be careful for which ones you're getting. I'm going raw organic nuts always. And then we got that cheese. I'm not going pasteurized, I like it raw. Clean up that protein for amazing health.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are 5 questions I ask before buying anything. Question number 1: Can I afford this thing? And here's a hint: if you have to charge it to a credit card, you can't afford it. Question 2: Can I pay the actual cost for the thing? You see, the cost of a thing goes way beyond its price tag. You have to care for the thing, and water the thing, and worry about the thing, and the thing takes up space. The true cost goes way beyond the price tag. Question number 3: Will it add value to my life? In other words, does it serve a purpose? Or does it amplify my joy? If not, I'm not taking it home. Question number 4: Is this the best use of my money? If not, I'm going to spend the money on something else. Question number 5: Can I get by without it for a while? If so, I'll leave it on the shelf. I can come back tomorrow and it will still be here.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Since potato chip bags from the store are basically half air, you might as well learn how to make them at home and even better. And I bet if you had to do a side-by-side blind test, you wouldn't be able to tell the difference. I mean, just listen to this crunch. We're going to start with some russet potatoes, wash and peel them really well. Then we're going to whip out our mandoline slicer. Use the ridge cut for ruffle style chips and the regular blade for some Lays chips. Slice them thin and as always, be careful while using the mandoline. Let them soak in ice water and rinse a few times until the water runs clear. And then you're going to pat each chip dry. It's a bit tedious, but this will ensure that they turn out super crispy. Heat up your oil to 320 Fahrenheit and you want to fry in small batches so that the temperature doesn't cool down too much. You can stir them and push them along the edges of the pot to help encourage the folds. Once they hold their shape and turn that beautiful deep yellow color, drain on the wire rack, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and enjoy.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Ever wonder how the same ingredients can result in drastically different cookies? Here is your guide into what causes different variations in cookies and what to look for in a recipe. For a more standard chewy soft cookie that spreads, look for a cookie recipe that has a high ratio of fat and sugar to flour. The sugar and fat will cause the cookies to spread more as there isn't a strong structure to hold the shape. For thin, delicate, airy, crunchy cookie, choose a recipe that has a low protein flour and reverse creaming. Reverse creaming is when you mix flour and fat together before adding liquids or sugar. The fat coats the flour creating this barrier from liquid, giving the delicate texture. For a thick cookie, choose a recipe that has a higher ratio of flour to sugar and fat. Cream the butter and sugar together and you want to make sure the recipe has a high protein flour like bread or whole wheat. Whole wheat absorbs more liquid than all-purpose, so it's going to create a chewy cookie that will spread less.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Let's talk upside down cake pops. If your cake pops look like this, and like this, pay attention. Cake pops, you need your chocolate at a 96 temperature wise so just make sure it's not super hot. Sheet with parchment paper, wax paper, and then a cake pop stand and obviously some sticks. You're going to start by placing your cake pops the way we want them, right? And we're going to take our stick with a little bit of chocolate, we're just going to insert them in. Make sure that the stick is standing up straight and we're going to let this dry. You work fast, you can do all of your cake pops. If you work a little bit slow, only do a few at a time because if you guys don't know, if air starts to hit your naked cake pop, it'll start to crack because it's drying out. So, going to dip, shake off the excess. Once we've done that, we're going to place it on the cake pop stand and you're going to let this dry for just a few seconds depending on how cold or hot your chocolate is. So if you guys can see, it's already drying because my chocolate is not very hot. So it's setting from the bottom there and it's just a little bit wet there, you guys see the little shine? Now I can place it down. There we go, and see it's standing up straight and I'll show you guys the bottom how it looks. Because my chocolate is a little bit warmer, I can do two at a time before I have to place it down. But because my chocolate is a little bit on the colder side right now, it's starting to set pretty quickly. But if I really want to, I can dip another one while that one's drying. You just want to keep an eye on it, you don't want to let it dry completely or else it's not going to stand up straight. Alright, so now that I've set that one there, this one's basically almost ready for me to flip it. So like I said, you guys can see from there, it's losing some of its shine here, it's a little bit shiny, that's when I'm going to flip it and put it on my sheet. Let me show you guys this one. So here's the bottom, it has full coverage, there's no cake dough showing or anything like that. It doesn't have a big old puddle of chocolate, it looks super clean and it will stand up straight. Hope this is helpful, I hope you guys get to try it out.

videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de autoridad inmediata ('fui a la escuela') y ahorro de tiempo para el espectador ('para que tú no tengas que hacerlo').
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de experto simplificado.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturación alta, tonos cálidos y limpios.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con curiosidad y confianza, pasa por la fase educativa de 'puedo hacerlo' y termina con la satisfacción visual del producto terminado.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: I hope you can do this too (Implícito: intenta la receta).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Today I'm going to show you how to make the perfect macarons because I went to pastry school so you don't have to. Now, all French macaron shells are actually only made with four simple ingredients, two of which are just sugar. So it's all in the technique and today we are going to tackle it with a French meringue, which is by far the easiest way to do it. Now, I like to make my French meringue the lazy way, which is just by dumping the egg whites and the granulated sugar together in the stand mixer. Now it's going to take much longer for the sugar to dissolve completely into the egg whites, but it's going to lead to a more stable meringue. So while that's whipping up, because it's going to take a while, we're going to make the tant pour tant, which are just equal parts by weight of almond flour and powdered sugar. We're going to blend it up in a food processor so that way they're evenly combined and also so all the powder is nice and fine. You can skip this step if you don't have a food processor, you can push everything through a fine mesh sieve instead, it's just going to be a little bit lumpy. Let's go back to check on our meringue and right now it is super stiff and you can see you can lift it over your head, nothing is falling out. Now this is the super important part where we mix the flours and the meringue together. And we're going to do this step called the macaronage, which is where you beat the dough aggressively together. This is where it gets hard people, because I'm just going to tell you the consistency should be like lava. Look, I know you don't know what lava is, but actually err on the side of under mixing if you're unsure because when you pipe them out you're continuing to deflate the batter as you squeeze it through the piping bag. Once all your little cookie shells are piped out, we're going to leave them out until it forms a skin on the top and then we're going to stick them in the oven. Let them cool, enjoy some of removing ASMR. And then you're going to fill them with whatever filling you choose. Leave a little bit of room between the border of the cookie and your fillings so that way when you squeeze on the top cookie it squeezes out perfectly to the edges. And yes, you can eat them now, but all the macarons that you buy in store have been matured, aka they've sat in the fridge with their filling for at least a day. But I promise you it's worth it because look how cute they are. And now I hope you can do this too.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: It took me two full hours to decorate this tiny little cake and I originally allotted myself 45 minutes to decorate this cake... but this is what happens when my clients give me full creative freedom... my client Madison reached out and said she wanted to order a cake for her mom... she said her mom loves gardening, veggies and flowers and she loves her two dogs... Molly and Maui are the dogs... I made sure for the buttercream to do brown and then I put black and gold in the piping bag so it would come out all different colors... I think it's super fun... I did green for the base... I then went in and added all the little details to the dogs like their arms, their legs, some ears... I of course had to give them each a tail... I piped some carrots and some beets onto some parchment paper... I also piped out a cute little garden basket... I did these off of the cake so I could make sure they looked perfect... I piped out some flowers all over the cake... I added the basket to the top... I added their greenery to them... I also placed some all around the cake... I went back in on top and added more flowers... I threw some lavender in the basket as well... I always do party hats on all the animals on my cakes... each got a star... I added a cute little pink bow to it... so here's a slow mo glitter spray for you... this is the cake! I'm freaking obsessed with it.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Take your posture from this to this. Look at that jawline that just magically appeared. First you will shrug your shoulder up to your ear and hold that for about 15 seconds and then you will pull your head in the opposite direction stretching that muscle out. You're going to do this move on each side about three to five times. Now you will turn your head to a 45-degree angle, put your arm behind your back and pull your head down. This move feels so good on the area where my head meets my neck. Next we will stretch our head diagonally back, this feels so good on the muscle on the front of our neck. Next stretch your arm behind your head and pull your elbow down stretching your hand down the middle of your back. Now, this goal post exercise, be sure to squeeze your back muscles and finish it off with my favorite stretch. This one is so great at opening up your chest. These moves help to improve your posture, decrease facial puffiness, and improves your blood circulation which gives you a healthier glow.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: ¿Eres equipo pantalones o equipo falda? (Are you team pants or are you team skirt?)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Give me 10 seconds and I'll let you know which one suits you best. Now this has nothing to do with your height or your leg size, so don't worry about it. Do it once and you'll be able to spend wiser on your wardrobe. Get yourself a measuring tape. Measure your upper and lower body. Upper body from your belly button to your head. Lower body from your belly button to your legs. Upper body divided by lower body. If your value is less than 1.4, skirts suit you better. If the value is more than 1.6, pants is the best choice for you. But if your value falls between 1.4 to 1.6, congratulations, both look good on you. My value is 1.65 so I look better in pants. Are you team pants or are you team skirt?

videos_analizados.hook_template: Anyone even notice how some people look naturally (insert adjective) without doing anything crazy. If you’ve ever secretly (insert action) and hope no one
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación minimalista, calles de Nueva York (DUMBO/Brooklyn Bridge), cafetería.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Anyone ever notice how some people seem naturally skinny without doing anything crazy?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores (golden hour) y luz suave en interiores.
equipamiento: Micrófono de solapa (opcional, voz en off de calidad)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Aborda una observación común y frustrante para generar curiosidad inmediata sobre la 'solución' que esas personas tienen.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Explicación de un 'secreto' o concepto poco conocido aplicado a la vida diaria.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) y transiciones de movimiento (caminar hacia la cámara).
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, saturado, estilo 'lifestyle' vibrante.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la intriga/envidia sana por los 'naturalmente delgados' a la comprensión y finalmente a la motivación personal.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: 'Just get your body moving and trust me, you'll start seeing results'.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Anyone ever notice how some people seem naturally skinny without doing anything crazy? Like they don't live at the gym, they don't starve themselves, but somehow they're so effortlessly fit. Well, it's all about N.E.A.T. Non-Exercise Activity Thermogenesis. But don't let the name scare you. It's just the little things you do during the day that aren't intentional exercise, but that's significantly increasing your fat loss. Welcome to HOT GIRL IN PROGRESS, where we're becoming the best versions of ourselves one small habit at a time. So back to NEAT. NEAT is stuff like pacing while on the phone, cleaning your room, doing your skincare. And people with high NEAT can burn up to 1,000 extra calories a day. I sit at my desk all day and forget to move, so now I'm making it a point to get up more. Things like walking outside to grab a matcha or stretching while I'm scrolling on my phone. And it all kind of adds up. So if you want to burn more calories without going crazy at the gym, don't overthink it. Just get your body moving and trust me, you'll start seeing results.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you’ve ever unbuttoned your jeans at dinner and hoped no one noticed, this is for you. Welcome back to Hot Girl in Progress, where we’re becoming the best versions of ourselves one small habit at a time. So for years I felt weirdly uncomfortable in my own body. I eat pretty clean, nothing crazy, but by the afternoon my stomach has always had this tight bloated pressure that I just couldn’t ignore. Like I’d go out with friends and secretly be counting down the minutes until I could go home, take off my jeans and lie on the couch. Or I’d strategically place my bag in front of my stomach whenever I had to sit down. I was so tired of thinking about my stomach all the time so I started writing down everything I ate. And that’s when I started noticing: on mornings I had chia seed pudding, the bloating was noticeably less intense. Turns out when chia seeds soak overnight they form this gel-like texture, and that gel is full of soluble fiber. It slows digestion just enough to help your gut absorb nutrients and move everything through more smoothly. It’s nothing fancy, just milk, chia seeds, maple syrup... it doesn't work perfectly, like I have some pretty severe IBS, but I have noticed I don't think about my stomach as much. And that felt really, really good. So if you're also trying to figure out how to handle your bloat, follow along.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Today we are going to play did you know cleaning edition. Did you know that you should be rotating your mattress every 3 to 6 months? Yep, it's true. It's important to do because it evens the wear of the mattress, prolongs the lifetime, and helps reduce dust and allergens. Did you also know you should be sprinkling baking soda on your mattress every 3 to 6 months as well? It helps absorb odors, control the moisture, and refreshes the fabric. Let it sit for 30 to 60 minutes then just vacuum it up. Did you already know this tip or did you learn something new? Let me know and follow along for more cleaning tips and hacks! Bye!

I can assume it's not formulated specifically for thickening your hair. I can assume you're applying your conditioner like this instead of like this. I can assume the leave-in conditioner that you're using is way too heavy for your hair. I can assume you're brushing with your roots down instead of brushing with your roots lifted. I can assume that you're not scrunching in your gel upside down. I can assume you're diffusing like this and not like this. So I can assume your curls don't look like this.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are 4 ways to make fried chicken. Your basic fried chicken formula is a dip in egg, followed by a dip in flour and then deep fried until it looks something like this. Pretty good, but certainly nothing special and I think we can do better. If you replace the egg with a buttermilk marinade and instead of a regular flour use seasoned flour, you'll have something akin to a southern style fried chicken, juicier and more tender from the buttermilk and with an irresistibly flavorful skin from all the seasonings. If you replace the buttermilk with a soy marinade and then replace the flour with potato starch, you'll have something similar to a Japanese style karaage, often served with kewpie mayo and lemon. The soy marinade gives it that signature tang while the potato starch reduces moisture, giving it more crunch. If you replace the soy marinade with a wet batter and instead of potato starch use cornstarch, you'll end up with something similar to a Korean style fried chicken. Glaze it up and you'll have one of the most delicious bites in existence. The wet batter has air bubbles which are then trapped by a layer of cornstarch, which in tandem creates a crispy yet light and airy texture. Now, all that yapping aside, time to address the real question: what happens if you combine every single method here mentioned together? The result is the FrankenFry, sporting the fried chicken equivalent of full plate armor, protecting its body from any nutritional value that may have existed.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Those little fuzz balls are called pills. I'm a dry cleaner and I'm going to teach you a couple different ways to remove them. There are a variety of tools you can use, each has their advantages. The basic depilling comb is small, inexpensive, and handy. My favorite is the Gleener comb because it has three different heads along with a lint brush. The most fun to use is the sweater shaver. This is great for big jobs. If you're in a pinch, a cheap disposable razor will work decently. Just remember that you're removing material from your garment, so try not to overdo it.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You wanna know why it takes so long for salon-washed hair to get oily again? Well, it is unbelievable how to simply wash their hair correctly. So let me tell you the top 3 mistakes when washing their hair. Stop washing your hair like this. This will give you dry hair. Hairdressers know that shampoo is made for the scalp, NOT the ends of the hair. They don't give head massages just to relax you, they are trying to cleanse the scalp of dirt and oil so it doesn't get oily fast and stays healthy. At home, if you massage the shampoo all over your scalp, don't touch the ends and just rinse it. Do two rounds of shampoo like the hairdressers. One to clean product build up and two, your hair will be the cleanest it can be. It also depends on your shampoo. I'm gonna talk about that. Secret #2: on the benefits of your shampoo. If you're not doing this, if you don't emulsify the shampoo in your hands before applying it to the scalp, it will not be cleansed evenly or deeply. Secret #3: the wrong shampoo will sabotage your hair's potential. Hairdressers know that scalp health equals hair health, period. Use products that actually clean and nourish the scalp. If you want a natural shampoo that helps clean and lengthen the hair, it's the Slavic Hair Care Secret: Nettle Shampoo. If you know, you know. And a bonus secret: hairdressers don't wash hair with hot water. That's gonna cause damage. Wash your hair with cold water to avoid frizz and keep your cuticles from opening and splitting. Do you know of any hairdresser's secrets? Let me know in the comments.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If your pee is clear like this, you're likely overhydrated. But if your pee is dark like this, you're likely dehydrated. And if your pee has a light yellow tint to it like this, you're properly hydrated. If your pee looks highlighter yellow like this, you're peeing out excess vitamin B2. If your pee looks cloudy like this, it's likely a UTI or kidney stones. If your pee is frothy like this, it could indicate a kidney problem. If your pee is red like this, it could be from red-colored foods, but it is the most common sign of bladder cancer. If your pee is orange like this, it could be from certain medication or liver or bile duct issues. And if your pee is blue like this, it could be from certain supplements, medication, or you have too much calcium in the blood.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Stop buying your fish portioned like this, buy your fish whole. It's going to be way cheaper. No brainer. How to prep a salmon. So just going to start at the top, get the knife as close to the bone as possible. We've hit the rib cage in the middle here and now we're just going to take a big knife and cut through the rib cage. Turn over, we'll do the other side. Once you hit the rib cage in the middle, it's like a semi-circle shape, you want to come down the other side. And once you've done that, you're able to basically just free the rest of the fillet. Once your fillet's free, knife inside, hold the fillet, second fillet off. Keep this, use it for soup. And then to finish cleaning up the fillet, just run that knife along the underside of the rib cage and then we're just going to slide underneath it basically. And then we've got to remove the pin bones. Just need to portion them now. 3 kilos from the prep salmon and that cost me 35 pounds. And then this one, pre-prepared, this cost me 7 pounds and you get 240 grams. 50 pound saving. No brainer.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Did you know this tip? It's going to blow your mind. If you cut your store-bought flowers underwater versus air, they'll last up to two weeks longer. But why? Well, when you cut a flower stem in the open air, the exposed cut can draw air up instead of water, and the air can form a blockage within the stem's xylem. Thus they wilt because they can't siphon water properly. You can see the tulips I cut in air versus the ones underwater. Comment below and follow me for more tips.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I shake my head really, really hard right now, would you believe me if I told you that I have literally nothing holding my bun up? And by that I mean no hair ties, no bobby pins, no chopsticks, literally nothing. I've been gatekeeping this for way too long, so you need two hands, long hair, and bonus points if it's fried at the bottom. I suffer from a lot of migraines, so this hairstyle helps me a lot by keeping my hair out of my face without having a hair tie that pulls on my hair and hurts my head. Make a low ponytail with your hands, bring it to the side and twist it all the way down towards your ear. Face your fingers and thumbs down and put the tail in between. Then you want to wrap it up around your fingers. Keep it looped around your fingers but twist your hand so it faces down. Put your thumb through the hole next to your fingers and you can grab hold of the tail and let go with your other hand. With your free hand you want to grab onto this bridge part, pull the tail all the way through and then keep on pushing the bun all the way through. It can be a little tight right here but this is what makes it so solid and you can make it even tighter by pulling on the tail. I personally like to pull it really tight which seems a little odd but it's so comfy without hurting my head. Just tuck the tail in wherever you like it and you got yourself a solid little bun. My front pieces are too short so I like to pull them out and I think it looks really cute like this. It's so chic, so clean. It holds up to the headbang test and it will stay like this all day. Just don't headbang with a migraine.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is what all of your makeup brushes look like. I'm a pro makeup artist and I have had for 10 years. Divide your brushes into piles first. Do eye and make it so... for cleaning makeup brushes I prefer liquid cleansers, unscented always. The most important rule to washing brushes: NEVER SUBMERGE IT! Ferrule part this contains bristles and the glue. When you submerge this look what happens: water gets submerged, breaks down the adhesive and there go your bristles. And this is how sensitive it is, it's all little bristles connected with glue. So this is the right way: brushes pointing down while you're washing it. Water doesn't run in and notice how I'm just dipping because the true cleaning happens here when you're lathering. The brush tells wants to be cleaned. A round brush: round swirling motions. A flat brush like this: going to run it flat like this. Because this can be tedious doing it individually, I recommend grouping them within shapes. These face brushes have a similar shape, they're all round like this, all again pointing downwards. Time to rinse off. Use hot water? Instead, lukewarm or even cold water. Like to feel make sure that all the soap is off. I do this where I shake off the excess water and then I on a towel, paper towel, anything that's absorbent till they're dry. There you go!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Does it actually matter which type of flour you choose? To show you the difference that flour choice can make, I made one loaf of bread with four different doughs. Flour is mostly made up of protein and starch in different ratios. And even tiny changes in that ratio will have huge effects on the final product. That's why you'll often see protein percentage labeled in big bold numbers on the front of the bag. Hard flours like bread and whole wheat have more protein and produce more gluten for a stronger internal structure and a chewier dough. Good for things like bread, bagels, pitas, pasta, or pizza. Soft flours like pastry or cake flour have less protein and more starch, which absorbs water and helps things like muffins, cheesecake, or banana bread stay moist and crumbly after baking. And all-purpose flour is right down the middle, which makes it a safe choice for all purposes.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Mito vs Realidad / Educación basada en datos.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You would need to eat eight oranges today to get the same amount of Vitamin A that your grandparents obtained from eating just one. This is because our agricultural practices focus on improving size, yield, and pest resistance rather than nutritional content. This 49-year study found declines in protein, calcium, phosphorus, iron, and Vitamin C across 43 fruits and vegetables. And from 1975 to 1997, 12 other vegetables experienced a 27% decline in calcium, 37% in iron, 21% in Vitamin A, and 30% in Vitamin C. Another 50-year study with 20 different vegetables had similar findings. Ultimately, our food is less nutrient-rich than ever before. Now, just because fruits and vegetables aren't as healthy as they used to be, it doesn't necessarily mean that we should avoid them. Carnivores, I'm talking to you. Fruits and vegetables offer a wider variety of phytochemicals and antioxidants, making them our best sources for these essential compounds that help prevent disease. This is why it's so vital today that we focus on regenerative agriculture. Regenerative practices focus on restoring soil health, increasing biodiversity, and eliminating the use of chemical inputs. Healthy living soil produces more nutrient-dense crops, reduces environmental damage, and even sequesters carbon, which helps combat climate change. By choosing food organically grown from regenerative farms, you're not only supporting practices that rebuild the nutrient cycle, but you're also ensuring better food for you and for future generations. Every bite you take is a vote for our food system. The more often we can do this, the sooner our food will be what it once was: rich in nutrients, free of chemicals, and full of vitality.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Let’s unify our palette! I start by laying out all of the major colors I’ll need for this painting and then I choose my unifying color: yellow ochre. I then take an equal amount of yellow ochre and mix it into every single color on the palette, making sure to wipe off my palette knife in between. If you haven't seen my previous video, this is meant to unify the palette together and make it look more cohesive, making all of the colors look like they exist in the same environment. To put this to the test, I used the OG un-unified colors to make this painting, and now using our unified palette, we're gonna replicate it. Right off the bat, our unified palette already looks a lot different than the one in our original, but the key to this is to keep going and trust the process. We are essentially pretending that every color in our unified palette is its original form. I put our new green in all the same places that I put the old green, the new red in all the places I put the old red, and so on. The key to a unified palette is pretending all the colors are as they are in life and doing so will definitely reward you! Like look how gorgeous this is. Comparing the two side by side, you can see it looks like a filter has been put over the unified painting. Your unified palette will give you a different vibe depending on what color you choose to unify it. I hope you try it out! Thanks for watching.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Today I'm gonna show you the four most common ways to order your eggs in a diner. Starting with my absolute favorite: over easy. Butter in the pan, crack our egg right into the pan. Just let it cook, don't mess with it. Once this inner egg starts to cook a little bit, then we'll go ahead and flip. And then literally count to ten... It's done. Flip it over straight onto the plate. Finish with a little bit of sea salt. Oh yeah. For the over medium egg, you're gonna do the exact same thing as over easy. Everything changes when you flip it over. Turn off the heat and let them rest for 30 to 45 seconds. The egg will bounce right back. Beautiful. Now for the over hard. My least favorite. Pop the yolk, and then we're gonna rip this one. Turn the heat up all the way. Let me know, what's your favorite way to eat eggs? As soon as this one starts to cook, I'm gonna go ahead and flip right away. It's really firm to the touch and golden brown. Over hard, yolk completely cooked. Yeah, I hate it. Sunny side up egg. Crack your egg right into the pan. I like to break up this white on the second layer so it helps cook a little evenly. Low heat and let it cook for about 3 to 4 minutes. You know it's done when the white around the outside is cooked and the egg yolk is super bright and nice. It's perfect. Beautiful. Look at that, brother. Did I just eat four eggs in five minutes? Yes, I did.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Guys this is so nerdy but it's the coolest thing I've ever done with my pasta. So if you take two pieces of pasta and then put them on a piece of paper and then draw lines that are two pasta lengths apart, I drew four but you can do more, and then take your pile of pasta and start throwing them onto your lines, something almost magical happens. First count how many pieces of pasta you just dropped, and here it's fifty. Then count how many times a piece of pasta crossed one of the lines, and here it's sixteen of them. And if you divide these two numbers, you will get Pi. I got 3.125 and that's pretty close. That's so cool. And if you want to learn more, it's called the Buffon's Needle Problem, so definitely check it out.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Valor educativo y curiosidad 'mind-blowing' que la gente quiere mostrar a otros.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Cheese and white bread makes a ham and cheese sandwich. Adding Gruyere cheese and bechamel makes it a Croque Monsieur. Finishing with an egg on top makes it a Croque Madame. And then coating the whole thing in egg and deep frying it makes it a Monte Cristo. White bread, lettuce, tomato, bacon, turkey and mayo double stacked makes a Club Sandwich. White bread, ketchup, chips and British brown sauce make a Chip Butty. Milk bread, shredded cabbage, tonkatsu which is a fried pork cutlet and tonkatsu sauce makes a Katsu Sando. Rye bread, sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, corned beef and Russian dressing makes a Reuben. Changing the beef for turkey makes it a Rachel. Then baguette, ham and butter makes a Jambon Beurre. Changing the filling to cilantro, pickled daikon and carrots, cucumber, grilled pork, pate and mayo makes a Banh Mi. A Po' Boy baguette, pickles, lettuce, tomato, fried shrimp and a remoulade makes a Po' Boy. A hoagie roll, Cheez Whiz, thinly sliced steak and grilled onions makes a Philly Cheesesteak. Cuban bread, mustard, pickles, Swiss cheese, ham and roast pork makes a Cubano. Bread, ham, cheese, bread, cabbage, tonkatsu, sauce, rye, sauerkraut, cheese, beef, sauce, roll, cheese, beef and onion makes the Sandwich-Sandwich. A sandwich made entirely out of sandwiches.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here are 8 barbecue sauces from around the world. Let's break them down to their core components. If we use ketchup as our base, brown sugar to sweeten, vinegar for acidity and flavor it with Worcestershire sauce, we'll have a Kansas City style barbecue sauce. If we instead make our base yellow mustard, we'll have a Carolina Gold style barbecue sauce. Now, if we take the vinegar and make it our base and replace the Worcestershire with red pepper flakes, we'll have a Carolina vinegar style barbecue sauce. If we make our base a neutral oil, use lemon juice for acidity and flavor it with piri-piri peppers, we'll have the bones of a Peri Peri sauce from Mozambique. If we make our base soy sauce, replace the lemon juice with vinegar and flavor it with pineapple juice and ketchup, we'll have a very basic Huli Huli sauce from Hawaii. If we change the brown sugar to white sugar, replace the vinegar with the slightly acidic mirin and flavor with sake and sesame oil, we'll have a Yakiniku barbecue sauce from Japan. If we instead sweeten with hoisin and honey, remove the acid and flavor with Shaoxing wine and Chinese five spice, we'll have a very basic Char Siu sauce from Southern China. Now, if we use a base of Scotch bonnets and scallions, sweeten with brown sugar, use vinegar for acidity and flavor with allspice, thyme and nutmeg, we'll have the core of a jerk style sauce from Jamaica. You know what time it is. Let's combine every sauce together to make the ultimate barbecue sauce. Considering our barbecue theme here, I got a half rack of ribs, which I'm going to glaze with our sauce and bake until infused. Looks pretty good. What should we call this thing? It sort of tastes like a teriyaki version of a McDonald's barbecue sauce, but spicy. It was terrible. I had to eat all of it.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Too many green sauces in the world look the same. But are they? Parsley, red wine vinegar, olive oil, garlic, chili and oregano: basic Chimichurri from Argentina. Change the vinegar with lemon juice, the chili and oregano for capers and anchovy, you'll have the Italian Salsa Verde. Change the parsley to cilantro, the olive oil for tomatillos, and anchovy for onion and jalapeno: a basic version of the Mexican Salsa Verde. Now parsley, lemon zest and garlic makes the Italian Gremolata. Add cilantro, olive oil, cumin and green chili, and it becomes the spicy green Zhoug from Yemen. By replacing the cumin with paprika, it becomes similar to the Moroccan Chermoula. Swap out the parsley for mint, add green chili, ginger and cumin: a basic green chutney from India. Now take all that away. Basil, olive oil, garlic, pine nuts and parmesan for an Italian Pesto. By removing the parmesan and pine nuts, it then becomes a French Pistou. Now, if we combine every green sauce here together, we will create the Swamp Thing. It has a very generic green flavor, it pretty much just tastes like everything else here.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Flour, yeast, water, and salt are the basic components of a white bread dough. By changing the type of flour to whole wheat flour, you'll get a whole wheat bread dough. Now if you take your normal white bread recipe and replace the yeast with a sourdough starter, you'll have sourdough bread. Now change the type of flour to rye flour and add molasses to the mix and you'll have a basic pumpernickel bread. Then instead of molasses, do a mix of rye flour and regular flour and change the sourdough starter back to yeast and you'll have rye bread. Going back to our basic white, if we instead of regular flour use a high protein flour like bread flour and boil the dough before baking, we'll have a bagel dough. Now if we go back to our basic white bread but add eggs, butter and milk to the mix, we'll have a brioche dough. Swap the butter for sugar and the milk for oil and you'll have a challah dough. Take your basic white bread and instead increase the amount of water and you'll have a ciabatta dough. Then if you just layer it with a bunch of olive oil it becomes focaccia. Bring the water level back down and it becomes a pita dough. Add yogurt and milk to the mix and instead of olive oil use a fat like neutral oil or ghee and you'll have naan. Mix the flour with cornmeal, replace the yeast with baking powder, change the water for milk and add an egg and you'll have cornbread. Take everything away except for water, salt, and cornmeal, more specifically masa harina, which is cornmeal treated with lime water, and you'll have corn tortillas. What happens if you combine everything together? The result is CHAOS.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's a little mango 101 for you. When you're shopping for mangoes, this is what you're going to look for. Most of the time, you're going to see the common mango or the Haden and the Ataulfo. Now, these ones are not as sweet as these, but these people have a harder time figuring out when it's ripe. What you're going to do is [smell the] stem and you're going to smell. It's going to smell super sweet and floral. It smells like a good mango. I don't know how else to describe it. And then, you look for a third red, a third yellow, and then a third green. That is a good mango to look for. Now, for example, we're going to compare like this one. You only see greens and yellows, so we're not going to go with this mango. We're going to go with this one. And you can tell it's ripe when soft. That's how you know when you kind of squeeze it a little bit, you feel it give in, it goes in and it's soft. That's when it's ready for peel. So out of all these mangoes, we are going with this one, this one, this one, and this one because you can see that third red, that third yellow, and that third green. You're welcome! Here we go.

videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Muestra errores comunes para generar empatía y luego promete una solución superior ('el truco').

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I see so many people using their piping bags just like this or maybe like this even if you tie it like this. So let me show you the trick to perfectly tying piping bags. First get a container or cup that can hold the piping bag. Then take your piping bag and fold the top edge. Place it around your container and fill with whatever you want. The reason we fold the edge is because that way if we get anything on the side when we fold it up it won't be at the very edge of the piping bag. And to get your filling to the bottom of the bag all you have to do is grab and spin. And now your filling will be right at the bottom with no air bubbles. Scrape everything together nice and neat. Now all you wanna do is take some scissors and cut right down the middle leaving just a little space above the line of your filling. Now simply pull and spin between 3 to 5 times. Now all you have to do is tie it off. Trim any excess bits. Perfectly filled piping bag top to bottom with no air bubbles, won't explode or get on your hands even if you squeeze really hard. Super easy and clean and makes any job where you have to pipe that much easier.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Here’s how the form of (insert noun) you add into (insert noun) affects them. Why (insert action) like a normal person, when you can

videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina doméstica decorada con motivos navideños sutiles al fondo.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Here’s how the form of butter you add into cookies affects them.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Suave - Muy bien iluminado, ambiente cálido de cocina.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Comparación visual inmediata (antes vs después) y promesa de valor educativo.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Experimento A/B/C/D (Comparativa de variables).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para agilizar el proceso.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, saturado para hacer que la comida se vea apetitosa.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, interés creciente por el proceso técnico y satisfacción visual al ver el resultado final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en video, probablemente en el caption (ej: '¿Cuál es tu textura favorita?').
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here’s how the form of butter you add into cookies affects them. Why bake like a normal person when you can experiment? We are testing softened butter, melted butter, browned melted butter, and browned softened butter. Softened butter gives that classic structure. Melted butter makes them chewier and spread more. Browning the butter adds flavor, but the state it's in when mixed changes the loft and texture. Look at the difference in the crumb and the spread. My favorite is the browned softened for the best of both worlds.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Etiquetas físicas (Softened, Melted, Browned Melted, Browned Softened) para identificar las variables.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Why (insert action) like a normal person, when you can be a psycho instead. This is everything I got for free on my (insert event).
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina moderna y limpia
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Why scoop ice cream like a normal person when you can be a psycho instead?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz de cocina brillante y clara
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste entre lo 'normal' y lo 'psicópata' (perfeccionista), apelando al deseo de sofisticación.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de experto ('I worked in a Michelin kitchen').
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, colores vibrantes y limpios
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad/intriga inicial a la sensación de aprendizaje y satisfacción al ver el resultado perfecto.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito en la demostración del resultado)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Why scoop ice cream like a normal person when you can be a psycho instead? I worked in a Michelin kitchen and this is how you quenelle. Grab the curviest spoon you have with a pointy tip, not those flat round ones. Dip your spoon in room temperature water, smooth out the surface of the ice cream, dip it in water again and pull back the spoon until it fills the curve. With a swift twist motion, scoop up the ice cream, clean off the edge and warm up the back of the spoon with your hand and it should slide right off. Voila, perfect quenelle.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad (aprender una técnica nueva) y estética (el resultado es muy instagrameable).

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina un hook intrigante con el prestigio de la cocina 'Michelin' y un tutorial altamente visual y satisfactorio (Oddly Satisfying).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave resaltadas: 'scoop', 'person', 'psycho', 'Michelin', 'Quenelle', 'curviest', 'X flat', 'VOILA'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Foodies, cocineros aficionados, personas interesadas en estética culinaria.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Everything I got for free on my birthday. First stop: Cinnabon gives you a free latte. Starbucks gives you a drink of any size. Teaspoon: a six dollar drink voucher. Panda Express: a small entree. Habit gives you a charburger. Buffalo Wild Wings gives you six piece wings. Wingstop: seasoned fries. Taco Bell: item of your choice. McDonald's: McChicken or McNuggets. Cheesecake Factory: you get a slice of your choice. Jeni's: three scoops and a waffle cone. Dippin' Dots: a small cup of dots. BJ's: Pizookie with ice cream. Chick-fil-A: a brownie or cookie. Krispy Kreme: three original glazed donuts. Subway: a cookie. Nothing Bundt Cakes: a bundtlet. Yogurtland: five dollar froyo voucher. Sidecar Donuts: one donut. Baskin Robbins: a birthday scoop. AMC: large popcorn. Sephora: special gift of choice. Ulta: same thing. Madewell: twenty-five dollar store credit. Bath & Body Works: nine dollar ninety-five store voucher. If you love free stuff, make sure to save this video.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: English is my second language, so here are the seven tongue twisters that I used to do a lot and I recommend my students as well.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - bien iluminada, luz frontal suave
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de credibilidad (ella también aprendió inglés) seguido de una promesa de valor (lista de 7 recursos).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Listicle de utilidad (7 tips/recursos) con demostración de habilidad.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, colores cálidos y saturación equilibrada
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con empatía, pasa por una fase educativa de aprendizaje y termina con una sensación de reto y logro.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: 'Try to say them as quickly as you can'.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: English is my second language, so here are the seven tongue twisters that I used to do a lot and I recommend my students as well. One tongue twister for every day of the week. Monday: She sells silly seashells by the seashore, but seashells seldom sell in snow. Tuesday: Larry rarely likes lemon lollipops, but loves raspberry relish rolls. Wednesday: Three thirsty thieves found four thick feathers floating. Thursday: Owen owns only one onion, but Ophelia offers oatmeal and oranges. Friday: Charlie chose cheese chips, but Jack juggled juicy jelly beans. Saturday: Harry hurriedly hands hot honey to happy hamsters hopping home. And Sunday: If a bat fits in the bit, but a bet sits on the set, which bat bit the pet? There you have it, seven tongue twisters. Try to say them as quickly as you can. First start slowly, then increase the speed.

videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y hacer el contenido educativo más ameno
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el formato de lista (7 días) con un valor educativo real y un reto personal. Es altamente 'guardable' para practicar después.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Encabezados con el día de la semana, los sonidos específicos a practicar y el texto completo del trabalenguas.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Estudiantes de inglés como segunda lengua (ESL).
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: 7 Trabalenguas para Mejorar la Pronunciación en Inglés
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano de la creadora con gafas y trenzas, con texto superpuesto: 'HOW I IMPROVED MY PRONUNCIATION'.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you gave me six months to get a job as a data analyst, this is what I would do. For context, I currently work as a senior data analyst with over 8 years of experience. First thing I would do is learn SQL, probably the skill that is used the most as a data analyst. I will do the data analyst associate SQL... Once I know the fundamentals of SQL, I would also download a SQL software on my computer so I can keep practicing and work on future projects. Next, I want to get properly certified. Specifically, I will do IBM Data Analyst... I want to get a professional certificate as fast as possible. It looks good on my resume and it's a really good talking point during interviews and it just shows that I'm committed and willing to learn. Next thing I will do is make a portfolio. I'll download some data sets from Kaggle or GitHub... dashboard and a Tableau dashboard. And regards to what type of dashboards I like making sales dashboards or performance dashboards. With the dashboards, I will host them on the Power BI server or Tableau public. I will also make a SQL project resolving an issue and I will host that on GitHub. For the portfolio itself, I will create a profile on datascienceportfol.io... I'll explain my thought process and why these projects are beneficial. Next, I will find a course and a call center job. I will also try to find a really good manager. I'll tell him that I'm certified, I'm skilled, I have this portfolio, I want to be a data analyst. I will also only work part-time so I can study coding on the site. I will take advantage of the career and volunteering programs they have in a lot of companies these days to try to get my foot in the door. So that's what I will do. I'll see you guys next time.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Educativo y basado en la experiencia (Mentorship style)
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alto valor educativo; la gente lo guarda para consultarlo después como guía.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: So if you are that person, please DM me.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're the person that told me this I could kiss you right now because it literally saved my life. I didn't even believe them because it sounded way too good to be true. But I just got my grade back and it was a 98 and the class average was a freaking 70. So I won't gatekeep this website because everyone deserves to know what it is. The website is called turbolearn.ai. I like to upload my textbook that I don't wanna read cause it'll basically read it for me and generate all these notes, a ton of flashcards to study with, and it'll come up with these practice quiz questions to help me even more. I spent way less time studying and did way better than I normally would've. All thanks to that one person. So if you are that person, please DM me.

videos_analizados.escenario: Patio trasero de una casa con un cobertizo y una sauna de barril.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Here’s how I get people to their best price on Facebook marketplace. You do not want to miss number four.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Exterior día nublado
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Promesa de valor con un 'open loop' (mencionar el punto número cuatro para retener audiencia)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de 5 pasos con un 'arma secreta'.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente desaturado (estilo orgánico)
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con intriga, pasa por una fase de aprendizaje lógico y termina con una sensación de empoderamiento para el comprador.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'flip' and I'll connect with early access.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here’s how I get people to their best price on Facebook marketplace. You do not want to miss number four. Don't use the default. People hate being asked, hi, is this still available? Take the extra few seconds and use their name and ask the same question but in a way that annoys nobody. It sounds like this. Anyone scheduled to pick this up? If it is available, here's step two. Ask any questions that weren't covered in the description. Does it need any repairs? Smoke-free home? Are they available when you're available? Is there someone available to help you lift it? That kind of stuff. Now you got all the necessary details, here's step three: pump the brakes on your enthusiasm by saying just starting to have a peek at options, might get back to you. Their experience with you thus far is that you are pleasant, you're a serious buyer and you just won some favor by formally pausing the discussion. Most people would just ghost. Now they're ripe and ready for my secret weapon: the polite lowball offer. We're going to reconnect with them 20 minutes or two weeks later and test their price a little bit. They've got it priced here and they're going to take anything between firm and flexible. We're going to slide in an offer somewhere between optimistic and bordering on disrespectful. A normal lowball offer would get a rude response or no response, but our offer is going to be apologetic. It's not you, it's me. I think you've got it priced perfectly but I'm just chopping a little prematurely. I don't quite need it yet. My wife's not sure about the color. It's about an hour away. I need to rent a trailer. Don't be dishonest, this is about showing them your cards and in turn they're going to show you their cards and they're going to reveal their best price. If their best price is still too high, we're going to hit them with step number five and that's a back pocket offer. Thank them for their quick replies and encourage them to reconnect if the price softens. This framework allows me to buy high ticket items on Facebook marketplace way below market value. If a simple resale business sounds like you want my help with, comment flip and I'll connect you.

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Emprendedores, revendedores (flippers) y ahorradores.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Estrategia de Negociación en Facebook Marketplace
