videos_analizados.hook_template: Did you know that there are # types of (insert noun). Here’s how you can guess an almost perfect number
videos_analizados.escenario: Interior acogedor, sala de estar con una estantería de libros al fondo, reforzando la autoridad en el nicho literario.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Did you know that there's six types of readers?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Luz suave proveniente de una ventana lateral que crea profundidad.
equipamiento: Luz natural o aro de luz
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un número específico para despertar curiosidad y desafiar el conocimiento previo del espectador.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Clasificación/Categorización y Solución a un problema común.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (jump cuts) para eliminar silencios y mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y natural, con saturación moderada.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, validación de una frustración común, alivio al encontrar una solución nueva y entusiasmo por participar.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Go to the link in my bio, visit ReadYourColor.com, and comment your type below.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Did you know that there's six types of readers? So many people when they think about what kind of reader they are, they think of genres. I am a fantasy reader, I am a romance reader. But when we limit ourselves to one genre of books, it's really hard to find good books because surprise, there's only so many good books in a given genre. That's why I created the Read Your Color test. It's going to sort you into one of six reading types. Each type goes beyond just genre and actually looks at what you want to get out of a book. What kind of pacing do you like? What kind of things do you want to feel? What kind of parts of your brain do you want to be stimulated when you read? If you take this test, you're going to get sorted into one of the types and you're going to be able to find books that you love. The test is totally free to take, so go to the link in my bio or go to ReadYourColor.com and take the test today. After you're done, comment what type you are below and then share it with your friends.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I teach makeup to people like they're in kindergarten and it's time for class. Look at your lips, find a color that matches. Get a fluffy brush because fluffy helps with blending. In the outer corner of your eye, draw a circle. A little more eyeshadow. Again, start in the outer corner, draw a rainbow towards your nose. Follow your brow bone. If you don't know what that means, write brow bone below, I have a class for that too. Now grab a brown that's a little darker than your skin tone. Same fluffy brush, same place as before, draw a little circle, a little smaller than the one you did before. Pinch your brush so you can get more precision. Drag the brown just a little bit up the rainbow. Small dense brush. Now you need a color lighter than your skin tone. From the inside corner of your eye, up and over. Last, add a little highlighter here and here. Now you have a go-to look that is customized to your face and no one knows you learned it in kindergarten.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Fatty liver can be reversed. So let's talk about four things to improve your fatty liver. And for those who missed the first video, this is what a normal healthy liver looks like. This is what happens when you start to accumulate fat in your liver and develop fatty liver. The rest of these are what happens as your liver starts to scar down from having fat. So there are two things that cause fatty liver: alcohol use and metabolic syndrome, so conditions that predispose you to having fat on your body. So what can you do for fatty liver? No. 1: Stop drinking alcohol, even if it's once in a while, one or two drinks in a week. Stop drinking alcohol. No. 2: Weight loss has been shown to improve fatty liver. No. 3: Exercise, independent of weight loss. So even if you don't lose weight, exercise itself can improve fatty liver. And the fourth one, it's been shown in research studies that drinking coffee, whether that's decaf coffee or regular coffee, can prevent fatty liver from becoming fibrosis.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: the average person would have to run for 30 minutes to burn the 300 calories in this cosmic brownie. that seems a bit discouraging when you think about fat loss for the long term. but let me show you why that's a terrible way to look at calories. these weights represent the amount of calories you burn in a day. 60% of your calories are burned every day just to stay alive. this means that you could watch Netflix all day without standing up once and you would still burn 60% of your calories. you can thank the BMR for that. 20% of your calories are burned through something called NEAT. NEAT stands for non exercise activity thermogenesis. this is just a fancy way of saying regular movement like picking up your bag or walking to your car. around 10% of your calories are burned through the thermic effect of food. you can help with this part by eating a high protein diet. protein is harder for your body to process which means it's going to burn more calories through that thermic effect. but only 10% of your calories are burned during exercise for the average person. this little portion right here is exactly why it is impossible to outwork your diet. you really need to focus on the calories that you're eating rather than the calories that you're burning. you gotta make health and fitness as simple and enjoyable as possible. now you know.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you ate this one slice of bread, this is the amount of water that your body would store. By eating just this amount of pasta, your body would store this amount of water. And if you ate this whole pack of rice, then guess what? Your body would store this amount of water. This is about 1 kg worth of water and it seems pretty scary that your body can store all of that just through eating these foods. But carbohydrates act a little bit like a sponge in the fact that when you eat them, your body also soaks up some water. For every 1 g of carbohydrates, typically around 3 to 4 grams of water. And this is why when it comes to losing weight, you can't just randomly weigh yourself when you fancy it. Cause on the day you catch it down here you're gonna be over the moon and the day you catch it up here you're gonna hate yourself. This just leads to mixed emotions all the time and then you self-sabotaging and doing things differently depending on what your weight is in the morning. You have to expect that your weight is gonna fluctuate every single day whether it's by 0.2 kilos, whether it's by 0.5, whether it's by one kilo. But that isn't important and instead you need to weigh yourself every morning and take a weekly average. So you compare this weekly average to the next weekly average to the next one to the next one alongside other measurements of progression: taking progress pictures, doing measurements, going off how your clothes fit. This is gonna be the best representation of understanding how much fat you're losing over time. Cause remember you haven't gained a kilo and you haven't lost a kilo overnight. That is close to impossible. So if you are someone that's trying to lose weight and you are weighing yourself, make sure you take this into consideration and if you want more helpful weight loss content just like this, make sure you drop me a follow.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: The worst material you will regret buying for your shower is right here. But before I show you the worst, let me show you the best. For all your wet areas inside of your home like your showers, tubs, you're going to want to go with porcelain tile. Porcelain tile is more durable, it's waterproof. This will give you peace of mind why? Because there's absolutely no maintenance. Alright, I'm going to now show you the worst. The worst material to use for your shower is going to be marble. Guys, marble I have seen in my 32 years experience, every single homeowner saying I regret installing marble in my shower. It's an organic material so it absorbs moisture and then it off-gases a stinky nasty moldy smell. High maintenance. You will hate it. It's going to start to stain. Yes, you can seal it but it won't work long term.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I paid $262,000 in cash to build this house from start to finish and it only took me 60 days. This is the complete cost breakdown. The land cost $27,000. The building plans and this rendering cost $8,200. The water, the sewer, and the electric hookup cost $8,300. The culvert and the dirt work it took to build the pad cost $13,200. The concrete slab cost $26,400. The framing cost $27,400. The siding cost $10,600. The windows cost $6,400. The exterior doors cost $3,600. The roof cost $14,000. The garage door cost $2,500. The driveway cost $6,900. The landscape cost $4,400. The HVAC cost $8,900. The exterior and the interior paint cost $6,300. The plumbing cost $12,900. The electrical trade cost $10,500. The spray foam insulation cost $3,200. The drywall trade cost $8,900. All the cabinetry cost $8,400. The interior doors and all the carpentry cost $6,000. The quartz countertops cost $6,100. All of the tile cost $15,700. The LVP flooring cost $3,700. All of the railing cost $3,100. The appliances cost $1,900. And all the miscellaneous items cost $9,000. This is an 1,887 square foot house with three bedrooms, two and a half bathrooms that I built in the Houston area.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Corte seco (Jump cut) para cambiar de la comida grande a los snacks pequeños.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación ligeramente alta para resaltar los colores de los empaques.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la obviedad y confianza a la sorpresa y la toma de conciencia.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I told you that you would gain weight from eating all of this, you probably believe me right? Here's the thing, this is obvious. If you did this once maybe twice a week, it's probably enough to bring your calories to at least maintenance if not a surplus depending on what you're eating on the other days of the week. But what's not so obvious and far more realistic for most of you motherfuckers is actually doing something like this. This is the reality of overeating and when I tell you how many calories this is, your mind is going to implode. You've got one very squished bliss ball, you've got one row of Cadbury's chocolate, you've got half a Carmen's high protein bar, you've got a handful of cashew nuts, you've got the leftover sandwich from your kids or your partner, and then you got some cheese and crackers with dip or maybe substitute that for the cookie that you have at work right? Like there's something else that's in there. This all added up is over 1,000 calories. Do you know how easy it is to eat these things in a day and feel like you haven't eaten that much? Like does this make sense? It's not that you're eating a whole lot of junk food and crap and constantly eating really bad food, it's that you are not aware of how easily it is to get way too many calories than what you actually need therefore you gain weight or you cannot lose weight.

I use this budgeting tracker to break down all my spending and stay within my budget and this method saves me hundreds a month. then you wanna put 20% towards your savings. I use a saving challenge booklet as I always dip into my bank and this helps me track my progress as I go along. lastly you wanna put 10% which is $300 towards your wants like dining out with your friends and partying with your family.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I spent $274,083 on new machines for my laundromat and so I'm going to do a full breakdown on what each of them cost. I have my biggest machines at the very front of my laundromat so that when my customers walk in they are the first ones they see. These are my two 80-pound machines and these are my biggest machines I have in my laundromat and they each cost me $18,114. And then next are my 60-pound machines and I have a total of eight of them in my laundromat and each one cost me $12,600. I keep five of them in the front right next to my 80-pounders and I also have three of them in the back. I don't really love having my bigger machines in the back of my laundromat but that's just how it worked out with my layout. And then in my middle aisle I have 15 30-pound machines and each of them cost me $8,219. And then lastly are my nine top loaders and they each cost me $1,530. So in total with all of my machines, and this includes install and labor and taxes, total was $274,083. Even though that is a lot of money I am still so happy with my new machines and if you can afford new machines in your laundromat then I highly recommend it. And if you want to learn more about buying and owning a laundromat then comment link below.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: here is a quick way to shelter 66% of your income legally. I'm Tyler I'm a former financial advisor portfolio manager now I make financial content for free so that you don't have to pay for it. number one let's say I make $75,000 a year pre tax. number two I would start by putting 4,150 bucks in a health savings account above the line deduction. number three next I'd put $7,000 into a traditional IRA above the line deduction and we're rolling. No. 4 next I put $23,000 into my traditional 401k pre tax contribution. No. 5 then I'd cap it off by taking the single filer deduction of 14,600 bucks. number six and I turn around and tell the government that I officially made $26,250 of taxable income went from the 22% to the 12% tax bracket and at this rate am projected to be a millionaire in just under 14 years.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is the exact order in which you should be applying for all of your credit cards. Why is it we are all so stressed about credit but don't have a plan? Start with a debit relationship to build trust. Then move to your first starter card like Discover. After 6-12 months, you're ready for beginner cards with rewards. Once your score is 700+, we hit the medium tier for point earning. The high rewards tier like Amex Gold looks expensive but the benefits outweigh the fee. Finally, you reach travel and business cards for elite status and huge credit limits.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: Why is it that we are all so stressed out about the cost of living but also we all have seventy inch TVs

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la duda/culpa financiera inicial a la revelación y validación de la frustración sistémica.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Why is it that we are all so stressed out about the cost of living but also we all have seventy inch TVs and iPhones and drones and Xboxes and books and board games... are we financially irresponsible? No, there's something going on... In the 70s, a TV was $500 (21 inch). Average down payment for a house was $5,320. 11 TVs bought you a down payment. Today, a 55 inch 4K TV is still $500. Average down payment for a house? $102,600. That's 206 TVs. This is why older generations think we are irresponsible... they operate from a world view they developed when they were coming up. It's called commodification... bubblegum dystopia.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: today my kids rent is due so let's collect their payment each week they earn $5 for the chores they do around the house and at the start of each month we charge them $1 for rent food and utilities Hans has already paid his rent this month so we will write it down in the tracker sheet to confirm the payment in the binder before they spend $2 we ask them if they can afford the purchase is it a want or a need and will they in the future be happy they brought it this is to help them not to emotionally spend their money on toys and sweets and develop a habit to save their money towards bigger things for their future self as this is something schools don't teach kids and me and my wife used to struggle with finance and saving and these simple saving tricks helps to get back control of our finances and save towards a family vacation together so we made it a great family activity to save our money each month and now we have stopped depending on paycheck to paycheck and this saving challenge method has worked amazingly for us

videos_analizados.guion_completo: There are five things I like to do when I buy a new house. First, change out the air filter with Filterbuy, because you don't know what these people were breathing. I put my son on auto-delivery with Filterbuy so he doesn't have to think about it. Number two is take a look at the smoke detectors. Okay, hardwired into the house, that's good, I don't have to worry about replacing batteries. Number three is change the door locks, because you don't know who's got keys to this place. Number four, clean the toilets. Number five, clean the fridge and replace the water filter. And just one more thing... And now the real work begins. I'll see you soon.

videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Pattern Interrupt con una declaración de valor y una pregunta de 'costo oculto' para generar FOMO (miedo a perderse algo).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Listicle (Lista de consejos) con un ángulo de 'ahorro secreto'.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el dinamismo.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la preocupación/estrés por el mantenimiento a la empoderación y alivio por saber cómo resolverlo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow for more adulting hacks.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 3 things everyone needs to know how to fix in an apartment. Why does nobody talk about the cost of ignoring these? First, your leaky faucet. A plumber costs $150, but this washer is 50 cents. Second, the HVAC filter. If you don't change it, your electric bill jumps 15%. Third, the garbage disposal. Stop calling maintenance for a reset button. Save your security deposit and your cash. Follow for more adulting hacks.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Directo, educativo y orientado al ahorro.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La gente lo guarda para consultarlo después o lo envía a amigos que acaban de mudarse.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina utilidad práctica inmediata con el miedo a perder dinero, un motivador universal.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos palabra por palabra que cambian de color para resaltar palabras clave como 'COST', 'FIX' y 'MONEY'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes adultos, inquilinos, personas que buscan ahorrar dinero.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Guía de reparaciones esenciales y costos ocultos en el hogar

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'circle' to grab the audit checklist.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Why nobody talks about the cost of staying in the wrong circle. There's a reason I stopped hanging out with certain people. Not because I'm better, but because the conversation stays the same. Complain, blame, judging others without a single ounce of self-reflection. That's not connection, that's noise. Then I found my people: entrepreneurs, book club friends, my coach, snowboard instructor, diving buddies, running crew, Hyrox team, friends and family, life partner. All from different backgrounds, but one thing in common: they want to grow mentally, physically, emotionally. And the conversation they spark something in me. Your circle is your mirror. You don't rise when you're surrounded by comfort zone. You rise when you're surrounded by curiosity, discipline, ambition. And sometimes, the best mental clarity comes from cutting off the noise. Not everyone deserves your mental strength. Some people they don't need saving, they need to save themselves. High performers don't guess their circle, they audit it. Comment 'circle' to grab the audit checklist.

videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Impacto visual inmediato combinado con una advertencia de peligro mortal
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Advertencia de peligro desconocido + Demostración visual impactante.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado (colores otoñales resaltados)
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con asco/curiosidad, escala a preocupación seria y termina en una necesidad urgente de saber cómo prevenirlo.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Ew, what is this? This is your dog's stomach with kibble and water in it and it's the number one reason that large breed dogs die anywhere in the world and it's called bloat. Now what is bloat? Let's talk about it. You're gonna feed your dog some food, right? Maybe it's kibble, maybe it's raw food. That's kind of what it looks like in the stomach. Then they drink a whole bowl or two of water. What does that look like? Well it ends up looking like this, but this isn't exactly as representative as what I'm about to show you, which is that their stomach is shaped a little bit like a hammock. So when that is sitting in their cavity here, it looks like a hammock and with all that weight of the food and water, if they jump, if they run, if they roll over and you scratch their belly, it flips. And when it flips, God forbid it flips twice, we have all that gas trapped in there, the gases start to expand, the blood circulation is cut off, nothing can move on to the small intestine and it ruptures. And when it ruptures, all of that spills into their body and they die a horrible death. And the next videos we're going to tell you how to prevent it so it doesn't happen to your dog.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: thirty years as a vet and people still don't believe when I say these things. itchy paws soak them in diluted apple cider vinegar fifty fifty with water it clears up yeast and calms the irritation no more expensive paw creams. fleas keep coming back rub a little coconut oil into their coat it repels fleas naturally and soothes the skin clean simple and actually works. bad breath or yellow teeth rub the inside of a banana peel on their teeth once a week it breaks down plaque and freshens their breath no brushing battle required. constant ear scratching wipe the ears with cooled chamomile tea it calms inflammation kills yeast and prevents infections gentle cheap and no chemicals needed. and the one thing I tell every single dog owner collagen most people wait until their dog is stiff tired or stops healing properly by then it's already late start early and give it daily because once things start breaking down it's already too late that's why I always recommend Taily collagen it's 100% natural actually works and you can grab it on Amazon follow for more real pet care tips that actually help.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz de cocina brillante y clara
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de número específico + comando negativo ('never') para generar curiosidad y miedo a cometer un error.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Ranking de errores comunes
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rápidos para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturado para resaltar texturas de madera y metal.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con una alerta de error común, genera validación mediante explicaciones lógicas y termina con un sentimiento de 'ahora lo sé'.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Think first before you chuck everything into the dishwasher (implícito: guarda este video).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 5 things you should never put in the dishwasher. Number one is wooden cutting boards and utensils. The high heat and moisture can cause the wood to crack and warp. It also promotes a ton of bacteria growth if the wood isn't dried properly. Number two is knives. The heat and chemicals can ruin your knives and your blade will get damaged and dull, so you're better off just hand-washing. Number three is insulated bottles and cups. The dishwasher can absolutely ruin these bottles and cause them to lose their insulating properties, not to mention ruining their coatings. This bottle was put in the dishwasher one time and the coating is already flaking off. The fourth item you should never put in the dishwasher is cast iron. The harsh detergent will strip the seasoning, make it lose its nonstick properties and cause it to rust. You're better off just washing it with some dish soap. Finally, number five is plastic Tupperware. The high heat and harsh detergent can warp the plastic and cause it to leach chemicals into your food. So next time, think first before you chuck everything into the dishwasher.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I found out why Plato's Closet wasn't taking my clothes. So I am going to tell you what you're doing wrong. If you are bringing your clothes in garbage bags, stop. It makes your clothes look like trash and they treat it that way. Instead, you need to fold every single item. It shows you value your items. Then, put them in a hard bin or basket. It's easier for them to look through and they will take way more stuff. Trust me, this is the ultimate master trick.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: The viral mashed potato hack that landed me in Newsweek magazine. Toss your peeler and grab one of these: baking rack. You're not going to believe what I'm about to do with the... oh, there it is. No peeling, no dicing, no nothing. The days of losing life minutes to peeling potatoes are over. We just boil these babies with the skin on and then set them aside. Grab a bowl, a baking rack, and cut your potatoes in half. Place it cut side down on top of the rack and squish. Honestly, this is so satisfying, it's hard to describe. You might want to make the mashed potatoes just to do this part. Add your warmed half and half, a little bit of butter, some salt, and mix them like normal. Light and fluffy without all the work. Follow for more easy tips!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Did you know you could take a pair of plates or a pair of container lids just like this and quickly slice through some grapes or some cherry tomatoes? But remember, you can't slice democracy in half. Elected officials must represent all their constituents, not just the ones that agree with them. And did you know you could take freshly trimmed asparagus stalks and place them in sugar water? And that will enhance their natural flavor and reduce bitterness. But let's not be bitter towards people who drive a Tesla. That's random and divisive. Besides, you can't tell what's in somebody's heart by the car they chose. Except maybe a Cybertruck. That's fine. And did you know that you can gently crack eggs before boiling to make them easier to peel? Okay, but you can't crack the foundation of truth and expect democracy to stay intact. Each of us are entitled to our own set of opinions, so far. But we're not entitled to our own set of facts. That's the hard boiled truth. Need salt.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes secos (Jump cuts) muy rápidos para mantener el ritmo.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you ate nothing but junk food for 10 weeks, how much weight would you gain? Well, thanks to Mark Haub, a professor at Kansas State, we don't have to guess because he actually did it. And the results are pretty crazy. So the experiment was simple: Mark consumed 1800 calories every day of only junk food. Doritos, donuts, even Twinkies. Now, at the end of these 10 weeks, Mark ended up losing 27 pounds. But that's not even the craziest part. His body fat percentage dropped by 10%, while his bad cholesterol decreased by 20% and his good cholesterol increased by 20%. Trust me, I know this sounds a little too good to be true. Would I suggest this? No. But this just shows the power of calories in, calories out. Now of course, Mark probably didn't feel the best and probably lost some muscle, but if you're talking about decreasing the number on the scale, it is simply calories in, calories out.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alto, debido a la naturaleza polémica y simplificadora de la pérdida de peso.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos (Jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This Diet Coke has something in it called aspartame. And you drink enough of these and they'll kill you. But the real question is: how many? Well, they did a research study and found the safe limit for aspartame is 40 to 50 milligrams per kilogram of body weight each day. So for the average person, you'd have to drink over 20 cans every single day to even begin approaching harmful levels. But here's the thing: aspartame breaks down into these three compounds. And studies have tried to link these to cancer, blindness, and seizures. But what most people don't realize: one egg has 10 times more aspartic acid than Diet Coke. A tomato has 4 times more methanol. And a glass of milk has 3 times more phenylalanine. Yet, I don't see anyone considering these foods unhealthy. Because the poison is in the dose. Yes, technically the chemicals in Diet Coke can be harmful, but you'd have to consume an insane amount before that would ever become an issue.

videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la tensión y el interés durante la explicación

You didn't check, a lot of people hate on this monster but they won't explain why. Fact is, caffeine is caffeine. This has 165 milligrams, this Starbucks has over 400. For most, the FDA considers 400mg of caffeine a day to be safe. You could drink two of these. You could also drink one of these. It is a very regular thing people tend to do. I'm just sick of being told by people who don't know what they're talking about that I'm asking for more.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This guy bought a failing sports jacket company for peanuts. Now it's worth over $13 billion. Back in 2003, Remo Ruffini bought Moncler for just around 1 million bucks. And what did he do? He didn't lower prices to sell more. He did the opposite. He wanted to turn Moncler into a luxury brand. Jackets that used to sell for $100 were now priced at $1,000. He stopped selling in Italian sports stores, opting instead for exclusive boutiques in fashion capitals like Paris, Gstaad and Geneva. By collaborating with top designers, he successfully positioned Moncler as the ultimate status symbol. Of course, it wasn't easy. Everyone thought it would flop, but then celebrities started wearing it. Suddenly, Moncler wasn't just a jacket. It was THE jacket. Now Moncler is worth over $13 billion. And Ruffini is still growing his empire while chilling on his 180ft super yacht.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'Business Storytelling' que fascina a la audiencia interesada en emprendimiento y lujo. El contraste entre el precio de compra y el valor actual genera un 'gap' de curiosidad que mantiene al usuario hasta el final.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Cifras de dinero ($1,000,000, $13B), nombres de ciudades y años (2003).
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Emprendedores, entusiastas de la moda, inversores y jóvenes interesados en el éxito financiero.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: El Resurgimiento de Moncler: De 1 Millón a 13 Mil Millones
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Imagen de Remo Ruffini con una chaqueta acolchada y una captura de pantalla de la valoración de mercado de Moncler resaltada en amarillo.

After over a decade of coaching women over 40, here is what I actually recommend. First, stop the chronic cardio. It's spiking your cortisol. Second, prioritize protein at every single meal to protect your muscle mass. Third, focus on NEAT—just walking more. If you want my full protocol, comment 'READY' below.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay CTA explícito, pero la autoridad generada impulsa a seguir el perfil.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you want to lose fat running is the worst thing that you can do. I've been running now for a month and since starting my appetite has gone through the roof and I've actually gained weight. I've been an online coach for 6 years and in that time I've completed numerous fat loss phases for both myself and my clients. One thing I've never programmed and never will do for fat loss is running. Why? Because when you're running your body burns a lot more calories and your body's carbohydrate store gets emptied. This ramps up your body's appetite as it wants to replenish the carbohydrates it's burned, making sticking to the amount of calories you need to lose fat very hard. So what cardio should you do instead? Walk. It's the most powerful fat loss tool and the best cardio you can do for fat loss. Why? Because it doesn't require anywhere near as much glycogen as running, so your appetite won't massively spike. It's also low impact on the body, meaning your body can easily recover from it alongside any weight training you're doing, unlike running which is high impact on the body. I'm not shitting on running, I actually enjoy it, I'm just making you aware that if your main goal is fat loss, running will make it harder.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 219... what are you doing? Sit ups. I want to get a 6 pack before summer. More like a 6 pack of beer if you keep doing that. Fine. What should I be doing? First, let's make those silly sit ups 10x better. Step 1: grab a pillow and place it under your lower back. This will increase the stretch you get on your core and completely destroy your upper abs. I never felt so much pain. Great job, but this is where we move on to the hardest part: the lower abs. I thought they were a myth. They're real, but also very hard to get. Okay so... how do I get them? The best way to work them is by hanging from a bar and doing hanging knee raises. But if you can't do them yet, then supported tucks will do the trick. I have abs! That was episode 7 of fitness secrets.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un formato de 'maestro-alumno' con humor, resuelve un problema común con un truco sencillo (la almohada) y tiene una producción visualmente atractiva con un físico aspiracional.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Taking a sauna technically can increase your muscle growth but only with one strategy. This study proves that using this sauna strategy can increase your human growth hormones 16 fold which plays a crucial role in muscle growth. For this to work you can only sauna once a week or less but you have to do multiple 30 minute sessions in that day. Then later that day you do one more round bringing your total rounds to four 30 minute sessions. And for the best results you have to use the sauna in a semi-fasted state so not eating for 2 to 3 hours beforehand.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I don't really show the bench press a lot of love because I don't like benching. But today I'm going to show you a top set of bench presses. Let us begin. Starting off with Jack's Fit personal best: 47.5 KG. Up next, Jack McCarthy's: 100 KG. George Porridge, oh sorry, Porret, you are up next: 115 KG. Time for Stewart O'Neill's. Stewart O'Neill is Hardbody's manager. Sorry Stewart, please don't shred my contract: 125 KG. This may sound like a weird request, but are you willing to swap 20s? Is that alright? Yeah, no worries. Cheers. And now finally, David PTs, a fellow squat god: 150 KG.

Now you can fill the front. Okay. And take another piece of paper and go right over top. And what you've done there is you've made a drywall sandwich with the piece of paper and the compound. All right. Now you have paper on the back, paper on the front, and you squish the mud in the middle, just like drywall's made anyway. That's perfect every time.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: How to take better photos. If you want your fit pics to look like this, but they end up looking like this... don't worry, we've all been there. Here's how to level up your fit pics. 1. Poses. Don't just stand there, do something and make it look natural. One way in which I do this is using a prop. Number 2: Location. Don't just let the fit do the talking. Simple framework: loud fit, chilled background; chill fit, loud background. No. 3: Know your angles. Make sure you're centered, not too much feet space - makes you look taller - make sure the camera's straight. And this is how you get your fit pics to look like this.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This guy has made $4,600 profit in one day using the technique he is about to use. Hello, hello! If you just want to take one step, we're washing all the windows and then the frames and all the sills. And we're just giving a bit of a discount for young women with their hands on their hip. How much? You're asking all the right questions. I can take a quick look around, count up all the windows and just give you a quick price back. Meet you back here in like one minute. When you finally get them to say yes and you go behind the house, you count the windows. How do you know what to price it at? We obviously do want to count the windows so we know like how long the job is going to take, but we also want to go to the back so that way we can find the dirtiest window so we can take them to that pain point. Hello, hello! If you were to like just call us out just you, it is normally a bit pricey. It's normally $299 for the whole thing, but while we're here we've just taken off our transportation fees. Would come down to just $199 for every window, frame, sill. $199? $199. Let me ask my daughter. How are you? I'm doing good. We hand clean like obviously all the glass, but also all the frames. We have a full guarantee as soon as we're done we can look around make sure it looks really good for you. I'll just shoot you a text and then we'll be by probably in the next couple hours. Awesome, sounds good. My name is Nick. Like I said, should just be in the next couple hours or so. Awesome, sounds good. We'll see you in a bit. Thank you.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido y saturado
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad por una solución simple a la satisfacción de entender una regla lógica de estilo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Save it or send it, friend.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you wear a black shirt, or black jacket, with black shoes, with a different colored pair of pants, you're going to easily create very nicely balanced outfits with very little effort. And it certainly doesn't have to just be these colors. Let's try a brown shirt with a pair of brown shoes and a different colored pair of pants with the corduroys. Another simple and reliable way to look nice when you don't want to put a lot of thought into your outfit. Call this the sandwich technique. Pretty much going to work for any style genre. The shades of the colors don't have to match perfectly either. Green shirt, the green boots, the green Swedish military jacket, and a different color pair of pants. Think of it like the green on the top and the bottom are your bread, and the white is whatever is in your sandwich. Sandwich technique. This is also another one of those great moves for when it's really warm outside. We've got the white shirt with some white shoes and a different color in between them. When it's really hot outside and you don't have a lot to work with, it's smart to have moves like these in the back of your mind. Save it or send it, friend. No one likes standing in their closet being frustrated because they have no idea what to wear. Save this for yourself or send it to someone who sometimes struggles with that. See ya.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica: la gente lo envía a amigos que 'no saben vestir' o lo guardan como referencia.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Every thrift store has a million t-shirts and they all have different tags. But some of them are more special than others. So here are a few to look out for. First up is Giant. Founded by rockstar manager Irving Azoff, Giant produced merch for legendary music acts, TV shows, and cartoons throughout the 90s. And some of their shirts, like this legendary Nirvana tee, can sell for over four figures. Next up is Fashion Victim, a company that was heavily involved in 80s and 90s pop culture and is known for its anime-related designs. Some of the most desirable Akira tees are printed on their blanks. And finally, they may look boring, but Bay Club is a tag to know. A lot of vintage rap tees with phenomenal prints use Bay Club blanks, and some of them sell for ridiculous amounts of money.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz de baño clara y frontal

videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito en 'incline walking is the move')
videos_analizados.guion_completo: A lot of people ask me what's better: Stair Master or incline walking for fat loss? I lost 60 pounds doing one of these and it's not even close. Stair Master burns a ton of calories, builds leg muscles, but it's tough. Most people can't last long on it. Now incline walking burns more calories overall, is easier on your joints, and studies show you can do it longer without gassing out. I've done both and incline walking at 12 incline, 3 speed for 30 minutes helped me lose more fat because it literally burns more calories. So if you want to burn that fat without burning out, incline walking is the move.

And I'll be honest with you, usually salads just ain't it, but if you're dieting, this one's actually alright. videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're 18 to 22 college ish age and you wanna become the first rich and I mean really rich person in your family, you have to do these three things right now. I'm Vivian, your rich BFF and your favorite Wall Street girly and I'm teaching you the financial education you didn't get in school. 1. Earn income. Shovel snow, mow lawns, tutor if you're a nerd like me. Doesn't matter, but you need to have money coming in that you don't need. And I know you're rolling your eyes but stick with me. Step 2. This is where I f***ed myself but you're not going to because you're smarter than me. I spent all of the money that I earned on stuff that I didn't need like my 18th little black going out dress. Your money is better spent on experiences and memories than stuff. Step 3. Instead, you're gonna start investing. I didn't start doing this until I was 23 but again you're better than me. The money you make that you don't need for necessities, you're funneling into your investment account. In particular, if you wanna set it and forget it, I would open up a Roth IRA, contribute up to $583 a month and buy a target date retirement fund set for 2070. If you started at 18 and stayed on this path, you'd have over $3 million in retirement. How's that for rich?

videos_analizados.guion_completo: So this is your muscle before you lift, and this is your muscle after you lift. Most people think workouts build muscle, but they actually start by breaking it down. When you lift weights, your muscle fibers experience tiny tears like this. Your body repairs these tears by fusing them together, making them stronger and thicker. But this only happens if you give your muscles what they need. 1. Protein to provide the building blocks for repair. Aim for about 20 to 40 grams of protein after your workout to fuel muscle recovery. 2. Rest. Your muscles need about 48 to 72 hours to recover. You should leave at least two days before targeting the same muscle group again. 3. Sleep. Your body releases growth hormone during deep sleep, which is essential for building strength. Comment [muscle emoji].

videos_analizados.guion_completo: These two groups lost a similar amount of total weight. But as you can see, these lost a lot more lean mass and less fat. In fact, in this group, they lost more than double the amount of fat. This study took two groups and restricted their calories by roughly the same amount. So what caused such a crazy difference in losses between lean mass and fat mass? It was sleep. This group was restricted to 5.5 hours sleep a night and this group got 8.5 hours sleep a night.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: The bank offers you a debit card and a credit card. What do you choose? Your friend chooses a credit card. Whenever he shops, he's spending the bank's money instead of his own. He also earns cash back on certain purchases. But he has to pay the bank back each month. If he doesn't, he'll have to pay interest. Luckily, he pays his bills on time, which helps him build credit in case he wants to buy a house or a car one day. Your other friend has a debit card. Whenever she goes shopping, she's spending her own money. Follow for more tips. Answer: Get both, but pay your credit card back on time!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is buttercream at 18°C and it's perfect for piping. This, is buttercream at 24°C and no, it's not too soft. This is perfect for achieving smooth sides when covering. Welcome to: Stop doing these three things if you're serious about getting buttercream to work for YOU part 2. Stop using buttercream straight after you've made it. The consistency of butter and therefore buttercream changes depending on its temperature. Knowing how your buttercream behaves at certain temperatures will help you manipulate it to your advantage. If you wanna learn more about buttercream temperature manipulation then stay tuned for my next video. If you can't wait that long then check out my course, Buttercream Cake Mastery. I've left a link for you in the bio. Whatever you do, don't just mindlessly use buttercream.

Greek yogurt bowls: we go from half a cup of almonds, three dates, to a quarter cup of almonds and we remove the dates. Not because dates aren't good, but because we already had enough fiber. Lunch: medium ground beef to lean ground beef. One avocado to a quarter avocado. Two tablespoons of olive oil to sauté the broccoli and peppers to grilled peppers and steamed broccoli. And I'm about to show you one of the biggest mistakes people make when it comes to losing body fat. So if you don't like making mistakes, then consider hitting me a follow. So how is this one double the amount of calories as our one over here? So for the 1200 calorie meal, I used this much olive oil. For those of you who think I'm demonizing fats, I'm not. Get over yourself. But this just goes to show how easy it is to over consume calories from harmless little things. videos_analizados.guion_completo: Both groups grew the same amount. Except this group trained three days a week, and this group trained six. The study found it's not about time in the gym, it's about the amount of sets per muscle group. They found 10 to 20 sets per muscle group per week was the sweet spot. videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is what your muscles look like when you don't take creatine. And this is what your muscles look like when you do take creatine. But what you want to know is: is it safe and should you be taking it? Creatine works intracellularly... that is a hard word to say... which in simpler terms just means it pulls water from this layer outside the muscle into your muscles. Which is why when you take it, your muscles look more like this than this. Is it safe? Proven hundreds of times, yes. Will it help you lose fat? It can help increase your energy levels both physically and mentally, so yes, but it's not magic. Will it help you build muscle faster? Yes, maybe even a third faster. So if previously it would have taken you three months to gain three pounds of lean mass, now it might take you two. How often should you take it? Every day, and for most people around 5 grams is fine. How do you take it? With literally any liquid. Which one should you get? The cheapest one you can find, any monohydrate will be absolutely fine. Will it damage your kidneys? No. Will it make women get bloated? No. Will it make anyone get bloated? Also no. What does it actually do? It improves performance output and more research is showing that it can help with things like depression, mood and memory. Yeah, you heard that right, it's pretty good. So it's definitely worth a go. And if you find simple no-nonsense fitness useful, then you might want to drop me a follow.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 12 m&m's, 7g of sugar. But this banana, 14g of sugar. So is chocolate actually healthier than your banana? Let's break it down. When you pop one of these, okay let's be real, probably a few of these into your mouth, the refined sugars rush straight to your bloodstream, sending your blood sugar on a wild roller coaster. Your body freaks out, flooding you with insulin to bring those levels back down fast. But here's where it gets tricky. That sudden drop, it leaves you tired, craving more sugar, and can even mess with your skin and your waistline. Now let's talk about that banana. When you eat one, the sugar comes with a sidekick: fiber. The fiber acts like a bouncer, slowing down the sugar's entry into your bloodstream, keeping your energy steady and your body happy. So if you wanna keep that energy up and those cravings down, then hit that follow button.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la sorpresa inicial por la mención de McDonald's a la comprensión lógica y finalmente al alivio/motivación de que se puede comer fuera estando a dieta.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: probably click the follow button so you don't miss any more of them
videos_analizados.guion_completo: this is how you lose weight by eating McDonald's and don't worry I'm not about to tell you to swap your burger because nobody's going to maccies if you watch my videos then you know by now that when it comes to losing weight you need to be in a calorie deficit if you don't watch my videos probably click the follow button so you don't miss any more of them losing weight isn't about cutting everything out that you enjoy people enjoy a maccies I'm gonna show you how you can save over 500 calories just from some simple swaps 500 calories over the course of a week adds up to 1lb you did that every single week that's 52lbs in a year and we're trying to do like for like swaps here so I'm not expecting you to change your beef burger to a plain chicken burger we have large fries a double Quarter Pounder with cheese and a large full fat Coke altogether this meal comes in at 1,385 calories which for most people is a big chunk of their daily allowance the easiest swap to make is switching a full fat Coke to a Coke Zero or a Diet Coke which means that you save 212 calories I think it tastes better second swap is that you change your large fries to a medium fries large fries are 441 calories medium fries are 337 and then finally you switch a double Quarter Pounder with cheese to a double cheeseburger which they're pretty much the same in a different bun altogether this will save you over 500 calories and you still get to have a double cheeseburger fries and a drink but it doesn't take up all your calories for the day and that way you can still enjoy things like McDonald's whilst on a weight loss journey so it doesn't have to be boring you just have to get clever with it

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're REALLY a millionaire, why aren't we flying first class? I'd rather take the train, it's cheaper and there's more room to spread out! Okay, fine, but we're staying in a luxury hotel, right? Private spa? Personal chef? Nah, I've just booked us a cozy cabin, plenty of room for everyone, and it's got a fireplace! A cabin?! Alright millionaire, let's go eat at that Michelin-starred restaurant! Nah, I'd rather stop at that little café on the slope and get a crepe. A café? Seriously?! Let me get this straight, you don't fly first class, stay in luxury hotels or eat at fancy restaurants? You're not a millionaire?! Being rich isn't about showing off... it's about having the freedom to choose whatever makes YOU happy.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Just because you buy her flowers doesn't make you a good boyfriend. Because every single other man did this too. But it does give you signs that you can be a great one, because this has a deeper meaning. I asked my girlfriend the biggest thing that stood me apart from the other men she's dated and she said it's my reliability as a boyfriend, and that comes from being consistent with my emotions. Many men are consistent with their work. Many men are consistent with what they eat. But few men are consistent with their emotions. Do you get cold when something upsets you? Do you distance without telling her you're mentally unwell? Do you lash out and then apologize a few minutes later? If you do, cut that shit out. Even though she's an emotional wreck and you're more stoic, she needs to rely on your emotional maturity. This does not mean to tolerate her emotional distress, but it does mean to do your part in managing your emotions. The thing that helped her feel reassured last week has worn off. And so you have to reassure her again. But you can only do that if the way you act and feel towards her is consistent. If you can consistently give her the reassurance that she needs, it won't solve anything for good. This is a never-ending thing, but it will help her feel loved. Because when another dusty asks to buy her flowers, she can say...

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Tap that follow if you want that fat loss journey to become a whole lot easier.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is what 100 calories of Biscoff looks like. And this is what 100 calories of almonds looks like, that's 16 grams. And this right here is 100 calories of dry fruit or raisins. And this is what 100 calories of a protein brownie looks like. And this is what 100 calories of olive oil looks like... Oh shit, I went a bit over. 100 calories of olive oil right there. That right there is 500 calories. Now if you started eating 500 calories less on a daily basis, you'd be in a pretty good calorie deficit, meaning you'd be dropping about half a kilo of body fat every single week, which is probably close to an actual kilo on the scale. Now, this is also 100 calories worth of strawberries. It's actually a little bit less than 100 calories, I couldn't even fit it in the bowl. That, you could probably eat all of that and you'd be more full than if you ate all of this in one hit. So simply just start weighing and tracking your foods, probably tap that follow if you want that fat loss journey to become a whole lot easier.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: If you're weighing yourself just once per week, then you are fucked. And here's why.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural de ventana combinada con luces de techo de cocina.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de lenguaje fuerte y una declaración contraintuitiva que desafía la sabiduría convencional.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Shock inicial -> Curiosidad -> Frustración compartida -> Alivio/Claridad -> Confianza
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Drop me a follow (insertado orgánicamente a mitad del video).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're weighing yourself just once per week, then you are fucked. And here's why. Every single time you step on the scales, this is what happens. You have your muscle and bones, which I assume you wanna keep. Undigested food, and yes, it does look like a poo on purpose. Body fat you're trying to get rid of, and if you need any help with that, then drop me a follow. And finally water and other bodily fluids. Let's say you weigh in on a Friday and you are 80 kilos. You then spend all week absolutely nailing your targets, sticking to your calorie deficit, getting your steps done, going to the gym. But when you step on the scales the following Friday, it reads 82kg you fat shite. A bit rude, isn't it? This sends you into a spiral because you immediately assume those two kilos are body fat and you think 'fuck this' and you start deepthroating chocolate bars. But here's what's actually happened. You've actually lost body fat thanks to your calorie deficit. You've gained a bit of muscle thanks to going to the gym. You're holding on to more water thanks to the high carb meal you had the day before. And most importantly, you are due one almighty trip to the toilet. So as you can see, weighing yourself once per week is pretty fucking silly. You end up having a panic attack every time your weight goes up, even though it's usually caused by just a big poo. Instead of doing this, start weighing yourself every single day so you can work out your average weekly weight, which is a far more accurate way of measuring fat loss.

videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación con setup gamer, oficinas corporativas modernas y paisajes naturales de Islandia.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: LET ME DE-INFLUENCE YOU FROM GETTING INTO TECH
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luces LED RGB ambientales en el setup y luz natural en exteriores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una tendencia popular (de-influencing) aplicada a una carrera profesional aspiracional para generar curiosidad inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Expectativa vs Realidad / De-influencing
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) muy rápidos para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Tonos fríos y saturados en el setup; tonos naturales y vibrantes en exteriores.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con curiosidad, pasa por la envidia (lujos), luego a la empatía (estrés) y termina en una motivación realista.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Una pregunta final que invita a la reflexión ('Can you try again?').
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Let me de-influence you from getting into tech. A lot of tech content, mine included, shows the highs. The free food, the salary, the silent nod of approval from your Asian parents when you get Google stamped on your resume. But no one talks about the downs. First off, you will hate your life if you're just doing this for the money. I am not saying you need to be some sort of prodigy like coding a weather app in your mother's womb, but you do need to love it more than you hate it, because you will hate it. Also, the prod server will go down on a Friday night. I swear it's always Fridays. And imposter syndrome? It's not a bug, it's a feature. You'll feel like a fraud even after you fix the impossible. I only started coding in uni. There were moments I thought it wasn't for me. But when your code compiles? It felt better than being loved. I'm also not kidding when I say I booked a flight to Iceland after 4 months of leetcoding. Solving DP problems for hours? Yeah, I kind of forgot what joy felt like. Had to literally touch grass. There's no shortcut here. No 500k salary with 2 hours of work as some people try to sell you. There's a certain loneliness in chasing something bigger. You'll fail pretty often. The question is, can you try again? If yes, then you're in.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: It's time to throw away your microphone, you don't need it anymore. Because it's being replaced by AI. AI just leveled up again and this time it's replacing hardware, including your $400 Shure SM7B. If you were thinking about buying a microphone, don't! Watch this video first. This website uses a feature called Magic Dust to enhance your audio. In fact, this whole time you've been listening to the AI enhanced version of my audio. This is what I sound like not using the software. If you want to save yourself $400 and show yourself sounding like a pro, the software is called Podcastle. All you have to do is upload your video, then right-click and click Magic Dust AI. Just like that, now your video sounds like a freakin' Joe Rogan podcast. Comment 'Magic' and I'll send you the link and send this to a creator who sounds like they record through a potato.

videos_analizados.hook_template: They said, “(insert famous cliché or quote)” That's a lie. Don't (insert action) until you've done this one thing.
videos_analizados.escenario: Oficina moderna, cocina minimalista, pasillos corporativos, jardín exterior.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: They said just put your head down, work hard and someone will notice. That's a lie.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial / Oficina - Muy limpia y profesional
equipamiento: Cámara o Smartphone de alta gama
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Pattern Interrupt (Interrupción de patrón) al desafiar una creencia popular profundamente arraigada.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Myth-busting / Listicle de consejos de vida.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos al ritmo de la música y transiciones de movimiento.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Limpio, con tonos neutros y alta luminosidad, estilo 'aesthetic' corporativo.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con desilusión/realismo, pasa por la frustración de ser ignorado, y termina en empoderamiento y control personal.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Seguir la serie ('the next one's tomorrow').
videos_analizados.guion_completo: They said just put your head down, work hard and someone will notice. That's a lie. This is 30 lessons by 30. Own your career. I know you already work hard. You're first to come, last to leave. You spend Sundays prepping for Mondays and something about being the dependable one makes you proud. But people will take advantage of that. Make promises they retract, give you false goals and erase you from the credits. Don't be fooled. Nobody cares about your career so you have to own it. After all, the best way to predict the future is to create it. I don't mean don't work hard, but working hard does not mean you'll get promoted. You've seen that before. Getting promoted is a whole other game. Learn it. If you don't get it in writing, it doesn't exist. Save your receipts. Don't assume they're looking out for you. Be explicit with what you want. And if it's ever vague, always clarify. This world is full of carrots on a stick. Don't chase them. Grow your own garden instead. This is 30 lessons by 30 and the next one's tomorrow.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Directo, asertivo y basado en lecciones de vida (storytelling educativo).
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta identificación; la gente lo envía a colegas o lo guarda como recordatorio personal de su valor.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y dar una sensación de sofisticación.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Ataca un dolor común del trabajador moderno (el agotamiento sin recompensa) y ofrece una sensación de control y superioridad táctica.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Tipografía elegante y minimalista que resalta palabras clave como 'OWN YOUR CAREER', 'FALSE GOALS', 'BE EXPLICIT'.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 6-10 horas (múltiples locaciones y cambios de vestuario)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes profesionales, millennials en corporativos, personas buscando crecimiento laboral.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Own Your Career: Debunking the 'Work Hard' Myth
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La protagonista se recoge el cabello en un escritorio de oficina, seguido de un primer plano mirando a cámara diciendo 'That's a lie'.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Don't (insert action) until you've done this one thing. Stop using (insert item) for (insert result).
videos_analizados.escenario: Estudio oscuro con fondo minimalista.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Don't post on your stories until you've done this one thing.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz de tres puntos con luz de fondo azul (rim light) para profundidad.
equipamiento: Aro de luz o iluminación básica
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Pattern Interrupt (comando negativo) y Open Loop (promesa de una solución única)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de 'Hack' de productividad.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Contraste alto, tonos fríos en el fondo y cálidos en la piel.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la preocupación/incertidumbre (no saber qué postear) a la claridad y empoderamiento (tener un calendario listo).
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Steal it (con flecha hacia abajo indicando la descripción).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Don't post on your stories until you've done this one thing. Go to ChatGPT and type in: Generate a weekly Instagram story calendar in a table format featuring 2 daily story ideas for every 7 days. One story should focus on creating personal connections with the followers, and the other being more sales focused story. Subtly promoting my (product/service). I am a (insert career/business). My target audience is (x age, lives in x location, has x occupation), and wants to achieve (insert dream result). Make the story ideas quick and easy to create. Make a table with these ideas and give me pointers on what visuals and hooks I should use for each idea. Steal it.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Directo y basado en instrucciones (Prompt engineering).
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica extrema; los usuarios guardan y comparten para aplicar el prompt más tarde.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo sin distraer del mensaje.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina un nicho de alta demanda (crecimiento en Instagram) con una herramienta de tendencia (IA) proporcionando un recurso 'copiar y pegar'.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Texto grande para el hook y subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Stop using hashtags for better views. Instead, use these three Instagram settings to skyrocket your views. Step 1: When you're posting, go to Add Topics and then choose two topics that pertain to your niche or what you're posting. This setting helps Instagram to categorize your content and reach a bigger audience. Setting number two: Go to additional settings before posting and go to alternative text. Now add as many keywords as possible related to your niche and target audience. This setting will help reach people who want your content. This will help you gain a bigger target audience. Setting number three: Click on the caption when you're about to post and add a poll to ask a question to your audience. This setting will increase your engagement and skyrocket your views. Follow Creator Hacks for more value and better engagement.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural y Artificial (mixta)

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You cannot be financially independent before you enter your 20s. Um... you... here's my story. I've always dreamed of independence. I joined organizations, did a lot of work, got published, developed skills. Now at 15 I started earning real funds, business, freelance, spoke on podcasts, wrote projects. And at 19 my workload has expanded and I focus on diversifying and growing it even more. A lot even though I'm... Thank you little me. Financial independence... and if I can, you can too.

videos_analizados.escenario: Interior de un apartamento moderno con estanterías de libros y decoración minimalista.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: your life is boring because you don't have hobbies
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz suave frontal (posible softbox o aro de luz)
equipamiento: Micrófono (opcional, el del móvil parece suficiente)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Atacar un punto de dolor directo (el aburrimiento) para generar una reacción inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Listicle de consejos de vida (The 5 types of hobbies)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos entre puntos de la lista
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado, resaltando tonos de piel y el marrón de la ropa.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con una crítica dura que genera curiosidad/defensiva, transiciona hacia la inspiración y termina con empoderamiento.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Let me know which hobbies you're going to be doing in 2025.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Your life is boring because you don't have hobbies. You cannot just go to work, come home, watch Real Housewives, order Uber Eats and then call that a life. So let me tell you what to do. Okay, these are the hobbies that you need for a better life. Number one: hobbies that make you smart. That is reading, toastmasters, piano classes, language classes. Number two: hobbies that make you hot. Tennis, walking, pilates, whatever it is. Number three: I call this category hobbies that put you on. These are going to be the hobbies that help you elevate your social circle, meet new friends, mentors, sponsors. These are going to be junior boards, social clubs, members only clubs, art advisories, book clubs, wine clubs. The next set of hobbies that you need are things that make you feel creative, inspired or just childlike. This is going to be sewing, dancing, arts and crafts, cooking. And then lastly, if you can, hobbies that generate income. For example, if you love playing piano, start a YouTube page on how to play the piano. If you do hobbies right, not only is your life more interesting, it can put you on, it can give you career opportunities, you can make more money, you can have better friends. Let me know which hobbies you're going to be doing in 2025.

videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina/Comedor doméstico
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Pizza is actually a really good food for fat loss, however, we gotta talk about something.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz blanca frontal que resalta la musculatura y la comida
equipamiento: Micrófono (opcional, audio de cámara parece bueno)
Pizza casera y pizza de cadena
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Pattern Interrupt (romper el mito de que la pizza es mala para la dieta)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Comparación de alimentos (Homemade vs. Fast Food)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturación media, tonos cálidos para la comida
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con sorpresa, pasa por la lógica educativa y termina con una reflexión motivacional seria.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Apply using the link in my bio or reach out for questions.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Pizza is actually a really good food for fat loss, however, we gotta talk about something. You see, I made this pizza. Got the recipe from my friend Noah and it's about 900 calories. It's a stuffed crust cheese pizza and honestly, it's pretty freaking good. But there's a problem as I was crafting this pizza. This Pizza Hut pizza that I ordered for my son and that I can use as an example showed up. Now, after weighing and tracking to the best of my ability, these slices, it comes out to around 1000 to 1200 calories. But here lies the problem with these calorie comparison macro comparison videos. Because although this homemade pizza is double the protein, less than half the fat and a little bit less carbohydrates. Although the cutting and editing are going to make it seem like it was no hassle at all to make this meal, it definitely took me minus the filming like 35 minutes to make. While Old Reliable over here literally took three buttons and before I knew it, this pizza was here. And that's my point guys. Obviously, if your goal is to lose weight, heck, even just get your protein in, this pizza is going to be a lot better. But we know this and it's the battle that we have with how easy it is to order food that just might not be the thing that serves our goal right now. And so guys, it is literally all on you. Your reason why you want to lose weight, get in shape, get a little healthier, needs to be stronger than your want to order something convenient. And no, I'm not straight up just calling you lazy because you chose the easier route and no, I'm not saying you can't have pizza just because you have a fitness goal. However, you do need to know your options and that's what these videos are about. Now, if you do need help with this, you can apply using the link in my bio or you can reach out for questions. That's it.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un gancho que contradice la creencia popular sobre la 'comida chatarra' y luego ofrece una perspectiva honesta sobre la dificultad de mantener la dieta frente a la conveniencia.

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en fitness que luchan con la dieta y el tiempo.

videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador entra en plano sosteniendo una caja de pizza y mostrándola directamente a la cámara.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 3 things I would never do in my 20s if I wanted to look 20 in my 40s. If you look in the mirror and notice dullness or fine lines, listen up. Number one: skipping SPF even on cloudy days. It's the fastest way to age your skin. Number two: using harsh physical scrubs that create micro-tears. Switch to chemical exfoliants. Number three: neglecting your neck and hands. They show age first. Read the caption for my full routine!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I haven't washed my face in over 2 years and it's healed my skin. For reference, this is what it used to look like: super oily with a bunch of clogged pores. So I'm going to show you what my natural nighttime skincare routine looks like using natural and minimal products. Our skin produces its own natural oils called sebum, which is there to maintain a healthy skin moisture barrier and it's also antibacterial. But when we're washing our face morning and night and just using overall harsh generic skincare, we're completely stripping our skin of its moisture. And so to counteract that, it's going to produce even more sebum. That's what's going to give us that oily look and along with that, dead skin cells clog our pores. So sebum isn't the issue, the overproduction of sebum is. So instead what I do is that a couple nights a week I'll do a raw honey face mask. This is great to draw moisture into the skin and also has great antibacterial properties. I then rinse my face with water in the shower and moisturize with beef tallow. It's bioidentical to the sebum our skin produces so it makes it deeply moisturizing and nourishing. I really like it because it's very gentle on my skin without irritating it. Lastly, on the days that I wear makeup, I like to oil cleanse. It melts everything off and that is my super minimal and natural skincare routine.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: High protein meals that don't taste like pure bootyhole. Breakfast edition. I keep my breakfast requirements simple: done in under 10, +30g protein, mostly edible, keeps me full. Egg white oats. They smell a bit questionable? They aren't eggy. Me trying to convince you they don't taste like egg (the main ingredient is literally egg). 30g oats cooked in liquid. Water + pinch of salt. Use milk. Measure 200g egg whites. They're not cheaper (I can't do math). Egg whites: complete + highly digestible, all essential amino acids. TLDR: make you big and strong. Add egg whites to oats. When I say whisk, put your whole soul into it. Add in: one whole egg. Add ins: vanilla extract, walnuts, banana. Apart from protein powder, no one deserves that kind of suffering. Toppings: almond butter, cinnamon. 35g protein. Ridiculously filling. Not a single grain of protein powder.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My biggest regret in college was graduating with a 4.0. I graduated from Georgia Tech in May of 2022, but I signed my full-time offer in October 2021, eight months before. So that means regardless of my grades, regardless of even if I graduated or not, I still would have been fine heading off to San Francisco to start my big tech job. So naturally that begs the question: what actually matters if you're studying computer science or software engineering? 1. Your resume matters a ton. Follow the template that I have in my bio and if you follow me I do resume reviews. 2. And as much as I hate to say this, the school you attend to matters a ton. Certain employers only hire from some schools and the fact that I went to Georgia Tech helped me out a ton. 3. Projects. Start by doing a personal portfolio website and then start doing dashboarding projects because those are really impressive. 4. Referrals. You know when they say it's not about what you know, it's who you know? Well, in tech it's actually both. Reach out to people on LinkedIn, ask them for a referral and if you can get in contact with the hiring manager, bro that's golden. Lastly, stop pulling all-nighters, stop stressing over a B+, you Indians, and just chill out and follow me.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you drink this tequila, then yes I'm judging you. Clase Azul, Casamigos, 1942... you think you're drinking luxury tequila, you're drinking overpriced, over-marketed garbage. These aren't premium tequilas, they're just really good at making you think they are. Beautiful bottles, celebrity names, inflated price tags and almost always loaded with additives. You see, they're the Rolex of tequila. Certainly not the best, but definitely the most recognizable. So when someone demands one of these brands in a bar, it's not about the taste, it's about flexing something that they think other people will recognize as high end. So people always ask: 'Richie, then what is quality tequila?'. So if you want quality, try G4, Tequila Ocho, Cazcanes, Mijenta, Tapatio, just to name a few. You won't see any of them in music videos, but they will actually taste like tequila and not vanilla-flavored ethanol. You can drink whatever you want, but if you're flexing Casamigos in 2025, you're not classy, you're just easily marketed to. No shade... okay, maybe just a little bit of shade.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Cetaphil is trying to get this doctor's video removed from the internet because he exposes their product for being toxic. I would watch this now before it's gone, especially if you use this product. I'm a board certified allergist... Let's talk about the new and improved Cetaphil and the ingredient changes... first thing they did was remove pegs... changed cleanser from sulfate based to cocamidopropyl betaine... switched parabens for sodium benzoate... over 650 one star reviews... allergic reaction, burning, redness... sodium benzoate can cause itching, hives... cocamidopropyl betaine was named allergen of the year... still contains masking fragrance... CeraVe is toxic, Cetaphil is not that much different... owned by L'Oreal... pay influencers and retired dermatologists... Mark Twain quote: it's easier to fool people than to convince them they've been fooled.
