videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un hook de autoridad masiva, apela a un nicho muy activo (BookTok), y utiliza la estrategia de 'construir en público' que genera lealtad.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave como '350 books', 'terrifying', '75,000 words'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Lectores, aspirantes a escritores, fans de Haley Pham.
nichos_compatibles: Fitness (He entrenado X veces, ahora soy coach)
Cocina (He probado X recetas, ahora abro mi restaurante)
Finanzas (He leído X libros de inversión, ahora manejo mi portafolio)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: De Lectora a Autora: El Viaje de Haley Pham
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La protagonista acostada en una cama completamente cubierta de libros físicos.

videos_analizados.hook_template: This is a picture of my (insert what picture is of)
videos_analizados.escenario: Fondo blanco limpio con uso intensivo de pantalla verde (Green Screen)
videos_analizados.hook_texto: This is a picture of my first date ever with this guy named Ben.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox o anillo de luz frontal
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una foto antigua nostálgica para establecer una narrativa de 'antes y después'.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: How it started vs. How it's going / Timeline de éxito
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos sincronizados con el ritmo de la voz
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, brillante y saturado
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con una ternura juvenil, pasa por la admiración por su ética de trabajo y termina en una sorpresa total por sus logros financieros a tan corta edad.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, actúa como un video de branding personal y autoridad.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is a picture of my first date ever with this guy named Ben. When I was a freshman I spotted the most muscular 14 year old boy I've ever seen in my goddamn life, and I simply thought, give me. Anyway, 2 weeks later, my mom would drop me off at his house because I couldn't drive and we would have our first date. Little did I know that cute high school relationship phase would last for about a year and at age 15 we would really get into business. We started couch flipping, investing in real estate, reading business books together, becoming philanthropists. We fell in love with each other, became best friends and shared the same missions, values and goals in life. At 16, we got engaged and everyone thought we were f*cking crazy. 3 years later, we're 19 year old multi-millionaires, living in a house together, both never went to college, happy and married. Can't freaking wait to see what we do together baby.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La naturaleza controversial y aspiracional de su éxito a tan corta edad genera debate y deseo de compartir.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía y el optimismo de la historia
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el contraste extremo entre la edad y el éxito financiero. Rompe el guion social tradicional (casarse a los 16, no ir a la universidad y ser rico a los 19).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave en rojo y blanco con tipografía sans-serif gruesa (LOVE STORY, FRESHMAN, BUSINESS, 19 YEAR OLD).
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 3-5 horas (incluyendo recopilación de fotos y edición)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes emprendedores, Generación Z, personas interesadas en finanzas y relaciones.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Nuestra Historia de Amor: De Adolescentes a Multimillonarios
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La creadora frente a una pantalla verde mostrando una foto de ella besando a su novio en la mejilla.

videos_analizados.hook_template: X days/months/years ago I decided to (insert decision)
videos_analizados.ritmo: In crescendo (empieza pausado y acelera con la música)

videos_analizados.hook_texto: 3 Years ago I decided to change my life
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Natural en exteriores y artificial en gimnasio
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Storytelling de superación personal y vulnerabilidad inicial

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la insatisfacción y el esfuerzo humilde a la euforia del éxito y la maestría física

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 3 Years ago I decided to change my life. I bought my first jump rope to get back in shape. I wanted to get better so I trained 2-3 hours a day. Started uploading my videos on social media. Promised myself to never give up until I'm the best. And this happened. TO BE CONTINUE...

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato clásico de transformación (Before/After) que genera dopamina visual y esperanza en el espectador

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en fitness, superación personal y deportes

videos_analizados.hook_template: Yesterday I was at (insert location) when I noticed something (insert adjective)
videos_analizados.escenario: Parque urbano, oficina con pizarra verde, escritorio de edición.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Yesterday I was at the park when I noticed something weird.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y artificial suave en interiores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una anécdota personal cotidiana para introducir un misterio o descubrimiento.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Análisis de competidores + Reto público (Challenge).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos (jump cuts) y zooms de énfasis.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y vibrante, resaltando los colores de los dulces.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial -> Sorpresa/Confusión -> Entendimiento -> Motivación/Ambición -> Colaboración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow Joyride, like this post, and share it with a friend.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Yesterday I was at the park when I noticed something weird. Sour Patch Kids is the most followed candy brand on Instagram, but they post photos like this that somehow get tens of thousands of likes. The weird part is, on literally every single post, the top comments are from other massive brands: Oreo, Swedish Fish, Nutter Butter, Poptarts, SunnyD, Carl's Jr, Hanes? What does Hanes know about candy? I did some research and realized all of these accounts post practically the exact same content, and they all have thousands of shares. Since my goal is for my candy brand, Joyride, to pass Sour Patch Kids in Instagram followers, here's my plan. Step one: copy this template these brands use to go viral on Instagram. It took me less than 30 seconds. Step two: post it to our Joyride Sweets account. In step three: I need you to follow Joyride, like this post, and share it with a friend so we can pass them.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La audiencia siente que forma parte de un movimiento o 'misión' para ayudar a una marca pequeña a vencer a una grande.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y la energía sin distraer del mensaje.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el factor 'detrás de escena' del marketing y crea una narrativa de competencia amistosa contra una marca gigante, lo que genera empatía y deseo de ayudar.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra, números de pasos (Step 1, 2, 3), nombres de marcas.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Emprendedores, entusiastas del marketing, Gen Z y fans de los dulces.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Estrategia de Crecimiento: Joyride vs Sour Patch Kids
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador sentado en un banco de parque con un tablero de Scrabble, mirando su teléfono con expresión de duda.

videos_analizados.hook_template: X days/months/years ago I was (insert action) because I (insert pain point)
videos_analizados.tono_voz: Conversacional, reflexivo y por momentos dramático
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación juvenil con estanterías llenas de figuras coleccionables (Funko Pops)
videos_analizados.hook_texto: X months ago I was reading the Bible and single... so lustful.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural proveniente de una ventana lateral
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste entre una acción 'santa' (leer la biblia) y un sentimiento 'pecaminoso' (lujuria)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Testimonio de superación con giro educativo.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) y transiciones de zoom
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia en frustración/culpa, pasa por la curiosidad intelectual y termina en gratitud/paz espiritual.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en audio, pero el texto final 'IT IS FINISHED' invita a la reflexión.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: X months ago I was reading the Bible and single... so lustful. After trying to be like Jesus... I kept failing again. I give up. Then I realised: SIN IS NOT AN ACTION. Raw Faith Episode 6. Ken's Addictions: 1, 2, 3, 4... Voila, I found a verse in Psalms that made me really curious. 'Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.' What?? We're all born with it. So I thought I had to defeat lust, but it's in our hearts. Gotta be... oh. Did you see what He actually went through? Yup. All of that pain? Mhm. And just like that, we're saved? It is finished.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Identificación con la lucha interna y el deseo de compartir una perspectiva teológica liberadora.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar el arco emocional, volviéndose más intensa durante las escenas de la crucifixión
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Aborda un tema tabú en la comunidad cristiana (la adicción a la pornografía/lujuria) con honestidad, usando una estética moderna (anime, edición rápida) que resuena con la Generación Z.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave resaltadas en diferentes fuentes y colores (BIBLE, LUSTFUL, SIN IS NOT AN ACTION, RAW FAITH).
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes cristianos o personas interesadas en la espiritualidad que luchan con la culpa.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Superando la lujuria: De la religión a la relación
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador sosteniendo una Biblia y luego mostrando una foto Polaroid sugerente tachada.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Is it possible to (insert dream result) while (insert action) in X days
videos_analizados.escenario: Gimnasio, oficina minimalista o entorno exterior estético
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Is it possible to (insert dream result) while (insert action) in X days

Here is exactly how I did it. The results speak for themselves. Check the link in bio/caption for the full plan.

videos_analizados.hook_template: When is it time to do (insert action)
videos_analizados.escenario: Escaleras de ladrillo, calles iluminadas de noche, parques urbanos.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: WHEN IS IT TIME TO WALK AWAY FROM A FRIEND?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luces urbanas nocturnas con luz de apoyo frontal en el sujeto.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Aborda un punto de dolor emocional universal mediante una pregunta retórica con texto dinámico.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Lista/Serie de consejos con storytelling personal.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos sincronizados con el habla y cambios de locación.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y vibrante, resaltando los rojos y amarillos sobre el fondo nocturno.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Duda inicial -> Reflexión lógica -> Empatía por la historia personal -> Empoderamiento final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow for more (menciona que la próxima lección es mañana).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: When is it time to walk away from a friend? This is lesson number 17. Some friends are here for just one chapter of your life while others are here for the whole book. Knowing the difference saves you time, energy and peace. Surround yourself with friends who inspire you, challenge you and bring out the best in you. But when someone drains your energy, brings you down or limits your growth, that is your sign to walk away. After all, the company you keep determines the trouble you meet. I had a best friend of 5 years, we were roommates too. At some point it got strangely competitive from his side and if I shared a goal he would knock it down. For far too long I gave excuses for the ways he put me down. 'He didn't mean it that way'. In the end, enough was enough. I moved out, cut off all ties and to my surprise my life completely changed. All that time worrying about our friendship went into my own dreams. That's when I truly understood you are the average of the closest 5 people around you. Don't get me wrong, walking away is hard, but losing yourself is harder. This is 30 lessons by 30 and the next lesson is tomorrow, so follow for more.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta relevancia social: la gente lo envía a amigos que están pasando por rupturas de amistad o lo guarda como recordatorio personal.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Crear una atmósfera de confesión y reflexión tranquila.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'serie' (30 lecciones), lo que fomenta el seguimiento. Trata un tema altamente identificable (amistades tóxicas) y utiliza una estética 'clean girl' urbana muy popular.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave resaltadas en amarillo y blanco con efectos de brillo.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 4-6 horas (incluyendo grabación en múltiples locaciones y edición detallada)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Jóvenes adultos (Gen Z y Millennials) interesados en desarrollo personal y relaciones.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Cuándo alejarse de una amistad (Lección 17 de 30)
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Dos mujeres sentadas en unas escaleras urbanas, una se levanta y camina hacia la cámara mientras aparece el texto en amarillo neón.

videos_analizados.escenario: Campus universitario y biblioteca de estilo gótico

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural (exterior) y Artificial (biblioteca)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Curiosidad y beneficio extremo (ahorro de tiempo)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Desafío contra el tiempo / Storytelling de fracaso-éxito
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos y transiciones de zoom
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente saturado para redes sociales
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Confianza -> Concentración intensa -> Ansiedad -> Alivio cómico/Incertidumbre
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow to see what I got.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Is it possible to ace a final after studying for just 1 hour? So my Rhetorical Traditions final is tomorrow, and I have not gone to class in a month, so we're going to be doing our exact study method to get the highest grade possible after studying for just 1 hour. Step 1: Caffeine chug. Last time we did this we got a 95 on our previous final. So even though I am deviously screwed for this final, I got trust in the method. Now a lot of people ask me, what is this method? It's just a bunch of short-term memory hacks. But I realize one of the most important aspects to studying efficiently in my opinion is avoiding I'm Cooked Syndrome. It's that feeling that you get while you're studying and you're just like 'I'm cooked' and you mentally tune out. I used to do this thing in high school where I would study 8 hours the night before a test, but I would like just look at the textbook and my brain would not even be there. But it is exam day. I am cooked. Fun fact, I actually got points off on my last midterm because I filmed a video about it. Thus, I couldn't film during the test. 2 hours later... We just finished. I bs'd like every single essay question. I'm not even kidding. It was like 'reference the readings' and I was like I never read any of them. I think I relied more on context clues than memory for the exam. Follow to see what I got.

videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad extrema para estudiantes universitarios.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un punto de dolor común (estudiar a última hora) con un tono humorístico y una promesa de 'método secreto'.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave como '1 HOUR', '95?!', 'I'm cooked'.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 3-5 horas (incluyendo el tiempo de estudio real)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Estudiantes universitarios y de secundaria.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Desafío de estudiar 1 hora para un examen final
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Sujeto hablando directamente a cámara en primer plano con texto dinámico superpuesto.

videos_analizados.hook_template: After X years of (insert action/or habit) I stopped/started (insert action/or habit)
videos_analizados.escenario: Parque, habitación con estética gamer/coleccionista, iglesia.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: After 3 years of cycling through self-improvement and lustful addictions, I gave my life to Jesus at 19 years old because I realized one thing: I cannot trust myself.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y luz controlada (neón rojo/azul) en interiores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso del patrón viral 'Después de X años' combinado con una confesión personal impactante.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Testimonio de transformación (Antes vs Después).
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido en exteriores; contrastado con tonos rojos en momentos de introspección.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con una confesión pesada y vulnerable, pasa por la confusión de las reglas religiosas, encuentra claridad en la doctrina y termina en un estado de paz y propósito.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Invitación a unirse a la comunidad 'Creatopia' (gratis por 3 días).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: After 3 years of cycling through self-improvement and lustful addictions, I gave my life to Jesus at 19 years old because I realized one thing: I cannot trust myself. Welcome to the final episode of Missing Out. I've spent years indulging in many pleasures. I was basically lukewarm. Because most Christians would say 'oh you shouldn't listen to that song because it has swear words'. But using that same logic, that means playing Minecraft instead of reading the Bible is a sin. So how does it really work? Well first we gotta start off with the human condition. None of us are perfect. In fact, Psalm 51:5 says we are shapen in iniquity, which means we are born with a condition to sin. So now I knew why it was so hard for me to quit my bad habits, because I attached myself in this culture of instant gratification. But Jesus says 'lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, for where your treasure is, there will your heart be also'. Things like love, service, and peace. Understanding that without God, we are dirt. This is what real delayed gratification is: faith and obedience, letting go of yourself. So the answer to whether or not you're sinning is inside the word of God. It's not about '1% better every day', 'you versus you', because the Bible goes against every single human desire. Which is why putting my trust in Jesus through this gospel is where true perfection awaits. Amen?

Most students make the mistake of studying unnecessary material, so to avoid this go to YouTube and look up comprehensive reviews of your AP subject and paste the link in Turbolearn.ai. It'll automatically generate notes and quizzes for you that highlight the most important topics you need to know. This way you can study the concepts that actually matter.

videos_analizados.nicho: Lifestyle / Desarrollo Personal / Creadores de Contenido
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina moderna y oficina en casa
videos_analizados.hook_texto: So I lied to my parents for over a year and I've been living this double life as a content creator
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Suave proveniente de ventanas
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Confesión de un secreto impactante con alto contraste emocional
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Confesión vulnerable + Storytelling estructurado
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes limpios y transiciones de estilo analógico
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, ligeramente desaturado, estilo cinematográfico 'lifestyle'
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Tensión inicial -> Ansiedad/Vulnerabilidad -> Catarsis/Llanto -> Alivio/Amor familiar
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Stay tuned for part 2
videos_analizados.guion_completo: So I lied to my parents for over a year and I've been living this double life as a content creator, but I finally decided to tell them the truth recently and the experience was interesting. I quit my job. Yes. So last week my parents visited me and I decided it was finally time to tell them the truth about making videos online, this account itself, and quitting my full time job in tech to pursue more of a creative path. I sat them down and we went through this 78 slide powerpoint deck on everything that I've been up to in the past year. The thing is though, I couldn't get past the first slide without breaking down. Okay, so there's something I've been wanting to tell you, but I don't tell you sometimes because I don't know if you're going to get mad. No problem. But because you're both getting older and I just don't want to hide stuff anymore. I want you to know who I am because I feel like when we talk we don't actually talk about stuff that actually matters. And you know, Asian parents, I feel like they didn't really know how to react when I was crying. Eventually I did end up getting through all the slides and overall their reaction was surprisingly positive. You videoed yourself like that? Yes. How do you do that? Give her a hug. Take some kind of adventure, right? Not afraid of failure. It's good. Good job, we support you. So how do I feel now? I couldn't fit everything in one video so I'm gonna do a part 2. This has some really important points and things that I realized after that were unexpected, so stay tuned for that.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad (muchos jóvenes ocultan sus pasiones a sus padres) y el final satisfactorio (apoyo familiar).

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 10 years. It took me from living in a car to living in this penthouse. 10 years. It took me from bad acne to finally clear skin. 10 freaking years. It took me from working a job that paid me 750 a month to building my seven-figure companies and... I wish there would have been someone that guided me for making these ten years into ten months. And that's why I created the SHEEO Society. It's an app for women where for the first time you have access to business knowledge. Guaranteed personality upgrade through competitions with 500 cash prize. Live calls and direct connection with me. Kind of like a social media platform without all the trash. But guess what? This is all for just 1 Euro but only for the next seven days.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm Hank and I left my 9 to 5 job to start a bean and cheese burrito business. This is part 4 of how it's going. We went all in to fix up an old Taco Bell... we were gearing up to drop 2,200 burritos but the day the inspector showed up, we didn't pass. An all-nighter to knock out 23 fixes... we got the green light just in time to pull off the most intense burrito making session of our entire lives. Stirring beans in the 60 gallon kettle was one of the happiest moments. Six months ago I was cooking 100 burritos solo... now cranking out 2,000 in a commercial kitchen with four pros and eight drivers. Our last batch sold out in four minutes. But we hit a new wall: freezer space is the bottleneck. We're going to need an 8x10 foot walk-in freezer on our patio. We pitched the idea and they're meeting tomorrow to decide. Follow to see if it becomes a reality.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - muy luminosa y suave

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Reto contra reloj (Against the clock)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rítmicos con la narración.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, suave, estética 'aesthetic' y limpia.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con entusiasmo, pasa por la duda y el estrés extremo, y termina en triunfo y orgullo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Come back tomorrow to see the final result.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Come with me to make my own wedding dress. So a couple months ago I decided this was something I wanted to do. I've only made one other dress before, so I'm not really sure what gave me this crazy idea, but I have a habit of diving headfirst into big projects like this and figuring the rest out as I go. I only had two days to make this dress because the silk fabric I ordered arrived much later than expected, which definitely added some stress. But luckily I'd spent the past two months preparing as much as I could. I spent weeks researching every type of bridal fabric, taught myself how to use a serger, and even made a few practice dresses out of bedsheets. When the fabric finally arrived, I worked around the clock running on barely any sleep and honestly wanted to give up a few times. It was the longest and hardest 48 hours of my life, but I knew if I pulled this off I'd be so proud of myself. Somehow by the end, I managed to complete my wedding dress just in time. I can't wait to show you how it turned out, so come back tomorrow to see the final result.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La naturaleza impresionante del reto y la curiosidad por el diseño final.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar la narrativa emocional sin distraer del relato.

videos_analizados.hook_template: I started (insert business) at (age/life event) and I had no idea I would (insert result/outcome)
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina doméstica moderna, blanca, muy ordenada y estética (estilo 'clean girl').
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I started this small business at 20 weeks pregnant and I had no idea how many orders I
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy luminosa, proveniente de ventanas laterales en la cocina.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un hito de vida (embarazo) para generar empatía y curiosidad sobre el éxito del negocio.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de proceso (Behind the scenes).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para agilizar el proceso.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y brillante, resaltando los amarillos y azules de los Minions.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inspiración inicial, curiosidad por el proceso técnico y satisfacción final al ver el resultado perfecto.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (se basa en el engagement del resultado final).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I started this small business at 20 weeks pregnant and I had no idea how many orders I would actually get and every single one is so unique and I feel like I get to get so creative with my job. This order was for four dozen minion sugar cookies with seven different designs. And I think this is my favorite bake right now. Mastered the sugar cookie base but then decorating them is so fun. My tips for the cookie bake themselves is to make sure you take them out right before they're golden brown or else they're going to be dry and cracker like. And then right when they come out of the oven run a flat glass over the top of them to create a really smooth surface for the royal icing. I also love to plan every single design beforehand to make sure that I have something to reference and then to give the client to make sure that they love it. Once I got these designs approved from her, I got to making the royal icing colors and started to fill the bases. And another tip that I have is to stick with the meringue powder recipes. I feel like these come out so smooth with this type of icing. Yes, meringue powder is like stupidly expensive but it's a lot more shelf stable. Once I let the base layers dry completely, then I moved on to the designs for the cookie and something else that is expensive but worth it if you can save up for it is a cookie projector. I am very much a perfectionist and every single design has to be symmetrical so when I create a batch like this, a cookie projector makes that so much easier. This is not only just for cookies, you can also use this for other baked goods to project any design you want on top and I've gotten a million comments about using marker on cookies how it's not safe. Guys, they make food safe markers. I am not using Sharpie. Another tip is to dust them with cornstarch so that the colors don't bleed when you pack them. I learned that tip from Mack. She's amazing and it's one of the best tips I picked up so far. I was so happy with how this order turned out but the best part is the client really did truly love them.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Valor educativo (los tips de horneado y decoración).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente relajado pero productivo.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el factor aspiracional de un negocio propio con la curiosidad técnica de la repostería y el elemento emocional del embarazo.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: You know the feminine urge to open a bakery that's also a bookshop and sells flowers?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Luz de día suave, muy cálida y otoñal
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un meme viral ('feminine urge') para conectar con un deseo aspiracional común del nicho.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Expectativa vs Realidad (versión positiva) / Tutorial aspiracional
videos_analizados.color_grading: Tonos cálidos, saturación suave, estética otoñal y acogedora
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la fantasía aspiracional a la satisfacción de la realidad tangible y el servicio a la comunidad.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Inspiración implícita para que otros comiencen sus propios proyectos.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: You know the feminine urge to open a bakery that's also a bookshop and sells flowers? Well, this is my version of a small scale home-based bakery that I run right out of my kitchen... I may not have a shop on Main Street with big bay windows, but every morning I step into my micro bakery, I feel like I'm living in a romance book. It's just me, the smell of fresh bread in my oven and that quiet sense of magic that comes from making something with my own hands. I have a little stand outside where I display my baked goods for customers... I didn't need to invest in a big commercial kitchen or rent a space. I simply use the tools that I already had keeping my cost low and focusing on the fun stuff... My neighbors regularly stop by... It's a way to bring joy to my community... So, if you've ever felt the urge to start something of your own, just know that you don't need a storefront or a huge budget. Sometimes the dream that you've been waiting for can begin right where you are now with what you already have.
videos_analizados.costo_produccion: Bajo (si ya se tiene la cocina)
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La gente lo comparte porque resuena con el deseo de una vida más lenta y creativa (slow living).

videos_analizados.escenario: Mansión victoriana en estado de deterioro/obra
videos_analizados.hook_texto: It’s been six weeks since we got the keys to our huge restoration project.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - aprovechando la luz de las ventanas y exteriores
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de una línea de tiempo y un objetivo ambicioso
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso de transformación (Antes/Durante/Después)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rápidos (jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo
videos_analizados.color_grading: Naturalista, ligeramente cálido para resaltar la madera y la piedra
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la emoción inicial al asombro por los descubrimientos, terminando en una sensación de inspiración y comunidad.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow along if you want to watch us...
videos_analizados.guion_completo: It's been six weeks since we got the keys to our huge restoration project. I'm Milly, I'm 26, and me and my boyfriend Louis, who's 27, have just taken a huge risk buying a dilapidated Victorian villa that's been unoccupied for five years. It has a lot of character but needs a ton of work. In just six weeks we've discovered hidden windows, hidden doors, fireplaces, beams, beautiful flooring, and a couple of creepy objects. We are doing everything we can by ourselves, so luckily Louis has the skills and experience and I'll be learning as we go along. We'll be tackling five reception rooms, seven bedrooms, a cellar, old stables, garden, and garage. So we've definitely got our work cut out for us. We'll also be finding out as much as we can about the history of the house along the way. And we're just getting started, so follow along if you want to watch us put every last ounce of our souls into turning this wreck into an excessive home for two.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La escala del proyecto genera asombro y el deseo de ver el resultado final (efecto progreso).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Transmitir optimismo y progreso
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el voyerismo inmobiliario (casas grandes) con el nicho de 'hazlo tú mismo' y la narrativa de superación personal de una pareja joven.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is how I ended up at the NRL Grand Final picking up a camera for the first time in my life. Started with no experience, no portfolio, no job leads. I began learning special effects on YouTube, pulling all-nighters. Next thing I received my first job offer from Australian Oztag. The content I produced hit record numbers. Finally offered an interview with NRL clubs... I failed. But I didn't stop. I attended games and gained experience. I made an edit for Zac Lomax that broke club records. Then the Cronulla Sharks started following me. I did an edit for Shaun Johnson's last game, the GOAT delivered and SJ loved it. 24 hours before kickoff, I got a life-changing call. The Sharks secured me a pass. From an office in Sydney to the biggest stage filming The Kid LAROI. It's been a helluva year.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el arquetipo del 'héroe que empieza de cero'. Muestra pruebas tangibles de éxito (vistas y nombres famosos) lo que genera autoridad inmediata.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy buena, grabada en un invernadero/sunroom con mucha luz blanca.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente saturado para resaltar los colores de la ropa.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con la satisfacción del éxito, pasa por la intriga del unboxing y termina con un momento cómico/relatable.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: What happens when you buy a load of Ralph Lauren knits to resell... then you end up selling them super quick all on Christmas Day only to find out you clogged up your local post office. And that wasn't enough so you bought another box of stock to resell. But you then realize that I completely forgot what I ordered... so you get your trusty unboxing tool and open a late Christmas present. Whoa, I literally just opened it up and I see some really cool Japanese or Y2K style jeans. But I was finding brands I didn't even know existed... I found some Carhartt and Dickies trousers obviously because those sell beautifully. And these were all really good quality and not skinny but instead baggy which means I can model it myself and get a lot more money for it. But just after that I found something really rare... I thought I was getting a bunch of vintage stock until I found these. That's kind of crazy, I did not expect to get some Stussy t-shirts brand new as well... so yeah if you want these as well comment the word supplier. And then in here I've just got a load of Tommy Hilfiger knits... and it was all going well until... turns out the card was just full.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) entre B-roll y clips hablando a cámara
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, saturado, estilo 'Cottagecore'

videos_analizados.guion_completo: What if I told you that there's a way to run your very own bakery from home without needing a storefront or a big investment? Let me introduce you to my micro-bakery. Now I know what you might be thinking: 'Wait, are home bakeries even legal?' And they sure are, all thanks to something called 'cottage food laws'. These laws make it legal for you to sell homemade baked goods like bread, cookies, brownies, and more right from your home kitchen. The laws do vary state by state, but the basics overall are pretty simple. Often you'll need to register with your local health department and follow some simple guidelines, like labeling your products correctly and making sure that what you're selling is considered safe. That's things like baked goods, jams, and other non-perishable foods. And a great resource to start to find the laws in your area is forrager.com. What's great about a micro-bakery is that you can sell your baked goods in a bunch of different ways too: with a cute bread stand outside of your house, at farmers markets, or through pickup and delivery, which is a perfect option for apartment dwellers. Starting your own bakery from home is now more achievable than ever. With just your home kitchen and a love for baking, you can turn your passion into a business that brings joy into your life. Plus, who wouldn't want to make a little extra cash while baking cookies in their PJs?

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un deseo aspiracional (vivir de lo que amas) eliminando la barrera de entrada (dinero/legalidad). La estética es extremadamente placentera (satisfying).

videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La creadora saludando frente a un puesto de pan de madera estético ('Fresh Bread') en un entorno otoñal.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: I'll see you guys for episode 18.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I've been on [number] dates this year, and here's the one trait I learned you need to have to secure a second date with your super duper hot crush. Reverse Rizz. The ability to make anybody you're talking to feel special. Episode 17. Let's go. As dudes, we always want to impress the girl and have them think that we're special when they walk away from the date. And as a result, we only talk about ourselves. The problem with that is that it never actually works. Because as people, we want to feel understood and valued. So how do you actually have reverse rizz? You ask them interesting questions, you actively listen, and then you give them compliments. Genuine compliments. Let me give you guys an example. What type of person do you want to be in three years? You want to be a successful business owner so that your parents don't have to worry about you even if you don't become a doctor? I feel like it's very courageous and brave of you to go against what your parents want you to do, and I really, really respect that. I admire that in a person. I'm sure you're gonna do great. Genuine compliment, check. I hope that helped guys, I'll see you guys for episode 18. Peace.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave, título de la serie 'boyfriendmaxxing Episode 17' y lista de verificación en la pizarra.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Two years ago I was swiping through Hinge every waking hour because I was lonely and I wanted emotional validation. Did it help me find a girlfriend? Nope. But I did find out that I'm never gonna attract my future wife if I don't become the ideal husband. Welcome to boyfriendmaxxing Episode 1. In a world of hookup culture and red pill being mainstream, chivalry is clearly dying. 'Assert your dominance', 'Be a high value man', 'Don't be a simp'. 'Treat her like a princess', 'Provide for her', 'Emotionally validate her'. That's so confusing! What the f***? And if I wanna be a good father one day, then the first thing I have to learn is how to treat a girl right. I genuinely believe there's a right way to be masculine but still kind and compassionate. So follow along as I document and share everything from books, conversations and even dates in my process of learning how to actually treat a woman right.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el sufijo '-maxxing' (tendencia de internet) aplicado a un área emocional. Combina vulnerabilidad con una estética de 'High Value' sin caer en los clichés tóxicos, ofreciendo una 'tercera vía' entre el machismo y la sumisión.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y nostálgico, con alta saturación en paisajes naturales.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza divertido y ligero, pasa por la admiración de sus viajes, toca la confusión existencial y termina con una nota inspiradora y profunda.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, busca la conexión emocional y el guardado del video por su mensaje inspirador.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is Stella. We've known each other for 20 years, 5 months, and 3 days. And I've realised that she's great at one thing: the full send. Like when she agreed to come with me to the Arctic Circle on 2 weeks notice... or after going to art school, decided she didn't really like art and moved to Sweden and contemplated becoming an airplane technician. The problem is that even though Stella's great at commitment, she still needs to be 100% certain before she does something. Which means that a lot of the time she can end up a bit... directionless. I think it's the reason she has so many hobbies: she rock climbs, swims, and runs, so she can feel like she's moving forwards instead of standing still. And even though she never quite knows where she's going, that also means her life is one big blank page. Because if there's one thing that my sister's taught me, it's that uncertainty isn't the enemy, it's the start to a story worth telling.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Put your finger down if you told the entire internet you're going to create a website without having any idea how to start? Yeah. So I turned to the only person I can trust... ChatGPT. Let's start. I just finished a 3 hour tutorial on bubble. Spoke to some people who told me webflow would be easier. This is how I'm spending my Saturday night. Good morning, back to bubble like a toxic ex. Maybe getting ready will help. Found an instagram-esque template. We've got user profiles. Pass out. Engineer brother told me my formatting was off. Damage control! This is not looking good for us. Do we just give up? I failed. I don't wanna talk about it.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y luz de estudio/lámpara cálida en interiores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Identificación de un problema común (merch genérico de baja calidad) y posicionamiento como experto/solucionador.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso creativo con recompensa diferida (Part 1/Part 2).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos (jump cuts) y transiciones de barrido visual.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente saturado para resaltar el naranja del balón.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con crítica constructiva, pasa por la curiosidad del proceso técnico y termina en anticipación por el resultado final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow for part two to see the reveal.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I think sports merch should be better than this, so I've been taking matters into my own hands. In my last video I made tennis designs and I promised that the most commented sport would be the one I designed for next. I had to export 890 comments and after sifting through them, basketball was the winner. Right away I knew these black lines were going to be a big inspiration, so I held the ball up to my phone and rotated it to get these reference images that I then traced on my iPad and then brought into my computer where I began exploring these lines. But I felt like something was missing, so I literally went to the basketball court near me. There was a thunderstorm looming but I needed to get the perfect shot of the net swooshing like this. I promise you, just trust the process. There were many moments of doubt, but these designs turned out to be some of my favorite ones I've ever made. So make sure you follow me for part two to see the reveal.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: How I married my high school girlfriend. I started dating my wife in high school and then I broke up with her and then we got back together and then she broke up with me. I was heartbroken. I ripped up all her letters, unfollowed her and got off social media. I thought I'd never see her again. Then after a few months when I got my Instagram back, I saw the notification: Melinda started following you. I started hanging out with her again, reluctantly. I was scared. But then on my birthday, four years after our first kiss, she said: 'We should get back together'. I gave her every reason why we shouldn't date but she still wanted to try again. Then we started dating, but this time we did it right. We committed to always being best friends no matter what happened. We got engaged, planned a wedding and got married. I wouldn't have it any other way. And that kids, is the story of How I Met Your Mother.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La naturaleza aspiracional de las relaciones de 'high school sweethearts'.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My husband Josh and I got married in the infamous year of 2020 and when we got married, my husband told me he wanted to start his own business. He dabbled in a couple different things and officially started one other company but we decided to not continue with it. We ran into too many roadblocks and to be quite honest, we didn't see it as a loss, we saw it as a learning experience and we just had ideas flowing of other types of businesses we could start. Meanwhile, during all these business adventures that we had going on, I was falling in love with Tallow and just implementing it into every recipe I possibly could. We thought about selling my Tallow but I kept saying absolutely not. It's going to be such an insane amount of work and if I did it, I would want to do it really right and you know what? I was wrong. It ended up being way more work than even the amount of work I thought it was going to be. One day, I got particularly inspired and said, you know what? I am going to give it a shot. We started slow, we got a logo, I figured out what products I wanted to offer and I literally just looked at the calendar, chose a date that our website would open up but there is so much more that goes into running a business than just an awesome product to sell. Insert my husband who is probably the reason that you ever found my products in the first place. There is a crazy amount of work that is on the background of everything that you see and my husband takes care of the majority of it. But now I got him in the studio with me because we cannot keep up. We just can't believe what has happened since last May when we opened up our business. We've replaced both our incomes, we have a full-time employee now and we're having so much fun. And you know what? I just don't think that we're afraid to fail anymore because we see that every single failure has helped us get to where we are now.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You are not special is something I wish I could have told my younger self. Now as a kid, I was constantly praised for being ahead. I felt special, I felt gifted, I felt like I had so much potential. And then I got into my dream college. The moment that was supposed to solidify everything that I believed about myself. But the thing is, I wasn't the smartest anymore, I wasn't even above average. So my entire identity collapsed. Are you Tulio Sasaya because you're gifted or are you gifted because you are Tulio Sasaya? But what if being special was never the point? What if our worth was never about our potential, our grades, or how much success we had? And what if it was always just about being? Because when the titles, the praise, and the expectations fall away, the only thing that's left is you. And you're enough. You always were.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad (la gente se ve reflejada en la presión por el éxito) y valor inspirador.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un nervio común en la generación Z y Millennial: el 'burnout' del niño superdotado. La vulnerabilidad extrema y la calidad estética lo hacen muy compartible.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: i got engaged at 20 and got married by 21. if you are scared about marriage this is for you. my name is estée... following me because i wanted to share my experience to be married. 3 things i've learned as a young wife early 20s. the first thing: we're growing up together. as happy as i was married to my... a lot during because this was the first time moved out of home. there's a lot of change moving away from a new one. i very quickly independent leaning into of being married. the second thing: treat your spouse in who they are becoming. i was a completely different person got married looking back like i don't even... isaiah always chose to speak life and our future together to love me the hardest moments. the third thing: our individual relationships with God. our marriage especially coming from an asian background were actually more concerned than excited just because gossip going around but the reason why our future together despite not even exactly might happen we knew God we could trust Him easier to just for the good plans and soooo me what it's like i just tell them i've ever made and i'm grateful for bringing us together. 2 years already done so i'm just excited else He does!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 10 months ago I bought a laundromat with a engineering job. It was making around $2,000 revenue. Next 9 months my partner and I hit a milestone of 10k a month. Of 2024 every single day looked like this. To today, we're selling it. Financial situation: we're straight broke. Our overhead $15k a month with just rent and utilities. In our power, this laundromat as fast as possible before we ran out. Now we just have it back on the market where it was and it will likely take a FAT loss. This shit is all in the past. Now we're building our... as well as starting a brand new business with another dope ass partner. I'm documenting it for y'all. Go do cool shit, alright.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I hate to say this, but my wake up call wasn't as a medical student on my fourth 14-hour study day surviving off energy drinks and protein bars. No, my wake up call was as a first-year doctor when I fainted because I hadn't eaten all day. I promised myself I'd commit to meal prepping right there and then, and always making time for lunch despite how busy I was. And I've never looked back. And no, I don't prep every single meal and not every single week, but a couple of meals most weeks. And I get it, on the outside meal prepping can seem like a bunch of tupperware and bland chicken. But after a while, dare I say, it becomes an enjoyable and creative part of the week. You realize that when you eat enough consistently, you have this energy that spills into every other area of your life: your physical health, your relationships, your career, your goals, and your emotional health. If you don't know where to start when it comes to prepping, welcome to my page. I'll show you how quick, easy, and tasty it can be.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Is it possible to get an internship without your daddy being the CEO of Apple or growing up in the Bay? This is from the ground up episode 1. So I'm a freshman at Carnegie and I've applied to a million SWE jobs and I've gotten rejected from every single one. Freshman year internships don't matter. But if I want to be a founder, experience would help so so much. So I'm going to be documenting and ungatekeeping everything in my process of trying to be a founder and getting opportunities. I don't even know if I can get anything, so who knows where I'll end up because I'm starting from the ground up.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you're new to this channel then let me catch you up. This is me and my dad in the 90s and my family have owned Chinese restaurants since the day I was born. 1995. From doing my homework there as a kid to working in the kitchen as a chef. Eating and cooking was all we did, meaning I don't know anything but food. And after a hard day's work, this is what I cook for myself. If you want easy East Asian recipes to add to your repertoire, you've come to the right place. equipamiento: Micrófono (opcional para voz en off clara)

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturación cálida y vibrante en las tomas actuales para denotar salud.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con la frustración y el peso del pasado, transiciona hacia el empoderamiento mediante el conocimiento y termina con éxito e inspiración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow my journey so you can learn more.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Living in a Hispanic household has led me to having the worst relationship with food ever since I was a little girl. I was always told to finish my plate of food whether I liked it or not, whether I was full or not, and it led me to always eating more and gain so much weight as a 10 year old girl. And these are the three things that I learned on how to stop overeating in order to do that. 1. Portion control, because baby girl, there is no need for me to be eating that big ass plate of rice every single night. 2. Changing your beliefs. Just because you don't finish your entire plate of food does not mean you're ungrateful and does not mean you're a picky eater. It just means that you're full. 3. Mindfulness. Learning how to listen to your body when it's starting to get full rather than ignoring it has been essential in my journey. These sound like very simple tips, but they can make a huge difference in your life when you actually apply them. I learned how to build a healthy relationship with food, have lost over 30 pounds and now help other women do the exact same thing. Dieting does not have to be hard, but changing your mindset, beliefs and relationship with food takes work. And if that's something that you want to learn how to do, then follow my journey so you can learn more.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos de los 3 puntos (1. Portion control, 2. Changing your beliefs, 3. Mindfulness) y el título principal 'How I stopped overeating'.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: So apparently your frontal lobe does not develop fully until your age 25, so here are some things I've realized since mine came in. My parents were right, of course, and they were really right about the tattoos I got when I was 18. They said I'd regret them, in fact... Number 2: they were also right when they said phones... they just were. Number 3: I never regret the money investing in my hobbies. Number 4: every single second worrying about how my body looks... Number 6: burning yourself out isn't a flex. Number 7: slowing down, missing out if things... things that matter to you. Number 8: to feel everything, even the bad stuff, than feel nothing. Number 9: if you're doing something that's considered 'cringe', you're usually doing something right. Being seen to or doing things other people too scared to do. You won't ever become the best version of you while you're trying to be like someone else.

equipamiento: Micrófono (opcional, puede ser el del cel)

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) muy rápidos para mantener la energía.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I tried a 12 hour Harvard student exam routine, which is much harder than you think. Step one: wake up at 5:00 AM, but I made a big mistake, woke up 30 minutes late. Still got fresh, had water, looked outside, and sat to study at 6:00. The plan was to study for 2 hours straight, but just after one hour of studying, this is where I found myself at 9:20. It was at this moment when I realized there is no chance I can complete this challenge. But I'm not giving up. Had breakfast and sat to study for 2 hours straight. No excuses this time. 3 hours of study done, 9 to go. Took an active break till 12:00 and now it was time for our third study session. Right after 3 hours, my concentration level crashed. So instead of scrolling, I decided to work out. And out of everything, today was leg day. It's 5:30 PM right now and let's get back to the grind. Knowing that this is my last university exam, I went all in for the next 3 hours. Took a break, edited a video, had dinner, and again tried to study for one more hour. The challenge is officially over. Not 12, but at least I managed to study for 10 long hours. But that's not it, I even completed walking 10,000 steps, hit the gym, and edited a video. I did my best and that's what really matters. Comment down what's the longest you've studied in a day and let's complete this episode here. Hope you all liked it, meet you all soon, till then, bye.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You think you're a failure? Let me introduce you to my life. My name is Evelyn. I'm 30. No partner, no kids. No stable income. Still living in my hometown. No clue what's going on. And every other day I find myself asking: How did I even get here? So let's go back to the beginning. When I was 19, I thought I had it all figured out. I was gonna become a filmmaker. So I went to a pretentious film school in the UK and learned how to make films. But turns out that my degree was completely useless. So three years later I was £24,000 in debt. Was rejected from every job. Got dumped. Had the worst acne. No one even cared about my short film. So I ended up working at Zara for the next two years until I said f*** it and I did the most cliché thing. I backpacked to South America to find myself. When I came back, I was still lost. By 2020 I wanted to move abroad permanently, but then Covid hit and I found myself stuck, jobless and directionless. Which led me to finally start posting content and I shared my acne journey. I shared my acne struggles online and I found a community I never expected. But plot twist: I still am unemployable. No one wants to hire me. But the good thing about me is I'm pretty resilient. I still don't know if I'm gonna become a content creator, move abroad or succeed as a person. All I know is that this doesn't feel like failure anymore.

Meal 2 we had these unreal yogurt bowls. And meal 3 we ate steaks or chicken broths. And for pudding we ate these dark chocolate covered fruits. It's a bit sad how hard it is to eat non UPF these days. But once we got started it was crazy how big of an impact this had on our mood and our sugar cravings. Plus we actually loved the meals. videos_analizados.guion_completo: The first time I heard this I was driving, and my dad just dropped that line out of nowhere. He said: 'Stop looking for happiness in the same place you lost it'. Build a LEGO bouquet with me while I process how much that changed my life. I spent years trying to fix relationships and situations that were already broken, thinking that if I just tried harder, the joy would return. But sometimes, the lesson isn't in the fixing, it's in the leaving. As I put these pieces together, I realize that new beauty requires new foundations. What's one piece of advice that changed everything for you?

videos_analizados.escenario: Tren, estación de tren, oficinas corporativas de JPMorgan, comedor
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I had 0 sleep in 24 hours and landed a SWE Internship at JP Morgan. Here's how.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz de oficina y luz natural en el tren
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste extremo (sacrificio vs. recompensa de alto valor)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytime de éxito + 'How-to' encubierto
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación ligeramente alta
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con éxito, retrocede al esfuerzo, muestra el proceso emocionante, llega a la crisis de la madrugada y termina en una victoria profesional.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'match' and I'll personally DM you all my tips.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I had 0 sleep in 24 hours and landed a SWE internship at JP Morgan. Here's how. 3 months ago I saw a LinkedIn post about this hackathon and I heard people get internships from it. I applied and was accepted out of 15,000 applicants. Fast forward, I'm on an all-expense paid trip from New York to Wilmington, Delaware for Code for Good. After an hour and a half of train ride and Uber, we got to JP Morgan's technology center. First on the agenda was workshops where I listened to Jake yap for 2 hours and snacked away. We also had lunch and I met my team for the first time. Then the two participating non-profits presented their challenge which we're solving with technology. First we have to choose between the two challenges and decided on Spur Impact after a mini debate. This is my third or fourth hackathon and how I approach it differently is that I really took time to understand the problem. It is tempting to focus on building as many features as possible, making API calls, using a fancy tech stack. However, you will add little value without a deep and accurate understanding of the client's problem. For Spur Impact, the fundamental challenge was a manual and ineffective process to connect non-profits with young leaders. So I suggested we build a job matching platform that enables personalized ranking of non-profit opportunities for each candidate and automates a screening process for Spur Impact. 7 hours into Hackathon, we finally got a working landing page. I was mainly in charge of the LLM integration, finding better ways to embed slash vectorize the candidate's profile and non-profit opportunities so we can match them. There are apps out there that already solved this for tech recruiting. Rather than sending 200 applications with 0 response, they match you to the job that best fits your interest and skill sets. One example would be Massive, the Tinder for jobs. You can literally apply by swiping rather than fill out the same information over and over again. At 3 AM the frontend broke because of merge conflict. I absolutely did not sleep entire night and push a working prototype right before the deadline. Although we didn't win, I did get the offer call. Comment 'match' and I'll personally DM you all my tips.

Priced our estimated project cost at about 300K... we eventually found a lender willing to lend 90% loan to value... we thought our loan value was boutta be 333K... life ain't sweet... our 2.5 acres are subdivided like this... for some reason they'd only qualify the value of these 2 lots... so now instead of 333K we only had 180K... we needed an extra 10,000 out of pocket... we closed a couple weeks ago, dug the footers, don't blink is coming to life, follow me mane.

videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación personal, cancha de baloncesto nocturna, oficinas corporativas y bosque.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Artificial en interiores y luces de estadio/calle en exteriores nocturnos.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un hito vital (graduación) combinado con el formato de 'carta al pasado' para generar empatía inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Carta al pasado / Resumen de transformación (Before vs After de vida).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos entre el A-roll y el B-roll.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con tonos ligeramente fríos en las escenas nocturnas.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la inseguridad y timidez inicial hacia el éxito multifacético, terminando en una confianza humilde ante lo desconocido.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, el video busca engagement por identificación y guardado.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm graduating college in 3 months and I just wrote a letter that I wish my 18 year old self would have read. Dear Rajan, about going to college, you didn't go to a single party in high school, you were busy, but we know you were scared of social interaction. That's about to change. It's gonna be a culture shock for you. Your dorm roommate is gonna help you break out of your shell. You'll be awkward at first but trust me, you'll develop great social skills and meet amazing people. You'll be missing your best friends from home all the time, but you should focus on building new relationships. Sophomore year you'll restart at school. Don't make my mistake and use it as an excuse to be antisocial again. You'll miss out on a lot that year. But you'll buy your first house on your 21st birthday. Isn't that crazy? Your parents will be super proud. Summer after junior year you landed an internship at JP Morgan in Texas. Living in a new state was a great experience. Don't forget to call Amma daily, she'll miss you a lot. Remember how you wanted to start a business on the internet? Beginning of senior year you finally grew the balls to do that. I wish we started earlier. We're almost at 35,000 followers and so many people support our journey. We're even starting to develop land with a high school friend into luxury cabins. I'll document the whole thing. Stay humble, thank every person who shows love. They're probably smarter than you anyway. Get rid of that self-doubt living in your head. In the next 4 years, postgrad is uncertain and I'm scared, just like everyone I know, but I'm confident we'll handle business. Good luck, this will be the best 4 years of your life.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Shock inicial -> Tristeza/Empatía -> Reflexión profunda -> Esperanza/Determinación
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow along. I'm going to share exactly how I plan to rebuild mine in the next video.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm leaving my $350k dream job at Google to move into my parents' basement. This is why it's not insane. Six months ago I hit a wall. Depression. I started therapy, I started journaling, I even did a 10-day meditation retreat where I didn't say a single word. I kept hearing one thought that I couldn't ignore: what if the life I'm living isn't actually mine? What if I just built a life that I was supposed to want? In these 50 days, I'm going to prove that I can make my first $1,000 online without a job, without a salary, and without a safety net. I'm not a guru and I'm definitely not a genius, but that's the point. Like Dr. K says, if you're always looking for a guarantee, you will never believe in yourself. So if you've ever felt like you're living a life that isn't your own, follow along. I'm going to share exactly how I plan to rebuild mine in the next video.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación media, aspecto limpio de gimnasio.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Two weeks ago I went extremely viral for sharing this cue on how to get your very first pull up. A lot of you got your first pull up, but some of you still had questions. Alright, watch me take this stick and break it in half. That's the motion you wanna have. By breaking the bar in half, you're gonna activate more muscles in your back and not just use your arms in the pull up. And here's an example of me not using breaking the bar cue by just trying to pull up without my back.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad práctica: la gente guarda y comparte consejos técnicos que 'hacen clic' y parecen fáciles de implementar.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el dinamismo y la energía del video.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el 'Efecto de Arrastre' (Bandwagon Effect) al mencionar que ya fue viral. Resuelve una duda real de su comunidad, lo que genera alta retención y gratitud (shares/saves).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Incluye el texto 'Break the bar in half' y subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la voz.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en el fitness, específicamente mujeres que buscan su primera dominada.
nichos_compatibles: Marketing (mostrando resultados de un consejo anterior)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Explicación del Cue Viral 'Break the Bar' para Dominadas
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La creadora haciendo una dominada con una superposición de su video anterior donde se resalta el número de reproducciones (3.1M) en un círculo rojo.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y artificial en cines/estudio
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: POV inmersivo y planteamiento de un misterio común
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Investigación / Revelación de secretos

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la duda y curiosidad inicial, pasando por la intriga de la investigación, hasta la satisfacción del éxito.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito de voz, pero los créditos finales agradecen a los cines, fomentando la comunidad.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Have you ever wondered why movie theater popcorn tastes so much better than your popcorn at home? Me too, the only way I knew how: [visiting theaters]. And here are the 3 ingredients that most had in common. 1st: Kernels. Surprisingly, some smaller theaters use Orville Redenbacher's, but most use Preferred. 2nd: Oil. Some use canola, but most use butter-flavored coconut oil. But by far the most important ingredient is this orange dust. Thanks to Lee at Cinema Village, he confirmed firsthand the industry standard has been Flavacol. The secret ingredient to making movie theater-style popcorn. It's way more fine than regular salt. I wanna note 2 odd things: At some point, Regal theaters switched from flavacol to Season-It salt. I think a decent alternative. But who really stumped me was AMC. I couldn't find anything about 'Funtastic' until... basically the same thing, just packaged differently? It is! So now that we finally have all 3 ingredients, now we can actually put them to the test. Add the flavacol in the beginning because its fine-ness lets it coat the kernels more evenly as they pop! It's time for the moment of truth: Does this actually taste like movie popcorn? I think we finally nailed it! But remember that extra secret ingredient? I'm not sure if you're supposed to know this one, but I actually got the intel on it at Cinema Village: So that's it! That's the secret. And there's one more ingredient you're forgetting... Love.

I went from being in the cozy corporate world to working multiple jobs and I'm loving every minute of it. I spend my off days doing prep work, planning posts, and building my business. videos_analizados.guion_completo: My wife and I, when I told her I was going to start doing this, she thought it was the worst idea ever. But now it's actually paying our mortgage. And we're using things in our house like this Hocus Pocus spell book. So let me show you how it works. So first you have to go to Amazon and find that product and then just make sure there's a video on the listing. If there is, you can get your phone out and make a quick video showing how that product works and then upload it to the video section. And then when someone goes to this product on Amazon, watches your video and makes a purchase, you're gonna get a commission. Just like this person up here earns commissions. I've done this with a bunch of products that we have just sitting around the house and we're making commissions every single day. And these videos you don't have to show your face, they don't have to be long either, 30 seconds to a minute is fine. You don't need a website and you don't need to post these to social media, you just post them to Amazon. Plus once your video is up there, it's gonna keep getting views and commissions over and over because most products are sold thousands if not hundreds of times a month. So if this sounds like something you can do and you want to get started, comment the word VIDEO and I'll send you over a quick video tutorial that'll walk you through the whole process.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Confesión de errores / Storytime de fracasos.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación ligeramente alta para resaltar el rojo del chile.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con curiosidad, pasa por la lástima/empatía, luego al humor por la acumulación de desastres, terminando en una risa compartida por la frustración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay CTA explícito (implícito en el branding de los frascos).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: These are the dumb things that I've done during production for my chili oil as someone that has zero experience in food manufacturing. So one time I was measuring out chili flakes and it bounced back and went all over my face... and blinded me! So one time I had a major oil spill, and I lost 60 liters worth of oil and that was thousands and thousands of dollars down the drain of products that I just spent 8 hours producing... and I cried and cried and cried and cried! I have a labelling machine but I forgot to tell the printing company for the labels to print it in a certain orientation. So now I have to hand-label 3,000 jars of chili oil! One time I was using a machine to fill my jars of chili oil, and it got clogged... and so the machine exploded and I was covered head-to-toe in chili oil! One time I was pushing a baking rack of freshly-cleaned jars and I didn't see this lip on the floor and my jars went all over the floor and broke!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This painting single-handedly changed my career. It all started about a year and a half ago when I made a painting series called Numbers. The idea was to create a unique style of art based around iconic athletes and the numbers that resemble them. One of the first athletes I chose to do was the one and only Michael Jordan, the iconic 23. This painting went absolutely crazy and got around 20 million views across social media. After that happened, I began doing athlete after athlete, number after number, and the series began to grow. We've touched many sports like basketball and soccer, but also ones like cricket and Formula One. The series has grown so much that the originals have been sold worldwide to four continents and even given to the very athletes themselves. When I first started this series, I would have never thought it would grow to this, and I can't thank everyone enough for changing my life and inspiring me to continue creating. In honor of Thanksgiving and Black Friday, please enjoy the discount code BLACKFRIDAY for 35% off everything on my website. Thank you everyone for the past amazing year and let me know who you want to see next.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Have you ever tried to bake something that was supposed to look like this but ended up like... This? Yep, this is what my cakes looked like when I started baking. So if this has happened to you you are definitely not alone. In fact, it took me a very long time to learn to do the kind of cakes that I do now. I had a lot of trial and error and it would honestly have been so much easier if I've had someone to show me how to do the different techniques. How to create clean cake looks and in general just how to succeed with making entremet cakes. So if you don't want to do it the hard way like me. Join the Early Bird Waitlist for my upcoming online course: 'Master Entremet Cakes A-Z at Home' where I'll teach you my proven 5 Step-Formula to creating flawless Entremet cakes. Join now via link in bio and get my FREE Mirror Glazing Troubleshooting Guide + early access to a Live Glazing Class upon enrolling in the course.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow along at OrigamiComputing.com
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I just graduated from UC Berkeley to make this guy. It's like a Tamagotchi with tools to help you get off your phone. How can this little toy possibly help my screen time Sarv? Let me explain. Switching my phone off is the only screen time method that works. But I leave it on even when I want to be offline because of all of the useful tools. So I figured why not put all of the useful offline tools into one device and make it cute. So I taught myself electronics, PCB design, 3D printing, worked on this for one entire semester and now I have this guy. Now the same device can wake me up, be a timer, let me take photos, listen to music and record thoughts on a walk. No distracting phone required. Now I don't need to replace my phone, I have a sidekick that lets me switch it on only when it's essential. See I think the future of tech should be fun and colorful and good for us. I'm working hard to make that a reality. So if you agree, let me know and follow along at OrigamiComputing.com.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta relevancia del problema del tiempo de pantalla; la gente etiqueta a amigos que necesitan dejar el móvil.

videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de una premisa épica y de gran escala con una recompensa inmediata de datos.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: El experimento de 'Hice X cosa masiva para obtener Y resultado'.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y vibrante, estilo aventurero.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la incertidumbre juvenil a la acción masiva, pasando por el rechazo, hasta llegar a la iluminación y sabiduría compartida.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Pregunta retórica final: 'So why can't you?'
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I wrote a letter to the leader of every country in the world. Here's how many people responded. At 18, I had no idea what I wanted to do with my life. Get a job, go to uni, travel the world? So I wrote 200 letters to kings, presidents, and prime ministers to ask for some tips. 179 of them never even responded. And of the 21 who did, 11 didn't give any advice. But 3 years later, thanks to the 10 that did, my life is completely different. These are the five that stuck with me. 'Those with small goals will get small things.' 'Be as open as possible.' 'Be kind to yourself.' 'Be kind to others.' And everyone has that inner voice that tells them they're not good enough. But all the people you look up to learned to overcome it. So why can't you?

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina un esfuerzo extraordinario (escribir 200 cartas) con el acceso a figuras de altísima autoridad, entregando valor práctico en un formato muy digerible.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos en el centro, grandes, que resaltan palabras clave y nombres de los líderes.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: What happens when you pack your bags, get on a train, travel two hours in a car for a picture? You arrive in Edale, walk through a forest, cross a bridge, hike up to the top of a hill that looks like this. You try and get the perfect picture, think you've got it, then you head back. You get home, find the picture, only to realize it doesn't look that good. It needs to be a campaign asset, but it's the wrong size and the lighting is off. But then you remember Adobe Express. With one click, you can resize for any platform. Use generative expand to fix the framing. Use the AI rewrite tool to make your copy sound better. And just like that, you have a full campaign. You can try all these features on Adobe Express for free now.
