Not by your girlfriend, not by your boyfriend, not by your fiance, but by the market.' The market doesn't care about your feelings. It doesn't care how hard you worked. It only cares about the value you bring. If you don't provide value, you are gone. You are replaceable until you are not.

That person's gonna dump you one day and you won't be ready for it. You'll ask yourself 'if I just did this, we'd still be friends'. You'll start to think maybe I'm a bad person. But here's the thing: It's them. It's not you. Get off that hamster wheel. You are good enough for me, and more importantly, you're good enough for you. Don't worry, you've got me.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con sorpresa/descubrimiento, pasa por la educación/aprendizaje y termina en satisfacción/aspiración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Next time you're trying to spice up your brown eyes, definitely try this look out.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I was today years old when I found out that if you have brown eyes, Monica Bellucci makeup is going to look really good on you. So let me show you step by step how to get this Monica Bellucci inspired makeup look. You're first going to feather out your brows with a brow gel. Monica Bellucci tends to have a matte finish for her foundation, so I'm going to go in with the Ultra Teint foundation. Blending that out with a brush. I feel like Monica Bellucci is always rocking a bright under eye. I'm going to use that leftover concealer on my eyes. Now there's not much color in Monica Bellucci's face, so we're just going to go in with a very cool tone contour. Make sure to blend upwards. She uses her blush very sparingly. Going in with some powder, again she has a very matte base. We're going to bronze up a little more on our cheeks. I'm using a warmer bronzer on top of that cool one that we applied earlier. We're going to go in with a gel liner and we're just going to line our eyes. You're going to smudge it out with a brush. Then I'm going to go in with a brown eyeshadow stick. I'm going to place it over top of that black eyeshadow. With a brush I'm just going to blend that out. Then I'm going to go in with a very warm brown shadow on the very top. We're going to throw on some Ardell faux mink lashes. Mascara on both the upper and lower lashes. Monica is always doing a nude lip. I'm going in with a nude lipstick. And that's the final Monica Bellucci makeup look. Next time you're trying to spice up your brown eyes, definitely try this look out.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal para resaltar el maquillaje y los ojos
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Desafío a la percepción visual y validación social
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de 'Catfishing' (mejorar rasgos naturales drásticamente)

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la sorpresa inicial a la comprensión del proceso y la satisfacción del resultado final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Thanks for watching, bye!
videos_analizados.guion_completo: People are always shocked to hear that these are my natural lashes. Like girl, I'm already built flat so I can't have my lashes be flat too. So here's how to keep your lashes up all day and how to catfish long lashes. First, use a dark eyeshadow and an angled brush to drag a small line at the ends of your eyes. This only helps build a template for your eyeliner but also makes your lash line look more full. Second, use an eyeliner, I love this one from NYX, and make sure to only do a half liner. I think this kind of eyeliner looks good on anyone and it's not too heavy so it's perfect for school or work. Three, use this Shiseido curler, I swear by it. I would choose this curler over a man, except for like Jungkook or Byeon Woo-seok. But I start by curling the roots and then slowly moving up to the tips of my lashes. Four, Vaseline. The amount of times I've mentioned them on this page you would think I'm sponsored by them, but no girl, it's just my holy grail. I put a bit on this crusty ass spoolie I've been using for years and I brush the Vaseline on the tips of my lashes ONLY. Please don't get it on your waterline. Five, waterproof mascara and waterproof mascara ONLY. I use this one by Heroine Make from Amazon and girl it's changed my life. And make sure to go from top to bottom when you're applying your mascara. So start at the tips of your lashes and make your way down to the roots so your mascara doesn't fall the second you put it on. Last, if you want to be extra, you could put a small lash cluster at the ends of your eyes to kind of lift your eye but it's not necessary. Okay, thanks for watching, bye!

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial/Gimnasio - Clara y profesional
equipamiento: Micrófono de solapa (Rode mencionado en el video)
Software de edición con capacidades de motion graphics

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Debunking (Desmentir un mito) + Explicación visual clara.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, con alta saturación para que los gráficos resalten.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad inicial a la confusión (con el giro), terminando en el entendimiento y satisfacción por el consejo práctico.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito de 'sígueme', pero el valor educativo fomenta el guardado del video.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Have you ever noticed that some cables feel ridiculously light in your hands and others feel ridiculously heavy? Why is that? Imagine you're lifting 100 pounds on a cable stack in three different scenarios... [Explicación de poleas]... WRONG. Most companies already account for this... [Explicación de velocidad/distancia]... Imagine you have three cars... [Analogía de coches]... Stacks that move more in the same amount of time feel heavier... I would generally choose the one that moves a smaller distance because it'll probably feel smoother and in the long run it may end up feeling a bit better on your joints.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: You may have come across one of my reels and thought wow that girl has beautiful hair. But here's a little secret: my hair wasn't always this way. I used to struggle with bald spots, breakage and tangled unhealthy hair. It was so frustrating. Then I made the switch to natural anti-aging hair care products and in under 5 years my hair grew over 38 inches! After donating my hair I'm now on a new journey to regrow it. If you're interested in your own hair care regimen, send me a DM and let's work together to achieve healthy, beautiful hair.

videos_analizados.hook_template: After (insert time period) of having (insert bad result) come get my (insert noun) done with me. Hi I am (insert name) and I am a (insert negative label).
videos_analizados.escenario: Interior de un vehículo y consultorio médico.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: After 15 years of having hormonal cystic acne, we're finally getting to the root of what is causing this imbalance
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural en el coche, artificial fluorescente en la clínica.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Storytelling de larga duración combinado con la promesa de una solución definitiva.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso de sanación / Vlog de salud
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, sin filtros pesados para mantener la honestidad del tema de la piel.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Frustración/Resignación -> Esperanza -> Acción Directa -> Alivio/Expectación.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Stay tuned (implícito para ver la parte 2).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: After 15 years of having hormonal cystic acne, we're finally getting to the root of what is causing this imbalance that is giving me acne. If you're new here, I'm a firm believer that acne is a symptom to an internal issue. This is literally a red flag of something going on inside my body. Sitting outside the lab right now, we're gonna go in together, get some labs done. Don't worry, I'm not gonna show you anything, but I'm gonna take you through this process of getting these tests done and then of course I'm gonna share with you what the results are when they come in. So let's go get some blood work done. That was quick and relatively painless. I cannot look when they're drawing blood. It's not even like I have a needle complex or anything like that, but just something about the blood coming out... no, don't like it. So they're gonna send those back in and as soon as I get the results I'll let you guys know, but I'm very excited to see what they find, so stay tuned.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta relevancia para personas que sufren de acné y buscan qué exámenes médicos pedir.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente tranquilo y de vlog diario.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un punto de dolor muy común (acné) con una vulnerabilidad extrema. El formato 'Acompáñame' genera curiosidad sobre los resultados médicos, creando un 'cliffhanger' natural.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: COME W/ ME: ACNE LABS + Subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 2-3 horas (incluyendo el tiempo en la clínica)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Mujeres jóvenes y adultas con problemas de piel o desequilibrios hormonales.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Vlog de análisis de laboratorio para acné hormonal crónico
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano de la creadora en su coche mostrando su acné real sin filtros con texto superpuesto.

videos_analizados.escenario: Apartamento moderno, limpio y estéticamente agradable (estilo 'Clean Girl')
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Hi I'm Elizabeth and I'm an alcoholic and I hit my rock bottom
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - muy luminosa, proveniente de ventanales
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Vulnerabilidad radical y etiquetado directo para generar empatía inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytime mientras realizo una actividad cotidiana (limpiar/maquillarse).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos sincronizados con el ritmo del habla
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, luminoso, saturación suave para transmitir paz
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia en la oscuridad y la confesión, pasa por la autorreflexión crítica y termina en la esperanza y la redención.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (el video busca generar comunidad y guardados por inspiración)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Hi I'm Elizabeth and I'm an alcoholic and I hit my rock bottom in September of 2022 when I was 26 years old and part of me wishes I had some super juicy crazy wild night to tell you about but that's just my attention seeking alcoholic ego talking. My rock bottom was death by a thousand cuts. It was eight years in the make, eight years of binge drinking disguised as being social or being a 'Wine Enthusiast' and very expensive era and manipulating people to get exactly what I wanted because I lived in such a deep state of victimhood and believed that my life was harder than anybody else's. My amends list was like the longest CVS receipt you've ever seen in your life. More than anything 24/7 I was deceiving myself into thinking that I had any semblance of control over my alcohol consumption when in fact everything in my life revolved around alcohol. If there wasn't alcohol at a party I wouldn't go. I couldn't wait to leave a night out with friends or a boyfriend and get home so I could drink like I wanted to drink alone in my apartment free of judgement from anyone else. Throughout it all I was so deep in my addiction that I truly believed that the worst thing that could ever happen to me was never being able to drink again. That is how insidious this disease is. When people ask how I got sober and went from being a girl who couldn't go a day without a drink to 800 days without a drink, I try to explain that it's actually nothing I did. It was a miracle that happened to me. One morning I woke up and finally received that gift of desperation. Something in me decided that I'd finally been through enough pain. I wanted a new life and that pain led me to my first 12 step meeting and I've been sober ever since.

videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar la carga emocional sin distraer del mensaje
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el contraste visual de 'ASMR de limpieza/productividad' con una narrativa extremadamente cruda y personal. Satisface el deseo visual de orden mientras engancha emocionalmente con una historia de superación.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: Here's how I lost a year of my life and counting because I'm still very much unwell.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Natural en exteriores y artificial en gimnasio/hospital.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Apertura de un 'open loop' emocional y de salud con una declaración de alto impacto.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de 'Mi lucha contra la adversidad'
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos para mantener el ritmo del storytelling.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, con tonos ligeramente cálidos en los momentos de esperanza.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, tristeza/empatía profunda, shock por el diagnóstico y esperanza inspiradora al final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Mensaje de apoyo comunitario (implícito: compartir para concienciar).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's how I lost a year of my life and counting because I'm still very much unwell. It started with small aches, tiny pains that most people might ignore but then, my body completely gave up on me. By March, I was bedridden watching my body waste away as I dropped 20 pounds which I still haven't been able to gain back. Why was my body failing me? I've always lived a healthy lifestyle. This didn't make any sense. It quickly became clear that this wasn't something I could fix with a balanced diet or exercise. Doctor after doctor, test after test. It was first thought to be an autoimmune disease but the truth was far from that. A parasite hidden deep in my bloodstream attacking my red blood cells leaving me immobilized trapped in unbearable pain and crashing fatigue. It had completely shattered my immune system. This parasite is called Babesia, a tick-borne illness similar to Lyme's disease caused by a simple tick bite. It became so bad that something as simple as locking my phone or turning my car's wheel became moments of agony. And while I'm doing better now than I was back in March, this battle is far from over. I'm still struggling, I'm still learning, still fighting and along the way I'm learning more about this illness and about myself than I ever thought. So to anyone else fighting this similar battle, I see you, I hear you, we're going to get through this.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I feel really bad for saying this but sometimes you just need to cut someone out in your life. I had this one friend (let's call him Bart) and I always realized he had these tendencies to be very sensitive and overreact and get angry on certain things. If this is really trauma and they're like struggling from that, I'll try to be like very understanding coming from but it comes to a point where it's actually damaging for myself because they're acting in a way that I'm getting blinded. I of course want them to work on themselves but if I know they're not trying to work and there's still negatively affecting my life, I'll just cut off the relationship. I'm not doing it because like I don't know how to deal with it, but I just wanted to share the message with you guys. If someone in your life is blocking you from achieving and you know they're not trying to improve to build a friendship or build a relationship, you need to cut them off because you're just damaging yourself.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta identificación; la gente lo comparte para validar su propia decisión de haber cortado con alguien o para enviar una indirecta.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un tema universal (relaciones tóxicas) desde una perspectiva de 'amor propio duro'. El uso del micrófono de mano da una sensación de autenticidad y 'podcast' que está en tendencia.

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial -> Empatía por el esfuerzo -> Sorpresa/Preocupación por el acné -> Motivación -> Satisfacción moderada.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: This is your reminder to drink your recommended amount.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I drank almost a gallon of water every day for a week to my skin. As a matcha lover and coffee drinker, I know I get dehydrated and definitely don't drink the recommended amount. So I decided to use this gallon of water as my water bottle for the week and it was alarmingly difficult. I have about 8 hours of the day left and this is how much water I still have to drink. Unfortunately, I did not succeed the first day. And what's even worse is that in the next few days, I started breaking out so much and I'm not really sure why. But it really motivated me to start drinking more water because I feel like this will solve all my problems. I also started icing my face because I don't know, is that supposed to help your skin? Either way, I was able to hit the goal amount of water every single day for the rest of the week. It's now a habit for me to wake up in the morning and drink a lot and end the day like that as well. I definitely feel like I have a lot more energy throughout the day and feel less dehydrated. As for my skin, it cleared up a bit since the beginning of the week, not completely though. I'm still gonna continue drinking this much water because it is good for me and I need to stop being dehydrated. It has long-term benefits to my skin and my overall health. If you're a coffee or matcha drinker like me, this is your reminder to drink your recommended amount, especially the first thing when you wake up.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I spent $2,000 on a cow. Here's all the meat I got. Everything's grass-fed, pasture-raised from a regenerative farm. Starting with the daily driver: 64 pounds of ground beef. We got regular ground beef and then gourmet ground beef, which may be... I don't know. 64 burger patties, half of them had bacon. Now to business: the steaks. I got eight ribeyes, eight New York strips, eight sirloins, and eight fillets. Top shelf we got our roast, eight total, four chuck roast... beef, eight pounds of ground beef, sirloin dogs, and of course eight pounds of beef stew. 130 pounds of meat for $1,820. A half cow, and that's you sorted.

It's a lot of work but seeing the final result makes it all worth it. Which one is your favorite?

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I have 44 pounds of buttercream, 20 hours, and a freaking pipe dream to get ready for this pop up. It took everything in me to get myself out of bed and get the day going. First task of the day was sitting down to write out the rest of my to-do list for this week. And this is where the first thing went wrong, okay? None of my specialty packaging came in for my Valentine's Day pop up. So my assistant and I spent some time doing some damage control and coming up with a plan... [continúa describiendo la producción de treats, el fallo del horno 'Arthur', el almuerzo y el empaque de frascos]... I went to sleep, see you later for the final part of the series.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: So, I was just sending my 5 cold emails for the day when I saw that the founder of a company valued at 3 billion dollars has just replied to me. I had to grab my mic and record this because this is the reward of doing it every day. Welcome back to Week 2 of sending 5 cold emails every single day till I get 5 clients this month. I know all of you have been asking me for the email templates I'm using, but before that, let's talk about the results for Week 2. So, I reached out to 25 people. Open rate was 48%, accepted rate was 20%, and reply was 16%. Now I wanna talk to you about the replies that I got. So 1 person replied this: 'No thanks'. But hey, that's still a reply. 2nd person replied this: 'No thank you'. 3rd person actually gave me a Zoom call link. And drumroll please, because I got a reply from ZoomInfo's founder. As for Week 1, I got on a call with a founder and their team, we actually got on two calls and I pitched them 6k dollars and this was their reply... So yeah, let's see what happens after 30 days. And I have one more call with another founder this week. Now, what all of you've been waiting for: how do I write these emails? Hi, if you made it till this far in the video, I know you really want to see the cold email, but I really hate to be doing this, but I just have to do it in Part 2 because it's way too long and this video will not get the reach. I promise I'll upload it in 1-2 days maximum, so do give me a follow and I'll see you in Part 2.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Welcome back to another episode of (insert series name) where I (insert action). We are not even (insert time frame) into (insert year) and have gone from being a (insert before state) to
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina industrial de restaurante y cocina doméstica moderna.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Welcome back to another episode of Scale Down series where I take a dish that I made a $@#% ton of at the kitchen and show you how to make just a little bit less.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz fluorescente en cocina industrial y luz natural suave en la cocina de casa.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste visual y validación de experto (chef profesional)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Serie de contenido (Episodios) y Tutorial de transformación.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el ritmo alto.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido en las escenas de casa.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la abrumadora escala industrial a la calidez y manejabilidad de una receta casera deliciosa.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, pero la recomendación final actúa como sugerencia de receta.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Welcome back to another episode of Scale Down series where I take a dish that I made a $@#% ton of at the kitchen and show you how to make just a little bit less. A few weeks ago I made these stuffed shells filled with a lemony ricotta baked in a slow cooked red sauce and topped with lots of mozzarella cheese. At home the process is fairly similar but because we're making about four portions instead of a hundred fifty, I thought we could pop off a bunch of broccoli rabe and chopping it up very fine before adding it to my ricotta mixture with egg, parmesan cheese, and lemon zest. Oh and a little bit of garlic. I feel like the garlic and lemon help cut through the bitterness of the broccoli rabe. Once the filling is made, I set that aside and get started on the red sauce. I kept it super simple by sauteing a bunch of onion and a little bit of garlic in olive oil until the onion is completely translucent. I think that's the key to a really good red sauce. It's not cooking it forever, it's not adding sugar, it's being patient when cooking your onions. Then I added my canned tomato, seasoned with salt, pepper flake. And while that simmered, I parcooked my shells and then it was time to assemble. I opted for no mozzarella on top because I wanted to keep things a little bit lighter but it would have been good. Instead, I opted for these fried breadcrumbs which I made by frying panko and lots of olive oil and then I finished them with lemon zest, parsley, and some more grated garlic. If I can influence you to make one thing, it's these breadcrumbs. They were so good. So once my shells were out of the oven, I topped them generously with these breadcrumbs to create a nice crispy topping and yeah, I will definitely be making these again.

videos_analizados.hook_template: I worked (insert hours) in just (insert days) making all of this (insert noun) for my (insert noun). If you told (insert age) me that I wouldn't be a (insert
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina doméstica moderna, interior de un coche y mercado al aire libre.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I worked 55 HOURS in just 3 DAYS making all of the inventory for my pop-up.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural / Mixta - Muy brillante y cálida en interiores, luz de día clara en exteriores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Cuantificación del esfuerzo extremo para generar empatía y curiosidad por el resultado.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Detrás de cámaras (Behind the scenes) con final feliz.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener la energía alta.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido, resaltando los tonos rosas y pasteles de los dulces.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia en el agotamiento/estrés, pasa por la tensión logística y culmina en una euforia de éxito y gratitud profunda.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito de venta, sino un cierre emocional de agradecimiento ('I love you so much, I hope you have a good day').
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I worked 55 hours in just 3 days making all of the inventory for my pop-up. It was the most stressful three days of my entire life. I cried so much, I almost quit 100 times. And even though it was long and so much went wrong, nothing can take away the excitement that I feel on the morning of pop-up days. Don't let my face here fool you, I was actually really, really excited. I did hate my past self a little bit for not just decorating these cakes the night before because I did have to wake up at 6:00 AM in order to finish these before I even packed my car for the pop-up. But I love decorating cakes so I didn't mind. One of the designs was a fully pink vintage heart cake with a kitty on top, and the second design was a pink and red vintage cake. I think they both turned out so freaking cute. Once I finished the cakes, I finally put my face on, poured some coffee. Pookie was building my coolers in the back for me. I quickly changed and then it was finally time to load up my car. Shout out to Michael for helping me with this because those cake jars, you guys, in these coolers, oh my god, they are so freaking heavy. This was like life-size Tetris. It was so stressful but it all worked out. But you know what's even more stressful is a windy morning when you have to set up a tent full of baked goods. I literally could not even start setting up until 15 minutes before I had to start selling. As you can see, the wind was crazy. It kept blowing over my phone, the tent blew away a few times. One of my customers, her dad and brother or boyfriend, I'm not really sure who they are, but they helped me with the tent when it blew away, so shout out to them. I got all of my cake jars unpacked, my rice krispie treats and the macarons that I did have, put on my apron and then we were ready to go. At this point you guys, I look up and suddenly, I swear there was nobody there and then all of a sudden there was a line of so many of you. It made me so nervous, I was immediately sweating. I kid you not, this line formed in less than three seconds. And I knew there were people around but I didn't realize that they were waiting for me and I was emotional, it made me feel real good, I gotta tell ya. Everyone was so patient and kind. I wanted to talk to every single person in line. We got some amazing, super sweet, thoughtful gifts. Pookie got this clip of everybody waiting in line. I can't even tell you guys, literally every single person who came out was so NICE. The vibes were immaculate. I did sell out, but I had some extra scraps and there were still people in line when I sold out, so I was like okay, you know what, I have scraps, I'm gonna cut these up and give out some rice krispie fries as samples for everybody who waited. Shout out to Winter Park Biscuit Company for letting me use their counter, their knives, their gloves. I love these rice krispie treats, I think they're so delicious. I was really excited that everybody got something, even if it wasn't what they originally wanted. I freaking love the staff at Winter Park Biscuit Company so I left some rice krispie fries for them as well. They gave me some extra gloves so I would be safe handing everything out and then I was on my way, okay. I gave out some samples to people inside, all of the people in line. We took pictures and chatted and made TikToks and it was so fun. The wind was really picking up so I sat down to weigh it down, Pookie sat with me and then finally we had a second to eat so I got, of course, Winter Park Biscuit Company, the kale salad with the chicken tenders on top. It's so good, it's my favorite thing in the world. And I'm gonna use the last like 30 seconds or so of this video to thank you guys so much. The way you guys have supported me, I have been crying so much over it recently, it makes me feel so good. You supported me during my apron launch and my pop-up and online in my comment sections and I appreciate it and I love you and I see all of that. Shout out to my mom and Pookie for coming with me to this pop-up and helping it be so successful. My family stopped by, it was so fun. I literally can't thank you guys enough. It's looking like this might have been my last pop-up of the year but we'll see, I don't know. So thank you for coming, I love you so much, I hope you have a good day, okay love you bye!
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La gente lo comparte por la estética de los pasteles y para apoyar el éxito de una pequeña emprendedora.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un tono optimista a pesar de las dificultades narradas.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el 'hustle' (esfuerzo extremo) con una recompensa visual y social masiva. La vulnerabilidad de mostrarse llorando o estresada genera una conexión parasocial fuerte.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración palabra por palabra.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 72 horas de grabación + 5-8 horas de edición
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Mujeres jóvenes, emprendedoras, amantes de la repostería y seguidores de vlogs de estilo de vida.

videos_analizados.escenario: Apartamento, tienda de suministros de belleza (Cosmo Prof), balcón y baño.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: If you told 15-year-old me that I wouldn't be a hairdresser one day, I'd probably tell you to go screw yourself.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en el balcón y luz artificial en interiores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste entre expectativa pasada y realidad presente con un lenguaje directo y emocional.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: POV / Storytime de 'Por qué dejé mi profesión'.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) y transiciones de movimiento al caminar.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Nostalgia -> Determinación -> Confesión de vulnerabilidad -> Aceptación y paz.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, busca la conexión emocional.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If you told 15-year-old me that I wouldn't be a hairdresser one day, I'd probably tell you to go screw yourself. In my sophomore year of high school, I told everybody that this was all I ever wanted to do. I went to beauty school when I was 17 years old and I graduated less than a year later. I had big dreams of this glamorous life and tons of clients and who knows, maybe even my own hair salon one day. I really struggled for the first year or two, partially because I think I was so young and then also because I was just not part of this world. I grew up in a family that wasn't very high maintenance. My mom barely wore makeup and I think I got my hair colored once. But with time and practice, everything gets easier and I like to think that I was a moderately successful hairdresser. And while I really enjoyed it, I knew somehow it wasn't fitting. Even 11 years later, taking new clients for cuts and colors gave me a ton of anxiety. So I left the salon life and I don't miss it at all. The only reason why I'm touching bleach again is because my friend is getting married soon. And it was a great reminder of why I left. I don't miss chemical burns, I don't miss only owning black clothing, I don't miss hoping that I got the right toner. In some world, 15-year-old me would be happy that she gets to get chemical burns because I think that implies that I'm busy. But I think she'd also be really proud of me for going after what I want in life just as she did.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Mixta - Buena iluminación interior que permite ver texturas de piel.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito verbal, pero el veredicto '10/10' actúa como recomendación de compra.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I review all the products you want with the honesty that you need. Does this eye tightening cream actually get rid of wrinkles or are all of these people just trying to make a buck? Well today me and my mama are gonna find out. I bought the Peter Thomas Roth eye tightener and although it does seem a bit pricey for a small bottle, if it works it should be well worth the price. My gorgeous mama just turned 69 and I couldn't find many wrinkles on her so I was worried there wouldn't be a difference when I tested it, but she was happy to try. We had already put the no filter primer on the left side of her face which did tighten it a bit, but her eye could still use a little bit more tightening. So on the right side we tried only using the eye tightening cream. I put literally a pea size on her and left it to dry for 8 minutes like the directions said and we had her come back and this was the after. We did nothing else on the right side except use this and let it dry and it lasted for the rest of the day. It is a must. 10 out of 10.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Conversacional y basado en el método científico simple (prueba/control).

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un producto que ya es tendencia, aplica una prueba de 'antes y después' muy visual y utiliza a una persona mayor (su madre) lo que genera ternura y credibilidad real.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Someone just scammed my small business. You guys won't believe this. I won't name her, but this customer bought three of our viral purple gromwell cleansing bars and then she reached out to return them because they were 'too purple'. Like, what? It's the natural color from the traditional Chinese medicine purple gromwell. Anyway, she said she didn't even open it, so I approved the return. But when the package arrived, it was an empty mailer. Like, who does that to a small business? My mom handmakes every single bar with our family secret recipe and it takes her six months to make a super limited batch. My mom was so sad. How do I get the refund back? But honestly, I get why people love this bar so much. Like my customer Monica cleared her acne with it and cleared her daughter's eczema. Another customer got rid of her son's backne. The ingredients we use naturally fight acne, calm irritation, and the bar is 100% natural. It's been sold out for two entire months and my mom has been working so hard to make more. This batch is super limited, so RUN to get yours before they're gone again.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Can you please pack my $500 marshmallow order? Of course I can! Let's make and pack the biggest order we got this week for Rob from Canada. First up is one of our newest flavors, we just launched this last week and I'm already obsessed. We combined three flavors together to make the perfect bubblegum ice cream flavor and then we added hundreds and thousands on top too. And how good does this color swirl look? This order is for Rob's wife, he said she's so excited to try our new flavors and he pretty much bought every flavor in our store. So let's get it packed for him. First is four of our best sellers bundle and they take up a whole large box. So we need a second box for two packs of salted caramel peanut, butterscotch pecan pie, two packs of bubblegum ice cream, fairy bread, lemon lime, strawberry and hot honey, vanilla bean, peanut butter jelly, banana cinnamon toast, cotton candy and hazelnut rocher domes. Thank you so much for your order Rob, we hope you love your marshmallows.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm on a journey to find the best kids meal for adults and tonight we went to Dairy Queen where the kids meal starts at five dollars and nine cents. With the kids meal you get the option of chicken strips, hamburger, hot dog, a side, a drink and a treat. For the drink I thought you could only get milk and bottled water so I was pleasantly surprised when he presented the option of a kids fountain drink. Also the fact that he put root beer on the receipt and I didn't even tell him... King. Anyway the root beer was crispy and fresh. For my side I did choose fries which came in this teeny tiny bag. First I tried this arched one dipped in ranch. The ranch was okay, the fries were good. I don't know what it is about Dairy Queen fries but I like them. I also got honey mustard which was so sweet and tangy. It was decent for coming in a package like this. Next we dove into the entree which I chose chicken strips. These chicken strips don't look like they would be good but the breading was so crispy and crunchy. The chicken inside was hot and juicy. Honestly I was pleasantly surprised. Now there only is two so this kids meal is not gonna fill everybody up but personally I was satiated after that. Lastly is the treat which I chose a chocolate Dilly Bar. Again I didn't even know this was an option. He mentioned there was a Heath one and I wish I chose that one cause I just need a little bit of crunch. All this for five dollars I think is a really good deal. Depending on how hungry you are this might be the kids meal for you. Dairy Queen you're coming in at sixth place at 8.85.

videos_analizados.escenario: Taller de arte / Habitación con alfombra
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Here's how I made a worthless artwork that's valued at billions.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial/Estudio
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste de valor extremo (inservible vs. billonario)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Desglose de costos / Proceso de creación
videos_analizados.transiciones: Jump cuts constantes para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Colores vibrantes, alta saturación en los cobres de las monedas.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con escepticismo, pasa por la curiosidad lógica y termina en una revelación irónica sobre la economía de la atención.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Ver y compartir el video aumenta el valor de la obra.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's how I made a worthless artwork that's valued at billions. Pennies are worth $0.01 and I used 25,000 pennies so I'm making a $250 artwork. But pennies may be going away forever. The government wants to stop making them because it takes 3.7 cents to make a penny. So I have a $925 artwork. But a pile of 25,000 pennies has to have some extra rare pennies. If there's the rare 1943-D Bronze Wheat Penny, we have a $2.3M artwork. Though pennies are made out of zinc and copper and based on the total amounts of those metals I have here, it's just a $189.42 artwork. Except that it's an artwork. I bet I could get a collector to buy it. This could be a $5,000 artwork. Except plenty of collectors would hate it and not pay a penny. I am making a $0 artwork. Except an artwork can be valuable because of the artist that made it. And all of you following me combined makes 8.2 million people, which could make the argument that this is a $10,000 artwork. Plus, you all are watching this video about the artwork giving it validation and jacking up its value. The more successful this video is, the more my worthless sculpture is worth a ton of money. It's now your fault this artwork is worth billions.
videos_analizados.costo_produccion: Bajo ($250 en monedas que son recuperables)

videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo sin distraer de los datos.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza la psicología del valor y el dinero, combinada con un proceso de construcción visualmente satisfactorio (ASMR visual) y datos curiosos que la gente suele compartir.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: So we pretty much threw out our entire abandoned home into bins. And the last time I updated you guys, we spent about $8,000 on bins. And since then, we've had accumulated 16 bins now and spent a total of $21,875.78 throwing out our house. Oof. And if you guys want to know how much $21,000 worth of garbage weighs, it weighs 168,000 lbs. Wow.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: El dato impactante del costo y el peso total de la basura.

videos_analizados.hook_tipo: Afirmación de logro con datos específicos
videos_analizados.hook_texto: 7 weeks ago I purchased the 150k ex council house as a single homeowner with a 15k deposit
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural / Mixta - luz de obra y luz de ventanas
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de números específicos y estatus social aspiracional (propietaria soltera) para generar autoridad y curiosidad.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso de transformación (Underdog story)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente desaturado para enfatizar la suciedad de la obra
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con orgullo/logro, baja a la vulnerabilidad/cansancio y termina con determinación/esperanza.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Stay tuned for the updates
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 7 weeks ago I purchased the 150k ex council house as a single homeowner with a 15k deposit and I'm using TikTok to pay my way through the renovation. Is it my dream house? Definitely not. Do I want to live here forever? Also definitely not. How much am I planning to spend on the renovation and do I currently live here? No, have you seen the state of it? I'm planning to spend around 15k to 25k. I know it's optimistic but your girl's on a very tight budget. I'm not asking for money, I'm not asking for donations, all I'm asking for is time. 60 seconds. I know in my heart I would definitely watch a 60 second video from a stranger if it meant they could create their dream house. So I'm hoping that like my last video that got 3 million views, there's just some people out there that will have the kindness in their heart to watch. It doesn't have to be the full thing, even just 20 seconds will help push my renovation series out to more people. Honestly I'll appreciate it more than you'll ever know. Renovating a house is hard and I know it was my own decision, I definitely underestimated how difficult it would actually be as a single homeowner doing it pretty much all by yourself. My boyfriend is helping out a lot which I appreciate so much but I don't really have that many friends and I don't really have many family members to help out. Currently on day 45 of the renovation, that's how long I've had the keys and things are progressing really fast but really slowly. So stay tuned for the updates.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Inspiración por el esfuerzo individual y deseo de ayudar a alguien que está trabajando duro.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar el ritmo de la narración sin distraer
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el sesgo de transparencia (dinero) y la psicología de la reciprocidad (pide tiempo, no dinero). La vulnerabilidad emocional genera empatía inmediata.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y resaltan palabras clave como '150k' y 'single homeowner'.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Presentación de una nueva serie (implica seguir para ver los próximos episodios).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: My fiancé has been hiding this from me for our entire 7 year relationship... pretty important from me and I can't quite believe it's taken me this long to finally understand the truth about her and it makes me realize how men in relationships and just men in general about women by sharing something that's not only changed my perspective on life relationship for the better every week for the past 7 years my fiancé's changed so much mentally where I kind of felt disconnected from her until she told me about the truth behind women's hormones... this was the answer to everything. I as a male operate on a 24 hour hormone cycle which in theory means I wake up every single day and on the other hand Rosie and every other woman in this world work on a 28 day hormone cycle... yeah pretty crazy which now explains why I wake up feeling the exact same every day whilst Rosie wakes up in a different mood every day stage in her cycle every week. I now understand as the loving and supportive partner will do different things to help her through each stage of her cycle. So this is a new series I'd like to call Understanding Hormones with Harry... and every week as a male to help and support Rosie through each stage of her cycle with the hopes of not only out there like myself to understand their partners more but with Rosie's help speak out about how important it is for other understand this about themselves. I hope you guys have enjoyed Understanding Hormones with Harry.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I always had the biggest dream of going at the biggest Division possible. Division two was really fun and I enjoyed it, but if I could get a chance to redo it again like my 14 year old self... The first thing is lock in on my nutrition and hydration. I was leaving so many gains and so much potential on the table doing that. Second thing I would do is put my phone away when it came to sleep. I'd leave it so it wouldn't even tempt me so I could get to recover, to recuperate the next morning and show up to training super high energy ready to go. I would listen to the sports podcasts or reading. That's one big hole in my development I believe was my mindset. I would focus on that. The fourth thing is go find a gym full of athletes who were better than you, bigger, more talented than you. When you walk in you feel embarrassed for being there. You're going to rise to the level of their competition. You want to be the worst one in the gym and better people will help you rise out of that. If I did those four things I really believe the process would have been a whole lot different.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Hey everyone, I'm Neil. I'm 22 years old, and I'm starting a coffee shop from scratch. I'm not worried at all though because I have 3 World Barista Championships and several years of experience as a roaster. I've also started multiple food & beverage businesses before, I have close to $1M in savings, and investors are lining up in numbers to throw money at this idea. Just kidding. I have none of those things. What I do have is a $100 espresso machine, a couple of books on coffee, like $211 in cash, a mirrorless camera, and a bunch of loving and supportive friends. So, I'm literally going to be building this coffee shop from the ground up. It's going to be a wild journey and I hope you can learn something from it. So stay tuned!

videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y cinematográfico, con tonos ligeramente desaturados en los momentos tristes.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Desesperación -> Resignación -> Frustración -> Claridad -> Esperanza -> Empoderamiento.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: Inspirar a otros a iniciar su propio proceso de cambio.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I didn't realize how bad I lost myself. I was showing up, doing everything right, checking the boxes, being disciplined, but I wasn't present. I was living in autopilot, comfortable but completely unaligned. I allowed people and patterns into my life that didn't reflect the woman I wanted to become. I kept peace at the cost of my own, and the worst part? I didn't even notice it was happening. Then my body started to speak. I was tired, dealing with acne, bloated, burnt out. No matter what I did, I felt like my body was fighting me. But it hit me: I can't grind my way into change. I can't restrict or push my way into alignment. Because the truth was, I was still holding onto a version of me who wanted to stay stuck. Everything shifted the moment I stopped performing and started listening. And for the first time in a long time, I chose me. I burned the habits, the stories, the people pleasing, and piece by piece, I came home to myself. And I became a woman I'm proud of, not because she's perfect, but because she's real. So if you're in a place where you've lost sight of who you are, just know that you don't have to stay there. You're not broken, you're not behind, you're just waiting to remember who you are. And when you're ready, you can burn what's no longer yours and finally become her.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I just left my $300,000 a year new grad job and now I'm just a disappointment. I'm 23 years old and I'm challenging myself to make $50k a month in just 75 days. Why $50k a month? It's cause my girlfriend works for a quant shop and I mean, call me superficial, but I feel the duty to make more money than her. So 300 days ago I left school early to join the startup of my dreams. We were working 12 to 14 hours a day, 7 days a week and honestly it was so much fun. I just enjoyed working on a product I cared about with a team of hyper-cracked engineers. But there was this constant voice in my head saying: Charles, why are you working so hard for someone else? So two weeks ago I left my job, I took a bet on myself. I have no salary, I have no product, I have no safety net. I've never even made an iPhone app before. Dude, you're so cooked. But if it wasn't hard, I wouldn't be doing it in the first place. So for the next 75 days I'm going to be documenting my journey. If you want to see me succeed, follow along.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I think I got scammed. Saw local vendor selling honey like this and got 500 grams pure honey, or at least that's what I thought. I'm gonna test it. This is our said suspect. Take a glass of water, add honey. If this honey dissolves, I was scammed. Pure honey clumps at the bottom like this. Put a small drop on your thumb and see if it stays or it spreads. Stayed. Burn a spoonful of honey over an open flame. It should caramelise, not get frothy. Put a small amount of honey on a paper towel. If it blots wet, it's impure. Make a tissue paper wick and dip in honey. If the wick burns, you have pure honey. Take a metal plate, add a blob of honey and swirl clockwise. It formed hexagonal honeycomb texture, which means this is pure honey.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My kids wanted a salad for dinner so I said thanks for the suggestion but that's not my vibe and made them steak frites instead. All this is is another simple dish that the French try and take credit for. The interesting thing about steak frites is it feels fancy but it also feels like you could eat it at a bowling alley, which is kind of confusing but I'm into it. This one time when I was a kid, the guy put a cigarette out in my Coke can at a bowling alley and I didn't realize it until I chugged it. Kids these days will never know that pain. In other bowling news, I used to eat cheese fries with a fork so I could bowl with my right hand without it getting greasy. School may not have taught me much, but that's because I solve real world problems. You can serve this with whatever sauce you want, maybe ketchup if you're a toddler, but I went with an au poivre. You probably don't even know what that is, it's like a creamy pepper sauce. I was really happy with how this came out. I presalted the ribeye and let it sit at room temp for almost 2 hours, which improves the flavor and allows it to cook evenly. Now I just need to figure out what to feed my wife because she doesn't eat red meat. Well, never mind, that's too far, I'll microwave her something. Follow for more marriage advice.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con humor y curiosidad, pasa por la incredulidad ante la obsesión del esposo, y termina en un tono dulce de apoyo mutuo y búsqueda de validación de la comunidad.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: ¿Vale la pena entrar en One Piece y dedicarle tanto tiempo? (Pregunta a la audiencia)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: One fun fact about my husband David is that he is deeply dedicated to One Piece. Like this man has been reading this since he was a small child and he has not missed a single week. And a few years ago when we were planning our trip to Japan he told me that we should avoid going during Golden Week, which I was shocked about because he is not type A and he's never the one that plans anything. And I was like how do you even know about Golden Week? It's because apparently One Piece takes a break during that week every year and he was traumatized ever since he was a child because he'd have to wait an extra week. All this time he's been asking me to read One Piece and I was like no, I'm not a manga girl. But today I decided I'm just gonna be a supportive wife and I picked this up. I'm gonna see what the hype is all about and I'm only committing to the first three volumes because I'm not that nice. There's like 150 apparently, I just googled it. So he can just read it with our future kids, I'm not committing to all that. But I will read the first three and maybe I'll get addicted, who knows? Honestly I was just really happy because I asked him to read Will of the Many and he finally caved and did it and now he's obsessed, so you're welcome David. But I wanted to pay it back and read something that he likes. If you've also read One Piece, will you please let me know if it's worth getting into and actually dedicating the rest of my life to because apparently there's a lot to catch up on and this guy is not slowing down. Okay bye!

I spent an entire time working but was it worth it? I've been getting so much support on the last video. I can get $500 from one video alone because of the rewards program. videos_analizados.hook_template: I spent an entire (insert time) (insert verb) but was it a waste of time? I am about to go on the biggest bender of my entire
videos_analizados.escenario: Jardín exterior con huertos elevados y árboles de fondo
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I spent an entire year growing garlic but was it a waste of time?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Luz de día suave (parece atardecer o día nublado)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Sesgo de negatividad y curiosidad por el resultado a largo plazo
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de proceso largo condensado (Time-lapse narrativo)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, otoñal, saturación natural
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con duda, pasa por la calma del proceso educativo y termina con una satisfacción gratificante y un sentido de comunidad
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Let me know if you want my full garlic growing guide.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I spent an entire year growing garlic but was it a waste of time? Garlic is one of the easiest foods you can grow but it takes about nine months before you can harvest it and once you've seen the whole life cycle, I think you're going to want to grow it too. So, let's get started. In late fall, we take our seed garlic and plant it with plenty of room to grow and a little fertilizer. It's important to plant as late as possible so it doesn't sprout before winter. Cover it with a blanket of mulch and wait. Once spring comes around, you'll see your garlic sprout and if you're growing hard-neck garlic, it will start to produce a flower called scapes. You will pick these scapes as soon as they get curly so the plant can focus its energy on making bigger, spicier garlic underground. About four weeks after you pick your scapes, it's time to harvest your garlic when the bottom half of the leaves starts to turn brown. Before you brush off the dirt though, cure it in a cool dry place for a few weeks and then you can trim the stalks and clean them up. By now, it's almost time to start the whole process all over again so you can replant the biggest cloves from last year's garlic in the ground. I'm a garlic girl so for me, it's not a waste of time for the best tasting cloves for my cooking. Let me know if you want my full garlic growing guide.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: I am about to go on the biggest bender of my entire life
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy bien aprovechada en exteriores e interiores con luz de ventana.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido, aspecto vibrante y veraniego.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la sorpresa inicial a la inspiración por el desafío autoimpuesto.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito (seguir la serie para ver el resultado).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I am about to go on the biggest bender of my entire life. My name is Anthony and I graduate college in 18 days. My brand is built upon hard work, discipline, waking up early and chasing after your goals. But lately I've been wondering: what if I'm missing out? And this is what everybody's been telling me. So I decided to run an experiment. 18 days and 18 nights out. And I'll be measuring three things in these 18 days. Does going out really make you happy? Is it possible to keep a good physique? And can you stay productive? All while staying disciplined and working as hard as I can during the day. Some say college is about the memories, others say it's about the foundation. And I'm about to find out which one's right. So welcome to the last bender.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos grandes en el centro, cambian de color para resaltar palabras clave como 'BIGGEST BENDER', 'HARD WORK', 'DISCIPLINE'.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I think I'm getting fired today. I was feeling so anxious I thought I was going to throw up so I started stress baking madeleines. Then I gathered my family to witness my unemployment. 'So we made the decision to cut head count at [company] by about 15%. If you are impacted you will receive a calendar invite.' I refreshed my email non-stop for 15 minutes before getting this message from my manager. 'I wish they fired you.' Madeleines have never tasted so great but today is not the day I become a full-time food creator. But if I get to 100k followers maybe I'll quit.

videos_analizados.escenario: Casa suburbana en Ohio (exterior, cocina, dormitorios)
videos_analizados.hook_texto: i had always wondered what it was like to flip a house
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - muy luminosa en exteriores e interiores remodelados
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Planteamiento de un deseo aspiracional seguido de una acción impulsiva
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso de transformación (Antes/Después) con resultados financieros.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos (jump cuts) sincronizados con el ritmo del habla
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y brillante, transmitiendo limpieza y éxito
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con curiosidad, pasa por la ansiedad del riesgo financiero y termina en el triunfo del éxito comercial.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Like and follow to stay tuned.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I had always wondered what it was like to flip a house. So on an impulse I made an offer on a home that I didn't think they'd accept. But they did. So in the last 30 days I've been designing and flipping my first house using polls on my Instagram story. We completely transformed this house, documented each day and became the most viewed home in Ohio. But there's more to this than just a series. There's real money at stake here. And while I felt good about the numbers, there's no guarantee that I walk out of this with my 10k profit goal. I could even lose money if it doesn't sell. So as listing day crept closer and closer, I couldn't shake that imposter syndrome. Does this house really have what it takes to stand out against the competition? And then, here's what happened. Listing day 7:37 am. Is this real? Do we really have this many showings on day one? At the house 10:02 am. Oh my god. I can't even park here in front of the house because there's so many cars in the driveway. And these people have nicer cars than me. Look at that Audi, a Tesla back there. Turns out it was real. Listing day was a hit. People loved the home. My mailbox is going crazy. By 5 o'clock we already had three offers. By the next day, seven more offers. And after just two days on the market we accepted an offer above our list price. Is it possible to make a 10k profit flipping a house in just 30 days? I'll know soon. Right now we are midway through inspections and my primary goal is to make sure the new buyers receive a great house. We'll count the loot after things are final. Like and follow to stay tuned.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: El éxito masivo e inesperado (las 26 visitas el primer día) genera un efecto de 'wow' que la gente quiere mostrar.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía y el optimismo sin opacar la voz
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el morbo del dinero real, el formato de 'reto contra el reloj' y la validación de la audiencia (ellos eligieron el diseño).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos palabra por palabra, etiquetas de 'Episode 15', 'listing day', 'real feedback'.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 30 días de grabación + 5-8 horas de edición final
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Emprendedores, entusiastas de los bienes raíces, jóvenes interesados en inversiones.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: So about three months ago, me and my wife had a little bit too much fun. Well, at least I did. Yep, this is where I'm going to make your grandchild, dad. Right here. Is this your room? And a few weeks later, this happened. No. Are you serious? Are you dead serious? Are you dead serious? Are you actually serious? First try? Are you serious? Dang. Whoa. **** I'm going to cry. Oh my gosh. I got swimmers. Oh my gosh. I feel like I'm actually gonna pass out. That's freaking awesome. But I'm, like, scared at the same time... And yes, I did dap up my wife after finding out she was pregnant. But I want to make a bucket list before I become a father. So if you guys have any ideas, let me know.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en exteriores y artificial brillante en tiendas/interiores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste de estatus (estudiante vs profesor) y prueba social (fajos de billetes)

videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y brillante para resaltar los empaques de los snacks.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con euforia (dinero), pasa por la nostalgia (graduación), el esfuerzo (mover la máquina) y termina en satisfacción (negocio funcionando).
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en voz, pero implícito en el estilo de 'sígueme para ver el proceso'.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I made more money than my high school teachers selling snacks and today I am going to be investing that money into a vending machine. I've been selling snacks since middle school but I just graduated high school so my business was going to end. But I decided to take it to the next level. I went on Facebook Marketplace to cop a vending machine for the low. I ended up finding this one for 500 bucks. It was a little dirty but it worked. We picked it up and took it to the building where I'm going to be placing it. Honestly moving it was the hardest part. I went to Sam's Club to get all the snacks I needed. I got a variety of chips, candy and drinks. I dropped about 200 bucks on inventory. Then I opened up the machine and started filling it up. Now all I have to do is relax and watch the sales come in.

videos_analizados.hook_template: (Insert person), (Insert person), or that one (Insert person) we just don’t have (insert title) like we did back then. Day # of trying to (insert action) all year.
videos_analizados.escenario: Habitación de adolescente (cercano) y set de video musical (profesional).
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Justin Bieber, One Direction, or that kid who yodeled that one time in Walmart. We just don't have popstars like we did back then.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural de habitación combinada con iluminación profesional de estudio en el clip musical.
equipamiento: Video musical profesional (para la parte final)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de figuras icónicas y un sentimiento de pérdida (nostalgia) para generar acuerdo inmediato.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: El 'Underdog' con talento que promete revivir una era dorada.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos y zooms digitales rápidos.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural en la habitación, saturado y estilizado en el video musical.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Nostalgia -> Acuerdo -> Diversión -> Curiosidad -> Impresión (por la calidad de la música) -> Deseo de apoyo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow me from the very beginning because you never know, I could be next.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Justin Bieber, One Direction, or that kid who yodeled that one time in Walmart. We just don't have popstars like we did back then. Don't get me wrong, we have some amazing singers, great rappers, some better than others... and Taylor Swift is still somehow doing her thing. (Sings Taylor Swift) Sorry, where was I? Taylor Swift and um, popstars. Right. We have all these amazing artists but the popstar era has just disappeared. Well, not for long. My name is Tyler Bragoli and I'm bringing back popstars. And because you got this far in the video, here's a little sneak peek of what you're getting yourself into. (Music snippet plays) This is your chance to say that you were following me from the very beginning, because you never know, I could be next.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La calidad de la canción presentada al final y la identificación con la 'falta de popstars'.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar el discurso y luego demostrar el talento del artista.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un 'hook' de comparación nostálgica muy fuerte y luego rompe la expectativa con un clip de música de alta calidad que valida su discurso.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Nombres de artistas y transcripción del discurso con estilo dinámico.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 10-15 horas (sin contar la producción de la canción)
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Generación Z y Millennials jóvenes amantes del pop.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Tyler Bragoli: El regreso de la era Popstar
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Clips rápidos de Justin Bieber, One Direction y el niño del yodel seguidos por el creador hablando a cámara con un micrófono de juguete (espátula/cepillo).

videos_analizados.hook_template: Day # of trying to (insert action) all year. This was the (insert noun) that changed my life forever.
videos_analizados.escenario: Estudio con parafernalia de juegos/coleccionables y un acantilado frente al mar.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: DAY 365
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Artificial controlada en el estudio y luz natural de 'hora dorada' en exteriores.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un hito temporal masivo (un año completo) para generar autoridad y curiosidad sobre el resultado final.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Cierre de reto (The Grand Finale)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos y transiciones de zoom suaves.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y cinematográfico, especialmente en las tomas de exteriores.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con anticipación, pasa por la nostalgia de un año de trabajo y termina con una resolución épica y satisfactoria.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito, pero el cierre visual invita a ver el resultado final en el perfil.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Day 365 trying to take a photo all year. The final lens I'm using on camera is a vintage 50mm on my Canon R6. To clarify, the last photo of 2024 was so special. One last photo and I'm very excited for tomorrow. The last time using a lens I've been using throughout the series. I wanted it to be special. This photo was, I guess, inspired by my most reposted photo where I got engaged. A fitting location to encapsulate this journey coming to the end. But since it's the final photo, I wanted a commemorative photo of the year. Today's photo is symbolic of the hard work I've put in. With that, my hard work for the day.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La belleza estética de la toma final y la admiración por la constancia del creador.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un tono nostálgico pero inspirador.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'desafío completado' que genera una gran retención. La conexión emocional con su compromiso personal y la calidad estética del resultado final (el caballero al atardecer) son factores clave.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra que resaltan conceptos clave.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Fotógrafos, creadores de contenido y personas interesadas en retos de disciplina.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Día 365: El cierre de un reto de fotografía de un año
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador hablando directamente a cámara con un texto grande que dice 'DAY 365'.

videos_analizados.hook_template: This was the (insert noun) that changed my life forever. Over # of you saw this video.
videos_analizados.escenario: Muelle exterior, estudio de grabación y habitación con batería.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: This sound changed my life forever.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Natural en exteriores, artificial controlada en el estudio.
equipamiento: Cámara o Smartphone de alta gama
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una declaración de transformación personal combinada con un elemento visual curioso (micrófono improvisado).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Historia de origen / Evolución artística
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con tonos ligeramente fríos en exteriores y cálidos en interiores.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con intriga, pasa por la confesión de un prejuicio, llega a la inspiración y termina en una nota de humildad y ambición.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Drop me a follow and we'll see where this music thing can take me.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This sound changed my life forever. Before I heard that, I was never a fan of rap. Actually I kind of disliked it. But, in the summer of 2021, one of my friends introduced me to Tyler, The Creator. Before that, I purely made instrumental metal music and I had no intention of ever doing vocals. But discovering the way that Tyler structured his albums, told stories, and produced everything himself completely changed my approach to music. It was because of him that I tried fusing genres and experimenting with storytelling. He was such a big inspiration to me that some of my first songs are just blatant rip-offs of his, but it was through doing that that I found my own style. And although I'm nowhere close to being the artist that he is, I've dedicated my life to get there. If you want to see my journey, drop me a follow and we'll see where this music thing can take me.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad para otros músicos o fans que han tenido cambios de gusto musical drásticos.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Reforzar la estética del artista mencionado y mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un hook de 'transformación de vida' muy potente, apoya la narrativa con visuales de alta calidad y apela a una comunidad muy grande (fans de Tyler, The Creator).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave resaltadas en blanco que siguen el ritmo de la narración.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Músicos, productores, fans del rap alternativo y jóvenes creativos.
nichos_compatibles: Fitness (cómo un atleta cambió su vida)
Negocios (cómo un mentor cambió su enfoque)
Arte (cómo un pintor cambió su estilo)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Cómo Tyler, The Creator cambió mi vida y mi música
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano del creador sosteniendo un micrófono con base de madera frente a un paisaje urbano con agua.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Over # of you saw this video. Today I woke up and realized (insert realization).
videos_analizados.escenario: Interior de un coche
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Over 88 million of you saw this video.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural (luz de día dentro de un vehículo)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una métrica de éxito masiva para validar la autoridad y generar intriga sobre el contexto real del video viral.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: La historia detrás del éxito (The story behind the viral moment)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con contraste ligeramente alto
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con asombro por el éxito, pasa por la crudeza de la lucha personal y termina en una profunda empatía y admiración.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay CTA explícito, busca generar comunidad y engagement a través de la empatía.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Over 88 million of you saw this video. Today I woke up and realized that most of you think I'm a father... I'm not. I grew up poor in Oklahoma, parents were addicts. I spent years as a first responder, dealing with PTSD, depression, and suicidal ideation. I suffer from body dysmorphia. But the reason I do this is my brother Wesley. He's disabled, and I'm his caretaker. I'm here at Aldi getting things for him because he's my world.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad con las luchas de salud mental y el respeto por los cuidadores de personas con discapacidad.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acentuar la carga emocional de la confesión personal.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Contraste extremo entre la apariencia de 'hombre fuerte' (músculos, tatuajes, bigote) y la vulnerabilidad emocional absoluta. Toca temas universales como la salud mental y el sacrificio familiar.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos coloridos que resaltan palabras clave en amarillo y azul.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Hombres interesados en fitness, personas con familiares discapacitados, personas que sufren de ansiedad/depresión.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Vulnerabilidad y Storytelling: La realidad tras los 88M de vistas
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: El creador hablando a cámara en primer plano con un overlay (PiP) de su video viral anterior.

This but that for supported watching more.

videos_analizados.hook_template: I am (insert verb) a (insert noun), on my (insert noun). Remember that time you were (insert adjective) so you (insert action) but the moment (insert person)
videos_analizados.escenario: Un baño pequeño en proceso de renovación.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I'm painting a sea wall on my wall because I want it to look like the ocean.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural de ventana y luz artificial de baño.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Presentación de un proyecto ambicioso y visualmente atractivo desde el segundo cero.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso de transformación con cliffhanger.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado para resaltar los tonos azules.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Entusiasmo inicial -> Esfuerzo -> Duda/Fracaso aparente -> Recuperación -> Anticipación.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Come back tomorrow to see me do the rest.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm painting a sea wall on my wall because I wanted it to look like the ocean. And after yesterday's prepping, it was finally time to paint waves, which is my favorite part because you'd be surprised on how much of a difference paint can do. Like this color, it's not black, it's actually a really dark blue. I want this ceiling to look like the bottom of the ocean. Oh shoot, I'm glad we covered the sink because I am the messiest painter. Luckily we were almost done with our first coat. Okay, I know it looks really bad, but I promise y'all it is gonna get better. I'm letting the first coat dry and we're gonna immediately put on the second coat, just because we were so messy with it. And this surprisingly took half the time. Whoo, I almost fell backwards there. And now that the ceiling is finished, bag up your brush cause we're gonna need it later. Because it's time to blend with a lighter blue. I want the colors to look seamless, so I'm blending with a fat brush, moving in circles to make it look like waves. So come back tomorrow to see me do the rest.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Inspiración para proyectos DIY y técnica de pintura inusual.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y hacer el proceso ameno.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato 'Trust the process'. Muestra un momento de fallo estético ('it looks really bad') que genera tensión y curiosidad por ver cómo se arregla.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Palabras clave grandes que siguen la narración (painting, abyss, deep sea, etc.).
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en decoración, DIY, arte y renovación de hogares.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Transformación DIY: Mural de Océano en el Techo
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La creadora sostiene dos botes de pintura frente a la cámara en un baño pequeño, con texto superpuesto que dice 'painting'.
