videos_analizados.hook_texto: Is it possible to quit gooning and reconnect with God in 3 days even if you’re in college and all your friends think getting freaky is healthy?
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Uso intensivo de luces LED RGB (rojo, verde, azul, púrpura) para separar ambientes y estados de ánimo.
equipamiento: Cámara o Smartphone de alta gama
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un gancho de 'falsa promesa' seguido de una realidad honesta ('I dunno, cause it took me 3 years')
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Confesión vulnerable + Reencuadre de creencia limitante.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos muy rápidos (jump cuts) y transiciones de movimiento.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Estilizado, con alta saturación y contrastes marcados por las luces de colores.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con curiosidad/tensión, pasa por la vergüenza y la lucha interna, y termina en alivio y aceptación espiritual.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, el video termina con una reflexión final para generar engagement emocional.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Is it possible to quit gooning and reconnect with God in 3 days even if you’re in college and all your friends think getting freaky is healthy? I dunno, cause it took me 3 years. So even though I'm a Christian 'influencer', ugh, my degeneracy filled for you page had me in a chokehold bro. And it's hard not to when society tells us 'erm, actually, gooning's literally good for you'. WHAT? But every time I fumbled, I felt so dirty to the point where I asked myself: do I even DESERVE God? So like any responsible college kid would, I definitely did not take it to ChatGPT... instead I grabbed my Bible and- wait a minute. 'But God (John 3:17) did not send his son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him'??? So you're telling me I'm not a dirty rotten sinner? No, you still are. Oh. But Jesus knew about all the addictions, the relapses, the fake repentance, and still said: 'Yeah, I want that one.' Cause he didn't do it for the 'perfect version' of you, he did it for the real one. Heh, litewally.
videos_analizados.costo_produccion: Bajo (si ya se tiene el equipo)
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Juvenil, usando jerga de internet (gooning, fyp, chokehold) mezclada con lenguaje religioso.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Identificación profunda con la lucha interna y el deseo de redención sin juicio.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo del montaje sin distraer del mensaje serio.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un tema tabú (adicción al porno/masturbación) con un lenguaje extremadamente actual y honesto, eliminando el estigma religioso tradicional.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave en colores (amarillo, verde, rojo).

videos_analizados.guion_completo: What do you do? I hate this question. I'm a filmmaker. Anything I would have seen? No. How many countries have you been to? Didn't know it was a contest. I'm a surfer. Do you surf huge waves? No. I'm a hiker. Are you going to climb Everest? No. I was a software engineer. Make an app? No. I studied as an electrical engineer. I'm a content creator. How many followers? Never enough. I've changed directions multiple times in my life. This means I fail far more than I win. But I get up and I try again. Like a motivational influencer. Are you on TikTok? Like and follow for more.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Last year I bought this land on the water for $30 million. Let's make 150 units. 2,000 sq ft each. 300,000 total sellable sq ft. $3,000,000 per unit x 150 units = $450,000,000 total sellout. The costs: The land $30M. Hard costs $600 per sq ft = $180M. Soft costs $90M. Financing $45M. Total $345M. $450,000,000 income minus $345,000,000 costs equals $105,000,000 total profit.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Artificial Mixta - Muy clara y brillante
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Visualmente satisfactorio (ASMR visual) combinado con una meta cuantitativa alta.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Tutorial de proceso con revelación de secreto industrial.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Colores saturados, énfasis en los tonos rosados y púrpuras.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Expectativa -> Sorpresa (por la técnica del vapor) -> Relajación/Fascinación (marmoleado) -> Admiración final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Mención indirecta (Amazon storefront) y despedida (Bye friends).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Let's make 100 funfetti cake pops. Okay, first we're gonna start off with a big ass vanilla cake straight from the oven into the mixer per usual. Everybody is always asking where's the icing, where's the frosting? We do not add any of that here. Look at the steam, look at how beautiful. The steam is just gonna bind everything together and you're gonna get that perfect cake pop dough every time, I promise. Then you're gonna let it cool, you're gonna add your sprinkles and you're gonna get it into your cakesicle mold. These are listed on my Amazon storefront. Then you're gonna insert your popsicle stick and then pop those right out of there. I'm showing you my trick, so get after it. Then you get your chocolate, pour it into your mug. I colored mine pink for a Valentine's Day type theme. And then we're gonna start our marble process. So grab your colors, I'm always gonna be using Colour Mill. Add as much as you want. This leaves no taste, that's the best part about Colour Mill, there is no taste at all. So get to dipping. I swirl it around with my knife, dip your pop, so beautiful. This is like my one of my favorite dips I've ever done. You're gonna see them in one second. Swirl it around, dip it up, so pretty. The sun was shining right on those. Oh, smooth as glass. And there is the finished product. Bye friends.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La técnica innovadora de usar el vapor del bizcocho caliente.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía y el ritmo de los cortes.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un 'hack' de cocina (no usar frosting) que genera debate y curiosidad, junto con una estética visual de marmoleado muy satisfactoria.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: No visible
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Reposteros caseros, entusiastas del DIY y amantes del contenido de comida visual.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Proceso de creación de 100 cake pops marmoleados sin frosting
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano de un cake pop siendo sumergido en chocolate con un patrón de marmoleado rosa y morado vibrante.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I had just three days to bake and decorate 250 sugar cookies for a mystery tiktoker. And you have until the end of this video to figure out who they're for. I 3D printed these custom cookie cutters... Sat down to write out my schedule... lots of butter... each batch was a double batch... spoiler alert: it was not enough. I put down the saran wrap... started with the knife cookie cutters... they're gonna be little chefs knives... I started on the second shape... I took a quick break to eat some Cava... I really needed to reset my kitchen... I put on Notting Hill... I finished the dough... here's your fourth hint: these are little hands... a very iconic thing for the girl I'm making these for... I had some more coffee... building my drying rack... I made that royal icing... denim blue to match an apron... fifth hint: the denim blue is supposed to match an apron... she always has super iconic nails... got some green sprinkles to match the razzle dazzle that she does... I finished at 2 in the morning and I had a flight at 4:30... come back to the next video for the big reveal.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y resaltan las pistas.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Have you ever seen 1300 cakes in one place? Well now you have. Welcome to the San Francisco Cake Picnic, where the only rule is: No cake, no entry. Each attendee brought their own masterpiece. Just look at this creativity! When you sign up, you get a wristband and a cake box to fill up. Then it's go time! We have 6 minutes to grab as many slices as we can. My friend Rachel and I went all out, and just look at this haul! After the race, we feasted. Cheers to cake and community! And until next time, thanks for coming along.

videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina moderna, blanca y organizada.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I have a confession. I have no problem cooking for my family of six but it’s the process of actually meal planning that feels so overwhelming.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy luminosa y clara, típica de nichos de hogar/limpieza.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Vulnerabilidad inmediata para conectar con un punto de dolor común (la carga mental).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Problema-Solución con un producto estético.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, brillante, alta exposición.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la frustración y el agobio por el desorden a la calma y satisfacción de tener un sistema organizado.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en el video (probablemente en el caption).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I have a confession. I have no problem cooking for my family of six but it’s the process of actually meal planning that feels so overwhelming and I realized something. It was because my favorite recipes are scattered all over the place, cookbooks, recipe cards, and don't even get me started on my disaster of a Pinterest board and the 50 million pop-up ads on every recipe. So because my family needs to eat, I came up with a simple solution. In 2023, I made a meal planning binder AKA a meal planning system and a cookbook all in one and let me just tell you how much easier it is to plan out our meals. Let me break it down for you. I choose our meals from the recipe index. Write them down on our dinner menu. Then I create a grocery shopping list right from the binder because I have printed off or scanned every recipe that I use. And then I place a grocery pickup order. I am shocked that such a simple solution could make meal planning so much easier.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Conversacional y basado en la resolución de problemas.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad: la gente guarda y comparte sistemas que prometen ahorrar tiempo y reducir el estrés.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente relajado y hogareño.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Ataca la 'fatiga de decisión' que sufren los padres. El formato de 'confesión' genera empatía inmediata y la solución es visualmente estética (satisfacción visual).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos estáticos con tipografía elegante (Serif) para secciones y el título principal.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Madres/Padres, personas encargadas del hogar, entusiastas de la organización.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Sistema de Organización de Comidas para Familias Numerosas
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Mujer en una cocina limpia sosteniendo papeles, con texto superpuesto: 'SIMPLIFYING MEAL PLANNING for my family of 6'.

videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido, evocando una estética de 'verano eterno' y nostalgia.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Ansiedad/Confusión inicial -> Autocrítica humorística -> Inspiración/Empoderamiento final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: So join me on a journey... to follow me there because you are not gonna be in your 20s forever.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm 22 and an identity crisis because I'm terrified of missing out and I'm sure too it's called the 20s crisis episode one: 20s crisis now there's many types number one like is this degree for me number two like is this the person I'm supposed to be with why am I still single? number three the financial crisis instagram while i'm still debating whether to get spotify premium I feel like I'm living 8 different lives university who romanticises uni academic weapon dubai and next I'm a brat drowning herself at an edm party on an island beachin surrounded by strangers by the sea on a who will then randomly start preaching about the importance of 'your divine self' with wondering who the f*ck is that all because I change my personality every single day last year so many things Because I kept saying or im too inexperienced or I don't wanna go there and this year i stopped waiting and my life did a 360 I don't really know and I'm sure you don't either but I do know 1. Side quests 2. Shape shifting 3. Solo travelling I will enter my 30s with pure regret so join me on a journey of doing absolutely anything regardless of bank account is in the negatives to follow me there because you are not gonna be in your 20s forever.

videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y títulos de capítulos (Episode one, Number one, etc).

videos_analizados.hook_template: I have been a (insert title) for # years of my life I started when I was # years old working at (insert location/place) in (insert location). When I was growing up one (insert noun) I always
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina profesional minimalista y limpia.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: I've been a chef for 16 years of my life. I started when I was 17 years old
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial controlada - Estilo estudio, suave y enfocada en la comida.
equipamiento: Micrófono de alta sensibilidad para ASMR
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de autoridad inmediata mediante años de experiencia y edad de inicio.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de trayectoria profesional (Timeline de carrera).
videos_analizados.color_grading: Tonos cálidos, saturación natural, enfoque en rojos del pescado y blancos del arroz.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con una declaración de orgullo, pasa por la nostalgia de los inicios, la incertidumbre del cambio de carrera y termina en una sensación de plenitud y éxito personal.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, funciona como construcción de marca personal.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I've been a chef for 16 years of my life. I started when I was 17 years old working at my uncle's restaurant on the Upper West Side in New York City. I've always looked up to my uncle and wanted to always emulate him and his success, so I started working at his restaurant. During my sophomore year of college, I went to Italy to do a study abroad program to learn Italian. But while I was there, I fell in love with the culture and the food. It made me realize that I wanted to get into the restaurant industry. So when I came back to New York City, I dropped out of college and I went straight into restaurants. One of the very first places that I worked at was called Neta. It was opened by two chefs that came from Masa at the Time Warner Center. They later opened up a restaurant called Shuko, which I ended up following them to. Then the documentary 'Jiro Dreams of Sushi' came out. That documentary started the omakase craze in the United States. Because of that, I wanted to learn from the senior apprentice of Jiro. So I left Shuko and joined Sushi Nakazawa. After spending 8 years at Sushi Nakazawa, I decided it was time to leave and start my own business. I moved to Los Angeles 2 years ago and started working with private clients and traveling around the world doing what I love to do most: making food.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La estética impecable y la mención de hitos culturales como 'Jiro Dreams of Sushi'.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente sofisticado y relajante que no distraiga de la historia.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina una historia de superación personal con visuales de alta calidad (food porn) y sonidos ASMR, lo que retiene la atención tanto auditiva como visualmente.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Texto elegante con tipografía Serif que aparece palabra por palabra o en frases cortas sincronizadas con la voz.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Foodies, aspirantes a chefs, amantes de la cultura japonesa y personas interesadas en historias de emprendimiento.
nichos_compatibles: Cualquier oficio que requiera alta precisión manual
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Trayectoria y Evolución de un Chef de Sushi
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Primer plano detalle de las manos del chef manipulando una pieza de pescado con precisión sobre una tabla de madera.

videos_analizados.nicho: Juegos de mesa / Estilo de vida

videos_analizados.hook_texto: When I was growing up, one card game that I played all of the time with my mom was speed
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Mixta - Muy bien iluminado, ambiente hogareño y acogedor.
equipamiento: Trípode con brazo para tomas cenitales
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Storytelling personal combinado con la revelación de un objeto (mesa de juegos)

videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener la agilidad del tutorial.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado, resaltando los tonos de la madera y el verde del tapete.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con calma y nostalgia, transiciona a una fase de aprendizaje enfocado y termina con la energía rápida y competitiva del juego.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito en audio, pero el formato invita a guardar el video para referencia futura.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: When I was growing up, one card game that I played all of the time with my mom was speed. And if you've never played before or if you need a refresher, shuffle your favorite deck and I'll teach you right now. In speed, you'll go head to head competing to be the first player to get rid of all of your cards. To the center, deal out a stack of five cards followed by two single cards and then a second stack of five cards. The rest of the deck is dealt out evenly between you and your opponent. Draw a hand of five cards from your personal deck and then you're ready to play. Simultaneously, you and your opponent will flip over those two cards in the center. This begins the game. You both will play at the same time, discarding cards from your hand into these center piles. To discard a card, it must be one rank higher or lower than the top card of the pile you're playing onto. This means you can only discard a five on top of a four or a six. Now, the rank order loops so you can play an ace on a king or on a two. You can only ever play one card at a time though. You can't put down a run of cards at once. Your hand is limited to five cards but once you've played a card, you can draw new cards from the top deck to bring your hand back up to five. If during the game, you are ever locked out and neither you nor your opponent has a play, you'll each draw the top card off of your stack of five and place them into the center piles. If you still can't play, just do it again. If you ever run out of moves and you don't have any cards left in the stacks, just flip over the center piles and keep going. You'll do all of this as quickly as you can rushing to play all of your cards before your opponent. The first player to discard all of their cards, shout speed and wins. How are you already out?
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad (enseñar a otros a jugar) y estética del mobiliario.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ritmo constante y una atmósfera positiva.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina un tutorial útil de un juego clásico con un elemento visual aspiracional (la mesa de juegos). El ritmo es perfecto para el nombre del juego.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Título 'How to Play SPEED' con tipografía retro/serif en color amarillo.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en juegos de mesa, familias, y entusiastas de la decoración/organización.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Cómo jugar al juego de cartas SPEED
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: La protagonista se acerca a una mesa de centro de madera, abre un cajón oculto y saca una caja de cartas decorativa.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Luz de día suave
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de narrativa de cuento de hadas combinada con una transformación de bienes raíces (antes/después sugerido).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Transformación de espacio / Proceso artesanal
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mostrar progreso.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, saturado en verdes y marrones, estilo nostálgico.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con nostalgia y encanto, pasa por la satisfacción del trabajo duro y termina con inspiración y anticipación.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow along because this cottage garden is just getting started
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Once upon a time we bought a fairy tale fixed upper cottage on 7 overgrown acres. Building an English cottage garden. Not an all you can eat salad bar for Bambi's family and friends. We need a fence. Our property is basically a forest with a house on it and the trees are begging to be thinned. So let's live off the land. We cut some timber with our Norwood Sawmill. There's something so special about building a garden using wood that grew right here. Post went in and our homegrown lumber became a fence. For the fencing we went with budget friendly hog panels. Just trim the tops and bottoms, then drilled holes to tuck the wires neatly into place. Same idea for the top boards. Slide, hammer, secure. Deer: denied. Of course no dreamy garden is complete without a whimsical gate. I sketched out a little arch and cut it with a bandsaw. My husband wanted to try timber framing and it turned out ridiculously cute. I can already picture kids running through it, arms full of flowers and me with a cup of tea taking a Jane Austen style turn in the garden. Then we mapped out our garden beds. For the center I found the most charming fountain on Facebook Marketplace. I pick up next weekend and I'm irrationally excited. Right now the fence is giving a little more functional farm than Fairy Tale Cottage, but hold that thought. We're adding a trellis and staining everything to dial up the charm. See that big unfenced area? That is where I'm building the cutest greenhouse that'll attach the garden. Just wait! So follow along because this cottage garden is just getting started and there's a whole lot more magic on the way!
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Estética visualmente placentera y utilidad del tutorial de la cerca.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Reforzar la atmósfera de tranquilidad y vida rural.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el nicho de 'Cottagecore' con el de 'DIY/Construcción'. El valor de producción es alto y el concepto de usar madera de su propio bosque genera mucha curiosidad y respeto (sostenibilidad).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos descriptivos con tipografía serif clásica que refuerza el estilo 'cottagecore'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en jardinería, vida rural, DIY y decoración de exteriores.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Construcción de un Jardín de Cuento de Hadas (English Cottage Garden)
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Plano general de una casa de campo roja con un camino de ladrillos y flores, estilo acogedor.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural y artificial de cocina (brillante y clara)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Relatabilidad extrema basada en un tropo cultural (el 'man cold')
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Relatabilidad de pareja + Vlog de cocina
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, saturación ligeramente alta para resaltar la comida
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la frustración cómica inicial a la satisfacción de la 'cura' a través de la comida.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito en la compartibilidad del tropo)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Can you name something more terrifying than a man with a minor cold? Today my husband was feeling under the weather so I said of course I'll drop everything and take care of you. He slept until 10, didn't have much of an appetite, so I made tomato and egg drop soup. 30 minutes later he was ready for solid food so I got back to work. I made beef and string beans with fresh rice and chopped everything into tiny pieces so he doesn't waste energy chewing. For his afternoon snack, I made a yogurt bowl with fresh berries, granola, chia and hemp seeds because ChatGPT said it's good for immune functions. Finally for dinner, I had some leftover chicken from yesterday that I sautéed with veggies and repurposed into curry noodles. He ate everything while watching March Madness and is now completely cured.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Las mujeres lo comparten con sus parejas o amigas que viven situaciones similares.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente acogedor y doméstico

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Parejas, mujeres jóvenes, entusiastas de la cocina

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de fracaso (Loss Porn) con estética de alto valor.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes rápidos rítmicos con el audio.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y contrastado, estilo cinematográfico urbano.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Optimismo y ambición inicial, seguido de una tensión creciente por el riesgo financiero, terminando en caos y decepción.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Follow for episode two.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Six months ago my friends and I started a luxury car rental business. But it turned out to be the biggest financial 'L' we took last year. When you mix business and friends and money gets involved, people can switch up... This is Sidequests Gone Wrong Episode 1. So this is Eric, my close friend, business partner and real estate agent... a friend of ours was getting out of the car rental game moving back to Dubai and offered to sell us his entire fleet for a 'good deal'... about $830k worth of cars... that's where Sonny comes in... Marine veteran and owner of a phenomenal restaurant... a wizard when it comes to credit... what could possibly go wrong? Besides pretty much fucking everything. The clientele were not in our wheelhouse... cars coming back with bullet holes, cars going missing... that rapper who needed your car for a day is now five states over in Texas... zero profit was being made... follow for episode two.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: El valor de la lección aprendida sobre negocios con amigos y el shock de ver autos de lujo destruidos.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y dar una vibra de 'estilo de vida de lujo'.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el morbo del fracaso financiero masivo y la estética de 'dinero rápido' que atrae a audiencias de emprendimiento.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra y títulos de capítulos.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Emprendedores jóvenes, entusiastas de los autos, personas interesadas en finanzas.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Sidequests Gone Wrong: El fracaso de un negocio de renta de autos de lujo
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Cortes rápidos de autos de lujo en un estacionamiento nocturno y el narrador hablando a cámara.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito, pero el ranking invita a ver los videos anteriores.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm on a journey to find the best kids meal for adults and tonight we went to Dairy Queen where the kids meal starts at five dollars and nine cents. With the kids meal you get the option of chicken strips, hamburger, hot dog, a side, a drink and a treat. For the drink I thought you could only get milk and bottled water so I was pleasantly surprised when he presented the option of a kids fountain drink. Also the fact that he put root beer on the receipt and I didn't even tell him... King. Anyway the root beer was crispy and fresh. For my side I did choose fries which came in this teeny tiny bag. First I tried this arched one dipped in ranch. The ranch was okay, the fries were good. I don't know what it is about Dairy Queen fries but I like them. I also got honey mustard which was so sweet and tangy. It was decent for coming in a package like this. Next we dove into the entree which I chose chicken strips. These chicken strips don't look like they would be good but the breading was so crispy and crunchy. The chicken inside was hot and juicy. Honestly I was pleasantly surprised. Now there only is two so this kids meal is not gonna fill everybody up but personally I was satiated after that. Lastly is the treat which I chose a chocolate Dilly bar. Again I didn't even know this was an option. He mentioned there was a Heath one and I wish I chose that one cause I just need a little bit of crunch. All this for five dollars I think is a really good deal. Depending on how hungry you are this might be the kids meal for you. Dairy Queen you're coming in at sixth place at 8.85.

But as I was reading Matthew 5:29, 'If your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off.' I realized that like my 5k, I held poor form, bad habits, mental blocks. I realized the 'me' spiritually was broken. But a challenge arose. It says that we are shapen in iniquity, meaning born with sin. I was part of that something with hopeless. But I found and is surrender to God things.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural de ventanas y artificial del sitio
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Confesión impactante sobre una imagen visualmente abrumadora

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Del asco/shock inicial a la esperanza y finalmente a la inspiración/conmoción.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito, pero el cierre emocional fomenta el engagement y seguimiento.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I lied to the lady who lives here and this is the last video I'm gonna be posting of the free deep clean that we did in Leeds. After removing the mountains of rubbish, this is what we were working with. It may look better but it's still very dirty. The majority of this was actually kitty caca which may have been stinky but oh boy it was satisfying to clean. The reason I said I lied is I told Jane from the get-go that most of her furniture wouldn't be salvageable. Safe to say I ended up eating my words because a few hours of scrubbing later and most of it was looking brand new again. Sadly other areas of the bedroom weren't looking as promising, the first of them being the carpet which is gonna be ripped out and replaced. The second thing is the wall, even though I went hot and heavy with my Eraser Daddy, it will most likely need to be repainted. And the last thing we weren't able to save was Jane's bed and her mattress, sadly they were too heavily contaminated. So they got to go. As soon as I told Gus from Scrub Daddy, that man ran to his car and came back two hours later with an entire car boot full of stuff for Jane. I'm talking lamps, flowers, duvet, pillows, sheets, throws, a new mattress protector. Gus had even researched what type of mattress would be most comfortable for Jane with her mobility issues. Not only that, he also volunteered to build all of the new furniture himself. Then it was time for my favourite part of the clean which was doing the finishing touches and putting the bedroom back together again. This time last year I was living in a filthy flat that was infested with black mould. Now thanks to you guys I'm able to clean for free for people in need full time. So from the bottom of my heart, thank you.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con tensión/tristeza, pasa por una calma productiva (cocina), llega a un pico de angustia (inyección) y termina en satisfacción y orgullo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (el video busca engagement por empatía).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Something I never thought I'd have on my bingo card as a mom is having to inject my child with a huge needle once a month. So in order to combat both of our nerves, I've started a tradition of making her a little sweet treat ready for her to enjoy right after the shot. And I'm especially excited because girlfriend has been begging for mangoes all throughout the winter. And her wait is finally over because mangoes are finally in season. Today I decided to go with a healthier version of mango sticky rice by substituting the rice with chia seed. I like mixing up the texture with a little bit of mango puree and all that's left to do is to plate it pretty. I've come to brace the fact that some months the anxiety is just worse than others. Even if we've done it month in and month out for a very long time. And it's going to go away. But it usually starts to get better once we start praying with her. Then it's 10 seconds of the worst thing possible and a hug. Usually after that she forgets about the shot real fast. And I'm so proud of her.

videos_analizados.escenario: Apartamento en estado de acumulación extrema (síndrome de Diógenes).

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural de ventanas combinada con la luz cruda del interior del apartamento.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con shock, pasa por la preocupación logística y financiera, genera empatía por el residente y termina en una satisfacción visual extrema.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Drop me a follow and I'll show you in the next one.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I had to spend £5,000 on this free clean before the clean had even started. Now, I only clean for free so the resident doesn't pay anything, but that doesn't mean the clean costs nothing. And most of those costs need to be paid before the clean can even start. The person living here was so heavily in debt with their utility bill provider that the company had switched off all their hot water altogether. So the first cost I made was paying off all of the resident's debt, getting their hot water switched back on, and putting enough money in their utility account so they didn't have to worry about bills for the next year. Fortunately, the resident living here was able to stay with a family member, but that family member was allergic to cats and the resident has a cat. So next on my list was hiring someone to look after the cat for the 11 days that this clean took place. Now there was loads of other costs that had to be made before the clean even started, such as services, PPE, and gifting, and of course the big daddy of them all which is waste removal. I don't want to bore you, but there was a lot of general waste that could be put in skips, and skips are expensive and we needed a lot of them. I also needed to hire a professional biohazard cleaning team to dispose of all of the biohazard waste in the bathroom, and that also wasn't cheap. But I like to do everything legally and safely over here, so it was worth it. Every time you interact with my videos, you are directly funding the next free deep clean. You're the only reason that I'm able to afford to do this. I love you. Here's a sneak peek of the living room. If you want to see how I achieved these afters, drop me a follow and I'll show you in the next one.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: I just left my 9 to 5 corporate job to start a frozen bean and cheese burrito business.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en casa y fluorescente industrial en la cocina.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste radical entre estabilidad corporativa y un nicho de negocio inusual (burritos congelados).
videos_analizados.patron_viral: The Journey (El Viaje): Del punto A (empleado) al punto B (dueño de negocio exitoso).
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente cálido para que la comida se vea apetitosa.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con la incertidumbre/emoción del cambio, pasa por la validación del éxito temprano, muestra el agotamiento del trabajo duro y termina con inspiración y esperanza.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Stick around, we're just getting started (implica seguir la cuenta).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I just left my 9 to 5 corporate job to start a frozen bean and cheese burrito business. Going so far, I'm Hank. Months ago I bought a giant pot to cook, sell, and deliver 120 frozen burritos to my friends. I took orders, maybe broke some health codes, and had my family, my sanity... I sold out in three hours. I've been making bean and cheese burritos from scratch for over a decade. You really can't buy the real deal. My friends have been requesting them for years, so I decided to see if they'd sell, and sell they did. After my corporate job, I went legit. I set up shop in a commercial kitchen, got a food permit, hacked together a website and recruited my designer/entrepreneur wife Sam to the cause. Since then, monthly-ish sold out batches. Gearing up to drop 800 burritos this week. Increasing production, no joke. It takes 15 hours for the broth and beans. So between my job and kitchen time, I had to... and we're sourcing ingredients, cooking and wrapping burritos, packing and delivering orders with the help of some of our friends. We've got some big plans. We're Bad Hambres on a mission. Thanks for making our bean dream a reality. Stick around, we're just getting started.

videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el hook aspiracional de 'dejar el trabajo que odias' combinado con un proceso de fabricación satisfactorio (satisfying process) y un producto altamente relatable.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos en mayúsculas que resaltan palabras clave del relato.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: I feel like 10 years ago I had my life together as a mom but just slowly over time things have shifted.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial brillante (típica de cocina moderna)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un 'hook viral de referencia' basado en la nostalgia y la confesión personal para generar empatía inmediata con otras madres.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Productividad extrema / Hack de vida
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos rápidos sincronizados con la narración.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y brillante para resaltar la frescura de los alimentos.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con una sensación de pérdida de control (nostalgia), transita por el esfuerzo productivo y termina en una nota de triunfo y organización total.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Mención del sitio web para obtener las recetas y aviso de que habrá más contenido (grocery lists).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: I feel like 10 years ago I had my life together as a mom but just slowly over time things have shifted and I just got away from doing the things that made my life easier. So I'm bringing it all back and one of those things is making freezer meals like clockwork. Every two weeks I used to make freezer meals enough to last for the entire two weeks and keep them in the freezer so our family always had a home-cooked meal. And I just got away from doing that and I don't really know why. But this entire time I kept all of these recipes and they were all written down on paper. So I've converted them all digitally and I've actually put them on my website which I'll put in the caption. So far I've already converted 14 of the recipes over to digital format and they're already on my website. In two hours I was able to make 9 different freezer meals from five different recipes. I love doing doubles of some of the recipes especially the ones that my family really loves. There are so many amazing recipes all over Pinterest for freezer meals, these are just the recipes that I've had for the last like 15 years and I know that my family loves them so I shared them with you guys. I'm gonna be documenting everything and I'm gonna be adding things like a grocery list and a rotation schedule for making the freezer meals and which ones are best to make together. But for right now there's only the recipes but those things will be added soon so keep an eye out. With my kids involved in so many sports and activities after school I knew I had to do something to make our afternoons a little bit easier and I am so glad I had all these recipes on hand. I am gluten-free and a lot of these recipes are not originally meant to be gluten-free but I do share the changes that I made to make them gluten-free. And these are all the meals that I made in 2 hours.

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con nostalgia humilde, pasa por la presión del éxito corporativo 'atrapado', y termina en una euforia de libertad financiera y felicidad familiar.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: DM me 'START' and let's chat
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 2015: I met my wife when she worked a Domino's. I worked at Dunkin' Donuts. Combined Income: $3k/month. 2016: We wanted a higher income so I got an entry level job in sales. And she got a job as an autopsy technician. Combined Income: $6,300/mo. 2017: We moved states so I could get a job Google. Combined Income: $12k/month. 2018: Then moved again. Both took new sales jobs. Combined Income: $20k/mo. At 25y/o we bought our first home and achieved the 'American Dream'. But we quickly realized... We were trapped in our 'fancy jobs' to pay off the mortgage... And didn't want to spend 8-10h a day away from family for the next 40 years. So we set a 10 year goal to become financially free. 2019: We took a risk & put 15% down on this $495k Airbnb property. 2020: We liquidated our retirement accounts & invested all our savings to buy 2 more Airbnbs. Job Income: $25k/month. Airbnb Bookings: $11k/month. 2021: Bought our 4th property by refinancing our personal home and partnering with an investor. Job Income: $30k/month. Airbnb Bookings: $50k/month. April 2021: Quit our jobs and traveled the US in a campervan for 6 months. 2022: Refinanced 3 of our Airbnbs to buy 2 more in Florida. Airbnb bookings: $75k/month. 2023: Bought and renovated this 6,800 sq ft property in the NC Mountains. Airbnb bookings: $86k/month. 2024: Transformed an old vacation home in NC into this Airbnb mountain 'retreat'. Airbnb bookings: $114k/month. 2025: Launched our 9th Airbnb in South Carolina 3 months ago. Airbnb bookings goal: $2M+ this year. If we hadn't bought our first Airbnb in 2019, we'd still be trapped working 40-60h/week... Instead we get to be stay at home parents for our 2 beautiful children... Because our 9 Airbnbs only take <1h/day to manage. If you want my help to start your first cash flowing Airbnb in 2025... DM me 'START' and let's chat.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My house has this weird little nook in the guest room and it’s haunted me ever since I toured the house for the first time. Like I literally couldn’t stop thinking about it. So the moment that I bought it, I knew exactly what I wanted to do. Turn it into the coziest little reading nook of all time. And of course Carlos is helping me make it happen. He built the bed frame, he added crown molding to the front and he painted it this dreamy dusty pink. We’ve done it! I’ve been dreaming about this daybed since the day I bought this house. Carlos is really my fairy godmother. He’s literally making all my dreams come true. Now that she’s painted, we’re going to DIY a mattress. I bought this six inch memory foam from Target. I just figured that we’d just cut it to the size of the bed. My brain is like loading. Yo, I’m just gonna sleep here instead. We created this little palette and I’m gonna cut the mattress out to this size. This room is so small, I’m like struggling. I don’t have like anything to cut this, so we’re gonna use a kitchen knife. It feels like I’m baking a cake. It worked! We’ve done it. I’m so proud of myself. I’m so excited about this, you guys have no idea.

videos_analizados.call_to_action: Comment 'CHICKENS101' or click the link below.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: 11 years ago we decided we wanted to know exactly where our food was coming from. We wanted to stop relying on the grocery store for meat and start raising and processing our own chickens. But here's the truth: in the beginning, we had no idea what we were doing. We struggled to figure out the right setup, how many birds to raise, how to schedule hatch dates and processing dates, and how to actually break down a chicken the right way. It took years of trial and error to get a system in place that made raising meat birds simple, effective, and stress-free. That's why we created Farm to Freezer, to give you the exact step-by-step system we wish we had when we started. Inside this course, you'll learn how to raise healthy meat birds with just 10 to 15 minutes of care a day, how to schedule everything so you're never scrambling at the last minute, step-by-step video tutorials for processing so you can confidently butcher your own chickens without stress or hesitation, the best time-saving strategies, and how to stock your freezer with high-quality homegrown meat. Because once you know how to do this the right way, raising your own meat birds isn't hard, time-consuming, or expensive. It's one of the most empowering things you can do for your family's food security. Comment CHICKENS101 or click the link below to join Farm to Freezer today.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: El 'Viaje del Héroe' resumido en menos de 90 segundos.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y cálido, resaltando los azules del mar y naranjas del sol
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inspiración inicial, empatía por el esfuerzo, asombro por los paisajes, tensión por los problemas y motivación final.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Nearly three years ago today I packed my bags and left for Canada, thinking I would only be gone for six months. I am attempting to be the first person to surf every country in the world. For the first year I worked odd jobs, fencing, cleaning, whatever I could to save for the resort. I learned to snowboard, but after being rejected by immigration and Covid hitting, I headed for Hawaii. I stayed for six months, saved all my money and landed in the Galapagos Islands. We swam with sea lions, we surfed with them. I saw Machu Picchu, I drank Ayahuasca in the jungle of Ecuador. I spent months moving through Colombia, Panama, Nicaragua, before ending up back in Hawaii. We drove across the islands, hiked the mountains, and after another six months, I headed for South Africa. I bought a car, lived in a tent, and started driving. I surfed the southern coast, saw whales in South Africa, watched the sun set over Victoria Falls, slept on an air mattress in the middle of Botswana. I've been to Mauritius, Madagascar (where I got food poisoning), Tanzania, and Burundi. I've stepped foot in 42 countries, raised money for NGOs, and I have no idea when this journey is ending. Follow along to see where we go next.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: We just spent all day painting this wall, burned through 30 liters of paint, and now I'm having a full-blown colour choice crisis. Welcome back to episode 4 of turning these old courts into a Padel club in Coffs Harbour. Today's challenge: transform this wall with a spray gun none of us know how to use. Last Friday hit us with a plot twist: our shipment got bumped up a week. Great for the schedule, chaotic for today. That 'eventually' paint job is now a 'finish it today or fall behind' kind of situation. First stop was the hire shop. We've got 24 hours with this spray gun to transform the wall. Just arrived on site, Dad is a legend and he is taping, and Roberto is... sawing? I'll give you a tour. Puppy! Hi my boy. Dad is doing us a solid by helping, but none of us had any idea how to use this thing, which became pretty obvious when the paint started going everywhere. After some trial and error, we finally got it working, only to realize we'd burn through all 15 liters of paint on just the first coat. I ran to grab another tin and Roberto took over the gun for round two. That's when lunch led to my crisis. Looking around at what we'd done, I started to seriously question my color choice. I find building a business is full of second guesses, but usually they're not in a giant wall staring back at you. Moral of the story: painting a wall is just like building this club. Nothing exactly goes to plan. Not long till our shipping container arrives and it's the same color, so hopefully everything ties together and we'll be back with fewer regrets.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My mom hasn't eaten meat in over 30 years, but she still makes the best butter chicken I've ever had. She's a pure vegetarian. No eggs, no fish sauce, nothing. Basically vegan except for milk. And yet growing up, she cooked non-vegetarian meals for us like a professional chef. My favorite dish was always butter chicken with garlic naan. No restaurant even comes close. You'd think that'd be impossible, cooking meat you can't even taste. But that's just mothers, man. They're magic. She tastes the curry for spice, salt, sweetness, but once the chicken went in, it was all vibes. And it didn't just stop at chicken. Mutton, shrimp, you name it. If we brought her a recipe or even casually said we liked something, she'd make it. This isn't a story about food, or about vegetarian versus non-vegetarian. This is a story about a mother's love. One that transcends personal belief. And if that's not a metaphor for sacrifice, I don't know what is.

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Millennials y Gen Z interesados en cultura, familia y gastronomía.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Four years ago my brother and I started... We're from a small town called Dimapur, Nagaland. And we just started making things out of wood. Ever since then we've made over 100 different designs. Went from 0 to 39,000 followers and taught ourselves how to take pictures and videos. A lot of you guys have been asking us if we have a website. No proper catalogue and our packaging still sucks. It's been four years since we started but the truth is that... and we're on a mission to get everything sorted out by the end of this year. So if you're into woodworking or just want to see two brothers struggle in the process of building their dream... and maybe we'll all learn something along the way.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Natural en exteriores de carrera, artificial suave en interiores.
equipamiento: Micrófono externo (opcional, pero mejora el audio)
Material de archivo (B-roll) de la actividad mencionada
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Establecimiento de autoridad seguido de una muestra de vulnerabilidad inmediata
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de vulnerabilidad (The Comeback/Lesson learned)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, ligeramente saturado en las escenas de carrera.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la euforia del logro inicial al dolor físico, pasando por la decepción del fracaso objetivo, terminando en una empoderada aceptación y orgullo.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (busca engagement por identificación emocional)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Yesterday I ran the London Marathon in 2 hours and 30 minutes. That's what this chafe is from. It really stings. And I massively missed what I was aiming to achieve. But I have 0 regrets and I will tell you why. 2 days before the race, my coach and I sat down to talk final game plan. On paper, I'd had the best training block of my life. I'm the fittest I've ever been and so we decided to seize the opportunity in front of us and take a big swing. That swing was to go out at 2:23-2:24 pace, which is around 2 minutes faster than my best. And we both knew that taking a big swing either meant a chance to pull off something sensational or that the wheels could come off at some point and ultimately we miss. So it was time to be brave. And I was brave. I committed to that pace and honestly I had a great time until at around 9 miles when my body started to fight me. And that started a long solo 17 mile grind to the finish. And over those 17 miles, I went from seeing my goals slip between my fingers, the bitterness of disappointment after 3 months of hard work, to immense gratitude and admiration for my body that despite the pain amplifying with every step, it endured. To pride for the resilience I showed to keep going when it would have been way easier to stop. And the clearest understanding that I will be better for this. With hindsight, would I do anything differently? Absolutely not. I've gained so much from having the confidence to swing big even if we missed this time. None of that fitness or hard work has been undone and the growth from this race is already a part of the next chapter.

videos_analizados.escenario: Interior de una casa antigua en proceso de renovación.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - muy luminosa gracias a los grandes ventanales de la casa.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Planteamiento de un problema inusual (falta de almacenamiento) para generar empatía y curiosidad por la solución.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Proceso paso a paso (Storytelling de proyecto)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Jump cuts constantes para mantener el dinamismo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y natural, resaltando los tonos de la madera y las paredes azules.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la incredulidad por el problema a la inspiración por la solución técnica.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito: 'Tomorrow, we get to the fun part' (invita a seguir la serie).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Would you buy a house with no closets? Well, my parents did! It's a 1915 home with lots of character, tall ceilings, and fun details... but no storage. So let's make over my parents' future bedroom and turn this entire wall into built-in cabinetry with a little reading nook for good measure. First up, I had to remove this stand-alone wardrobe left by the old owners, which was extremely heavy and a little dangerous but I managed to get it out of the room. And then it was time to remove the crown molding. I have big plans for this room in addition to the built-ins, including tackling the ugly popcorn ceiling, so I need these out of the way. And then it was time to break out my power saws. The 100-year-old baseboards in the room are extra tall, so I can't just use two by fours as the built-in platform. So instead I'm cutting them to custom heights out of plywood. These are my extremely ugly sketches... friendly reminder that you don't need to be an artist to build cool things. And once I got all of my pieces cut, it was time for assembly. I used wood glue, brad nails, and screws to anchor everything together, removed the shoe molding, and then leveled and anchored my platforms to the wall. Tomorrow, we get to the fun part... adding in the cabinets.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - muy luminosa, proveniente de ventanas grandes

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Day in the life (DITL) / Routine Video
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y brillante, alta saturación para resaltar la limpieza
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la curiosidad inicial a la empatía por la situación médica, pasando por la satisfacción de ver la limpieza, terminando en una sensación de calidez y ternura.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito en el valor educativo/emocional)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is what my morning looks like fostering three medical pug puppies. First I let everybody out and give them some yard time before heading back inside and getting all of their meds ready for the day. These puppies were surrendered to our rescue after their mom died during labor and have had some health complications along the way. Right now the two girls have some upper respiratory issues so they're both getting some antibiotics every morning and night. After meds have been administered it's time to eat. They all get puppy mousse on licking mats since they're all so small and they eat every four hours on the clock so I have to do this multiple times a day and it's easily their favorite part of the day. When they're eating I get to work cleaning their puppy pen but today was a deep clean day. I do this about once a week so I have to wash everything in there and make sure it's all scrubbed down. The sheer amount of laundry is the worst part about fostering puppies. I sweep and swiffer and deep clean the floor before laying down a new potty pad and I reset up their heating pad and humidifier that helps with their pneumonia symptoms. Of course the deep clean day fresh blankets are a must. They have a little nest area on their heating pad and then also their donut bed. I clean their water bowl and humidifier, refill the tank, get everything back in there and then get to work on taking care of myself, making myself breakfast. And then with the time I have left I eat breakfast, hang out with the puppies, get them nice and tired before putting them back in their pen. By this point it's about 8 AM and I can get going with the rest of my day once they're nice and tired.

equipamiento: Kit de limpieza de ductos de secadora

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Alarma inicial -> Curiosidad -> Satisfacción por la limpieza -> Shock por el descubrimiento del bloqueo -> Alivio/Seguridad
videos_analizados.call_to_action: I'm sharing to spread awareness (Implícito: revisa tu secadora)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: How cleaning my dryer ended with a repairman at my house telling me how lucky we were that the house did not burn down. Let's dive into it. In the United States alone, there's more than $35 million in property damage due to fires started by dryer. If you use dryer sheets, you absolutely should be taking your lint filter out and washing it with soap and water every few months because there's always going to be an invisible film... Now this was my first time unscrewing the actual hatch... I was able to suck out a ton of lint... Now it's time to get out my attachment and get down in there... Now I'm pulling the dryer out... I'm just gonna send my tools up this chute... Oh my Gawd, this tunnel is long as shyt... I knew there was problems because the flap was not moving... I'm on the phone with the repairman because it is 100% blocked. It's a wonder the house did not burn down. I'm sharing to spread awareness. Love your face.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My doctor told me protein powder was not natural (juice). My mom takes me to this doctor, right? And you would think a doctor has a little bit of common sense, basic knowledge. She's like 'Hey, what supplements are you taking? How's your diet like?'. I'm already at a very heavy weight because I've been pushing food for the past year. I'm just taking multivitamins, creatine, and protein powder. She straight up looked at me and said 'Uh, why are you taking these things? You do realize that anything that is in a powder form means it's not natural'. Bro, are you serious? Like, all this is, is supplement on my already diet. What does a multivitamin do? Supplement on the micronutrients you are needing. What does protein powder do? Supplement on hitting your protein goal. We go back and forth for like 15-20 minutes and the basic conclusion was 'Well, okay, if you're not going to listen to me, I have a degree and I studied'. Well, I guess if you take some protein powder, you're gonna have nothing but just some big arms and some nice gains. Check this out.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: A customer changed their mind and tried to avoid paying. This is what happened.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Exterior día
equipamiento: Micrófono de solapa (opcional para mejor VO)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Planteamiento de conflicto inmediato (Storytime)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Conflicto con cliente difícil / Lección de negocios

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, empatía por el trabajador, frustración con el cliente y satisfacción final por la postura del profesional.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (fomenta comentarios por controversia)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: A customer changed their mind and tried to avoid paying. This is what happened. Recently I had a job staining concrete for a customer and as I was giving the customer their quote, their main concern was the price. To help save the customer money, I gave them the option of using a cheaper stain which required more maintenance or a more expensive stain that would last longer. The customer decided to go with the cheaper stain so before I started the job I had them sign a contract. The following day I arrived at the customer's property and started pressure washing and etching the concrete to get it ready to be stained. Shortly after prepping the concrete, the customer came out of their house and told me that they had changed their mind and they would like to go with the more expensive stain. I told the customer that I would use the more expensive option but before I get started I needed to change our contract. The customer said that changing the contract wasn't necessary and that they promised to pay me the right amount. I told the customer their promises don't pay my bills and after they kept refusing to sign a new contract I decided that I wasn't comfortable and I walked away.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Relatabilidad para otros contratistas y profesionales de servicios.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el flujo de la historia sin distraer del mensaje.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'Storytime' sobre un conflicto laboral con el que muchos dueños de negocios se identifican. El final genera debate sobre si hizo bien en irse.

But today I picked up a paintbrush for the first time in 10 years and this is what I made.

The best brands nowadays realize that telling their story and being authentic is what actually engages people. So I'm documenting the entire journey 'Selling a Billion Bricks'. Episode 1. videos_analizados.guion_completo: You're losing your boyfriend this week but don't worry, I can teach you. It's actually very easy and it's my job. The first thing we're gonna start with is the offense. One part of the offense is known as the offensive line. This consists of left tackle and right tackle, the two guards and the center. These are the big guys and their job is to block these men from invading the quarterback. If you've seen the movie The Blind Side, Michael Oher plays left tackle, which is protecting the quarterback's blind spot. Now you have your quarterback, that's your Tom Brady, Patrick Mahomes. Think about him as like the captain of the ship. He is gonna control the ball and the offense the entire time. Now in order for the offense to score a touchdown, we need the quarterback to move the ball down the field. So what he's gonna do is he's gonna look for people that are open. Wide receivers can be open. Now the wide receiver's job is to just run and catch the ball. Another way we can move the ball down the field is if the quarterback hands the ball to these guys, they're known as the running backs. These guys are responsible for gaining yards and they can also block for the quarterback. And lastly you have your tight end. When you think tight end, I want you to think Taylor Swift's boyfriend. That is Travis Kelce's job. He could run, he could catch, he could block, he could do it all. That is the basic understanding of offense. Your first quiz is on Thursday. Ladies, go show off this season.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Suave y uniforme, estilo estudio
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste de expectativas y formato inusual (presentación corporativa para tema doméstico)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Expectativa vs Realidad / Traducción de lenguaje de pareja
videos_analizados.color_grading: Limpio, saturación natural, tonos cálidos
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Curiosidad inicial, risa por identificación, y satisfacción con el remate visual final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay CTA explícito (implícito en el humor para compartir)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: What my wife says versus what she means doesn't always equate to what she means. Let me explain. This is how I solve my wife's hunger paradox. This one's easy. If my wife says she's hungry, it means she's hungry. Got it? Good. Now she asks me 'Are you hungry?'. It means she's hungry. She doesn't really care if I'm hungry, she's just trying to tell me she's hungry. So when I hear this question, even if I'm not hungry, I always say yes. So now I'm driving home and I decide to pick up some food and I ask her 'Do you want anything?' and she says 'No'. What she really means is that she's hungry, she just doesn't even know it yet. Because by the time I get home, she'll be hungry and she'll want to eat all my food. This last one's a tough one. My wife's angry and I can't figure out why. I even try to say sorry, I don't even know what I'm sorry about, so I try but it makes it worse. And that's because she's hangry. So in conclusion, my wife is always hungry.

videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y dar una sensación de ligereza
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un formato de 'presentación seria' para un tema de pareja extremadamente común y relatable. La estructura clara y el remate visual final generan alta retención.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos en la parte superior y contenido de las diapositivas

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I do not believe in 'lucky girl syndrome', I believe in being intensely intentional. So here is my vision board for 2025. I've been sharing my goals for years now and this year's theme is... Simply Iconic. And yes, imma say it out loud, this year's BIG goal is to hit... 1 Million! (on Instagram). Besides my 30 Lessons series, I also want to launch other iconic projects like these: wedding series, music video, office drama, and be featured for them in the industry. I've also always wanted to attend award ceremonies like the Gold Gala, but I want to earn the invite, you know? It'd also be CRAZY to be featured in a Magazine. This year's also the year to seriously build my team and launch products. If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together. So I just wrote out my whole 2025 strategy and operations doc. It's kind of like a PRD, it has my core values, mission, milestones, and I even got my old manager to review it! Looking good. And then I gotta host my wedding. That is coming up! For my moonshot goals, I am an absolute fiend for fantasy. If I could be part of this world somehow through voice-acting, hosting, being in the cast, I don't know, that'd be insane! I only have a minute so the rest of my Vision Board is on my IG profile. I also made a template for you to use, yes, it is all free, so check it out. Hope this helps, what are your dreams? Lmk down below. Happy 2025 Unichlos! Also, do you like my socks?

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm trying a different coffee for each letter of the alphabet. Today's letter is F and we're making a frappe. The greek one, not the blended one you're thinking of. In 1957, a nescafe salesman couldn't find hot water for his coffee, so he grabbed a shaker. Two teaspoons of instant coffee, about four tablespoons of cold water, and a spoonful of sugar is all you need. Shake it pretty aggressively and as you do it, it'll turn into this thick, light brown foam. It's kind of like coffee clouds. Add it to a cup full of ice and fill the rest of the way up with cold water. By the 80s, this was everywhere in Greece. It's basically their whole personality. If you don't believe me, just read some of the comments. You know the drill, letter G tomorrow. Let's hear your bets.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La receta es extremadamente sencilla y los ingredientes son comunes (café instantáneo y azúcar).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener un ambiente relajado pero rítmico.

Do you think he is natural? I don't... look at the popping out forehead. His hands are shaking, he's busted on gear. Do you think he is natural? He's intimidating, I'm going to say yes. No, say no. Is she natural? I'm stuck on this one. She's currently been on gear. Do you think he is natural? Kinda gladiator, and also forehead... in fact, natural. Ohhh! Is he natural? I don't think so because his arms... no way. He's the size of a Mac truck. She's awesome!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My reels used to look like this and now they look like this. Welcome to Jumpstart January, a mini series designed to get you out of your content rut and make 2025 your best Instagram growth year yet. I'm going to give you my best tip when it comes to create eye-catching reels. First of all, as always, the hook is everything. Now there's three ways to hook people on a reel. The first one is obviously with what you say. The second way to hook is text. Make your text pop on the screen. And the third way is a visual hook. It could be just a funky jumper, it could be you pouring water, just do something that catches the eye. Tip number two: vary the length. Instagram recommends to create reels that are 30 to 90 seconds, but I would highly recommend that you try different lengths. Tip number three: cut out the fluff when you edit your reels. You really want to go straight to the point. Anything that does not help to understand what you're saying should be gone. You want to make every second count. Tip number four: show don't just tell. Use b-rolls, captions and text on screen to really emphasize your point. And the last tip: you really want to build a vibe. You want to be intentional with the vibe that you're giving off. Not only is it going to help people recognize you, but it's also going to help you stand out.

One: In the first three seconds of your video, zoom in slightly while you let your captions come on screen as you say them. Make the music go silent at your last word for anticipation. Two: Mess up. Seriously, mess up because people are much more likely to watch something surprising. Three: Release less content. Put out one video a week and make it excellent. Four: Don't over consume, but let yourself be inspired by other creators. Btw, if you want my entire viral video workflow so that you can learn how to make videos like this one, just comment the word NOODLE. It will be available for the next 72 hours. videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Softbox frontal que crea una iluminación uniforme y profesional.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de números específicos y promesa de revelación de método (breakdown)

videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, vibrante, con énfasis en los tonos azules de la ropa de la presentadora.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Just 3 videos took my client from 400 to 10,000 followers. Let's break it down. Number one is polarising content. Now this can have pros and cons. Pro because you'll be getting a lot of comments because a lot of people will be disagreeing with each other in the comment section. But the con is that a lot of people disagreeing with you publicly can feel really, really bad. Number two is connecting your content with something that's been trending for a while. Sure, a video on Ambani's wedding or Ozempic will get you the engagement right now, but let's say stuff that Andrew Tate talks about will be relevant for a certain age group for a few more years. Just pick the latter. And number three is using all the emotions. We didn't just stop at sad and happy. We played with inadequacy, fulfilment, excitement, guilt, gratefulness, amusement, admiration and so many more. So share this with your friend who thinks fast cuts is all it takes.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My student got into Stanford without a 3.0 GPA, no SAT, and no extracurriculars. Here's how. Before she met me, she struggled with making her application stand out since she had a 3.0 GPA, no SAT, and she had no idea how to showcase unique qualities. But top universities care more about passion than stats, so we centered her personal statement around her love for structure. 1. Begin with a captivating introduction with visual imagery and sensory details. 2. Body paragraphs that covered early experiences, turning points, overcoming challenges, and key epiphanies. And 3, a conclusion that summed up her growth journey and how she wants to move forward with the lessons she learned. Save this video.

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial (Softbox frontal para la creadora)

videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con intriga, pasa por la admiración estética de los productos y termina con el deseo de que la colaboración sea real.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Would you buy this?
videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I were to create a collab campaign for Skims, this is how I'd do it. First, I would partner with Uggs, which plays perfectly with Skims' mission of creating soft and comfortable clothing. I would create several accessories. The first would be a headband made out of the Uggs materials and with the Skims minimalist aesthetic. I would also create gloves made out of the same material and a coat which would be the star of the show. The campaign photoshoot I would create would be called Cloud 9 by Skims, where you would see models wearing the Skims and Uggs collab pieces while sitting really comfortably in clouds. This would portray this whole feeling of softness and comfort that the pieces provide. I would also create a pop-up which would sell these limited edition pieces that would have the same Ugg exterior and interior to tie it all in together. This would be a cozy spot for people to sit on soft chairs and take a look at the comfy products. I would also have hot chocolate with marshmallows served with Skims and Ugg logos, again emphasizing the softness of their products. Would you buy this? You know I would.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: How I got my reels from this to this in 30 days. Step 1: come up with 10 to 15 niche related keywords. videos_analizados.guion_completo: You look really fit. DROP THE ROUTINE. ur body looks so good. One workout routine and that exercise is called 12-3-30. This is a great exercise for someone like me who doesn't like working too hard for things. What 12-3-30 is, is going on the treadmill for 30 minutes at 12 incline. If you wanna see results quick, then do this every day, 3 days a week at minimum. I sweat so much afterwards but the 30 minutes go by so fast if you put on a show or talk to a friend. I put these on my wrist during it to keep me toned and I do weight training too but I mostly prioritize... if you're a beginner and cannot go the full 30 minutes, just start with ten.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: My student got into Stanford with no extracurriculars and here's how. Before she met me, she struggled with making her application stand out since she had a 3.0 GPA, no SAT, and she had no idea how to showcase unique qualities. But top universities care more about passion than stats, so we centered her personal statement around her love for structure. 1. Begin with a captivating introduction with visual imagery and sensory details. 2. Body paragraphs that covered early experiences, turning points, overcoming challenges, and key epiphanies. And 3, a conclusion that summed up her growth journey and how she wants to move forward with the lessons she learned. Save this video.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I'm in a calorie deficit and these seven habits dramatically transformed my physique in just 30 days. Take it from somebody who's actually seen real results and I'm not here to gatekeep. Starting today I'll post a new video every evening for the next seven days introducing just one little habit that you can start to implement from the next day. Make sure you hit follow to catch all 7 videos. But here's the key: you need to layer each new habit on top of the last. It's not about doing each habit for one day and then moving on. Stick with them every day, including weekends, and you could see a similar transformation in just a month. Coming in hot with number one: stop viewing yourself as an amateur and start viewing yourself as a professional at meeting your fitness goals. Anyone can do this, whether you're a beginner or an intermediate. It is all about what goes on up here. When you think like a pro, tough choices tend to get easier. That one chocolate bar, that single packet of crisps, that slice of cake won't feel so hard to resist. Remember these are just tests of your willpower. If you let it win, it will win.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: El valor educativo del concepto 'habit layering' y la curiosidad por los siguientes días.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo sin distraer del mensaje hablado.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el formato de 'serie' que obliga al usuario a seguir la cuenta para ver el resto de los días. Combina una promesa aspiracional con consejos psicológicos (mindset) en lugar de solo dieta.

videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas interesadas en fitness, pérdida de grasa y desarrollo personal.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: 7 Hábitos para Transformar el Físico en 30 Días

Most students make the mistake of studying unnecessary material, so to avoid this, go to YouTube and look up comprehensive reviews of your AP subject and paste the link in Turbolearn.ai. It'll automatically generate notes and quizzes for you that highlight the most important topics you need to know. This way you can study the concepts that actually matter.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I were to create a pilates brand today, this is how I'd do it. First of all, I would go with a metaphorical concept and call it The Kitchen, where your body is your recipe. All the classes would be on a daily menu with the trainers called chefs. Each class would follow the whole kitchen vibe and be called things like Prep Work and Chef's Choice. There would be different memberships called the Sous Chef, the Head Chef, the Commis Chef, where you would get different amount of classes and different perks based on the membership that you have. Also the merch would have a very kitchen-like theme, like this t-shirt that says 'I am the secret ingredient' and this tote that says 'Certified kitchen member'. There could also be a social media challenge called #mykitchencreations where members could show their progress and how they've cooked their dream bodies metaphorically. Would you go to this pilates club? I would.
