videos_analizados.guion_completo: If I ever made it to the news I thought it would at least be for something illegal, turns out it's for losing 10kgs. That's wild. This is part 3 of how I lost 10 kgs by turning weight loss into a game. I even talked about this before the video even hit 1 million views, you all clearly ate it up. The hack is: Reward System. I turned weight loss into a game. I made a list of things I wanted to buy, places I wanted to go, dumb things that I didn't need but wanted. Every kilo down, instant reward. I lost a kilo, bought a yoga mat. Lost another kilo, got a projector for movie nights. Big milestones, bigger bribes. At this point I'm a professional self-gaslighter. The problem is weight loss is boring as hell and I made it not boring and that's why it worked. If you're still watching, I know you're struggling with staying consistent, trust me this will fix that. Weight loss shouldn't feel like a punishment, it should feel like leveling up in a game. I've got more hacks, follow for part 4.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Adidas commented that I am their new favorite designer. So I am seeing this as an opportunity to create designs to try to convince Adidas, a huge brand, to hire me, a self-taught 24-year-old designer. Without further ado, let's jump into it. I started off by taking lots of images of my friend's Adidas, which then led me to start to experiment with Photoshop, creating fun collages and layering my designs over these images. But I wanted to take things a step further than I usually do. I learned how to turn my flat designs into shiny 3D objects. And then I wanted to add some movement, so I learned a new software called After Effects to animate my designs exactly how I wanted them to look. I then drew the Adidas logo as a cloud, no AI used here, and turned specific flowers from their most recent shoe collab into fun 3D designs. Anyways, enough blabbing, here are the final designs.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I literally used to have a... I worked so hard to lose belly fat. It took me a really long... Belly fat is probably the most stubborn area I swear. I would say it honestly took me three years to like fully get rid of sustainably. Really comes down to what you're eating you guys. Like you cannot be having chips and cookies all the time and expect to lose belly... I kept my diet really clean and I do ab routines at the end of my workout maybe two to three times a week. So I'm going to show you an ab... We're going to do it together and we're going to be consistent. So it's three exercises. We're going to do them back to back. We only rest at the end of the round. We're going to start with knee... So your palms are going to be behind you. Legs are going to be out. You're going to drive those knees towards your chest and back down. We're going to do 20 reps. Want you to think about using your abs during the movement. Don't just be like you know like use your abs. Mind to muscle connection. Remember no breaks in between. We're going to go into slowed down mountain climbers. Knees going to go all the way in and back. Then you're going to rotate. Keep it really tight. We're going to do this for forty five seconds. Now we go into our side plank because we want to hit our obliques. Gonna make sure your elbow is right underneath your shoulder. Fade a straight line with your body. Keeping my hips up as high as possible. And we're going to hold it for 30 seconds on each side. Other side... Squeeze your... I swear to God it'll keep your core... Now you start from the... You do it three times total. Call it a day.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: How I grew my glutes from this to this in a year and this is what I've done. I've been consistent, stop switching up your workouts and exercises too often. I've been doing the same exercises and just focus on progressive overload. Progressive overload means you're getting stronger and it's the key. It's not enough to just go to the gym, you really have to push your limits and train with intention. I've kept a high protein diet, 2 grams of protein per kg body weight daily and eaten enough food to fuel my workouts and get stronger. That's why food is your best friend. Effective weekly volume, I make sure to not overtrain by aiming for 10 to 20 working sets. Your diet and workout can definitely make or break your results. Apply for my online coaching to get a personalized meal and workout plan.

I'm going to show you the best resource to start your research journey even if you're in high school. It's called Project Cleris, a nonprofit by student researchers at MIT, Harvard, and NYU. And it literally has everything from research courses to email templates to send to professors. I personally wish I had this when I was in high school because not only does research make your application really stand out, but they also have a ton of opportunities to join their team and boost your resume even more. Did I forget to mention it's literally free? But if you want to learn how I personally got into research, comment 'CELL' and I'll send you a document that outlines my journey.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: She studied for one day and got the third highest grade in class. Just 36 hours before her exam, she sent me a DM telling me she was struggling and only passing because her teachers reset her grades. Two days later, she sends me this. Here's the strategy we came up with. 1: Multiple Choice Questions are designed to trap you. She needed to know what kinds of traps do they set, what types of answers repeat, and how do they phrase 'best' vs 'correct' answers. Step 2: Quick scan. Answer only the questions she was 100% sure about. No overthinking, no second-guessing. Prediction: instead of looking at the answer choices first, I advised her to read the question, cover the answers and predict her own. Then she would check which answer matched her prediction. Step 3: She learned to spot 'half-right' answers. If one part of an option is wrong, the whole answer is wrong. That's how she turned one and a half days of studying into her first ever A. Now imagine what you could do if you just knew exactly what to do every time. One guide, every academic challenge solved. Nothing like this exists. If you're serious about changing your academic life, comment 'I'm serious' and I'll send it to you, or simply click this in my bio.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: I jumped my SAT score from a 1270 to a 1580 in just one month. And how that score opened doors to Harvard, Stanford, Yale, Duke, UPenn, Rice and more. When I first took the SAT, the reading section felt impossible. But then I discovered this one simple strategy that made everything click. Focus on the main ideas and how they connect. As you read, ask yourself: what's the big takeaway? For example, is the passage making a claim or giving an example? Focus on what the test writers want you to notice. Then, look for relationships between ideas and passages. Do the ideas agree or disagree? For example, if there's two main ideas in a passage but they oppose each other, the answer choice will probably be about how idea one challenges idea two. Finally, skip unnecessary details like specific examples unless the question asks for that. Stick to the main ideas to save time and stay focused. If you want access to my full guide on digital SAT steps and the exact steps I took to get to a 1580, just comment 'DSAT' and I'll send it to you.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This used to be my shoulders and this is my shoulders now. And those who are asking, I did not get trap Botox. And here's some tips that I did for at least a year to achieve this shoulder look. The first thing I did was doing lots of stretches. During the day I'll do this side-to-side stretching at least like 20 times a day. Whenever I have time I'll just constantly do these stretches and it makes all your shoulder pains go away and it will constantly reduce the size of your trap muscles. Next, I slowly spin my head five times on each side. I'm doing it faster right now but it should be a lot slower so it can just really pull all the muscles. And next, when I'm sitting down, I'll purposely care about my posture. I'll never do this. I'll just put all my shoulders back. In addition to that, I did ballet for a year, like a full year. And my teacher was constantly pushing me to have the better posture and to make sure that my shoulders are back like this. Be consistent and you will one day reach this look.

Número 5: Péptidos, factores de crecimiento y células madre para regeneración profunda.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: After dropping 100 pounds of body fat, here's one thing I learned the hard way so you don't have to. However long you think this is supposed to take, double it. And not because you're not going to try, not because your plan won't work, but because we have been conditioned to expect instant gratification from our bodies and that is literally not the way you were designed. It took you 10 years to gain but you want it off in 21 days. There are 10 years worth of habits and beliefs and systems and routines that the carnivore diet can't unteach you. On top of that, it's going to be your birthday, you're going to get invited to travel, you'll miss a day in the gym, you'll have stop-starts for the rest of this year. At best, losing a pound a week is attainable, but your body won't lose weight every week. After you lose about 10% of your body weight, your body's going to decide that it don't want to lose weight no more. You don't know and that's what I had to learn the hard way. But that's why I'm here to teach you. Let's learn together. Leave me in the comments, I got something for you.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Okay bish, you don't need to starve yourself trying to lose body fat. Coming from someone who lost 30 pounds and kept it off for good, let me introduce you to volumizing meals. Here's a good example: cauliflower rice versus white rice. One cup of white rice is 206 calories while the cauliflower rice only has 50 calories. You would literally have to eat four cups of this to equal this. Here's another example: bananas versus strawberries. 100 grams of this is 90 calories, 100 grams of strawberries is 33 calories. You would literally have to eat 300 grams of strawberries to equal one piece of this shit. If you're a girly who loves peanut butter, regular peanut butter is 200 cals per serving while the PB2 powder is only 45. You can have four and a half servings of this for one of this. Here are some other things I add to my meals bish: shredded lettuce, 85 grams of that is only 15 calories. Sparkling water because of the carbonation it keeps you full, zero calories. Now I'm not saying this or that and what's good or bad for you, what I am saying is be smart about budgeting your calories so that way you can stay in that deficit, losing body fat and staying full at the same time. And that's it bish, if you wanna learn more on how to build an hourglass body without starving yourself, you guys know where to find me.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Here's how my student went from a 1310 to a 1590, opened himself up to MIT, Stanford, Dartmouth and Duke. His math skill was already strong, so the biggest growth happened in the verbal section. Here's what I told him: most non-fiction passages on the SAT are going to be following a formula. It's either going to present a problem or change. First you need to decide: is this a problem or is this change? If you decide that this is a problem, interview the passage with these three questions: 1. What is the cause of the problem? 2. What is the solution of the problem? 3. Can the problem be solved? If this is a change, interview the passage differently: 1. What was the old way? 2. What is the new way? 3. What is the result? If you want to get my passage template list, comment 'passage templates' in the comments.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: 78% of the time that I'm on set as a model I get compliments on my skin especially from makeup artists and today I'll be showing you my exact skincare routine that can help you achieve this glowy skin. First I like to use the cleanser from BRMUD. I like to emulsify it first and apply it on my skin for one minute. The biggest mistake I see people make is that they apply it on their skin and wash it off right away. After I wash the cleanser off I tap dry with my towel to avoid any skin irritation. Next I like to use my toner from Glow. If you don't know anything about toners it essentially hydrates and balances the skin almost like a primer for your next steps. The third step is the serum. I've really been liking the serum from Haua because it leaves my skin feeling really hydrated, glowy and also has anti-aging properties to help with my wrinkles. For the fourth step I like to use the noni ampoule from Celimax. I can proudly say that this product is my holy grail. If you want glowy skin this is a non-negotiable. The next step is a moisturizer. The one I'm using here is from Aestura. Guys if you guys go through my page 90% of the videos will have this exact product. Also if you want your moisturizer to absorb better try this upward motion that I'm doing here. And for the final step I've been using this tinted lip balm from AOU and it's been great at giving my lips some color and keeping it hydrated. Follow this routine consistently and you will see results like I did. Thanks for watching.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is how I got my teeth from this to this in just one month completely naturally. And no, I'm not going to be trying to sell you some 80 dollar product. My name is Nick and I like to find free scientific ways to make yourself look better and get healthy. And this is all you need: coconut oil and baking soda will make your teeth white. You simply put a tablespoon of coconut oil and then mix a tablespoon of baking soda. Baking soda can have a negative effect on your enamel, so I recommend only using it two times per week. The rest of the time just use coconut oil. Coconut oil in itself can be a toothpaste because it's so antibacterial. But if you're really trying to go the next step, I would add a drop of tea tree oil and a drop of peppermint for the taste.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: These are the products I use to keep my teeth white and healthy as a dental hygienist. First things first, are you surprised? No, flossing is so important. I love using Cocofloss, it has a thick filament and I like using the C-shape method to really get underneath the gums. Brushing with my OG Euthymol toothpaste. This guy is so spicy and so good, but I love it because it helps remove plaque buildup and really helps kill bad breath. It has eucalyptus and thymol and I'm obsessed. PureDia whitening kit has been my go-to since I drink a lot of coffee. The gel is formulated with Teraloba, which is a unique blend of 35% hydrogen peroxide and lettuce complex extract. Okay, dentalist next level, so it comes with the whitening gel, a shade tracker to track your progress, gauze to put underneath your tongue, and a mouthpiece that is so comfortable to use for 30 minutes. The regular way to use this whitening kit is to dry the teeth and apply the gel directly to the teeth and then put the mouthpiece. But recently I discovered a hack where I actually applied the gel directly to the mouthpiece and it's so efficient and mess-free. Lastly, I had a lot of questions asking if this gave me sensitivity and the answer is no, I used it for 14 days. But if you're prone to sensitivity, my biggest advice is to use Sensodyne one week before and then one week after. So if you have any questions, let me know. Bye!

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Same recipe! But what's the difference? For this muffin I used my 4 tips for making big bakery style muffins at home and you can tell that it really makes a big difference. Tip number one: rest your batter for 15 minutes in the mixing bowl before you scoop it into your pan. Tip number two kind of goes along with tip number three: you're only gonna fill every other hole in your muffin pan and you're gonna fill them super full with batter. I usually do a heaping half cup. And tip number four is that you're gonna start your muffins off at a high temperature initially, so that's gonna be 425 degrees for the first 7 minutes and then you're gonna lower them down to 350 degrees for the remaining bake time. The high heat start allows the muffins to rise rapidly setting the outside surface creating that dome shape.

videos_analizados.patron_viral: Lista de recursos / Tutorial de habilidad rápida
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural, tonos cálidos y saturación equilibrada
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inspiración inicial, curiosidad por los retos, y satisfacción al ver el resultado final.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Screenshot them and practice every day
videos_analizados.guion_completo: English is my second language, so here are the three tongue twisters that I did over and over and over and over and over again to improve my pronunciation. No. 1 Betty Botter. You know, Betty Botter bought some butter but she said the butter's bitter, if I put it in my batter it will make my batter bitter. This tongue twister will perfect your flap T like water, butter, better. No. 2 the 33 thieves. You know, 33 thieves thought that they thrilled the throne throughout Thursday. This will perfect the pronunciation of the sound th and th. And No. 3 red lorry, yellow lorry. So red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry. This tongue twister will perfect your R and L. Red lorry, yellow lorry. So say these three tongue twisters every day, first a little bit slower, then go faster and faster and faster. [Fast versions of all three]. I'll put these tongue twisters at the end so you can screenshot them and practice every day.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Utilidad educativa y reto personal (tratar de decirlos rápido).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Enfocar toda la atención en la fonética y pronunciación clara
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina un problema común (pronunciación) con una solución lúdica (trabalenguas) y un factor de 'guardado' alto al ofrecer una lista al final.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que resaltan palabras clave y el título principal fijo al inicio.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Estudiantes de inglés como lengua extranjera (ESL).
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: 3 Trabalenguas para mejorar la pronunciación en Inglés
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Texto superpuesto 'IMPROVE YOUR PRONUNCIATION' y la creadora hablando directamente a cámara en un entorno natural.

videos_analizados.hook_template: How I took this (insert title) from 0 to (insert #) of (insert noun) in 1 week. I am only (insert metric) but I have became one of the
videos_analizados.nicho: Marketing de Redes Sociales / Crecimiento Digital
videos_analizados.escenario: Sala de estar moderna con un reloj de pared grande y un sofá, ambiente profesional pero cercano.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: How I took this creator from 0 to 50,000 followers in just one week.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural/Mixta - Bien iluminada, luz frontal suave que resalta el rostro.
equipamiento: Buena iluminación (aro de luz o ventana)
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Prueba social inmediata combinada con un marco de tiempo corto (1 semana) para generar alto impacto y curiosidad.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Caso de éxito + Tutorial estratégico.
videos_analizados.transiciones: Jump cuts constantes para eliminar silencios y mantener el ritmo.
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido y saturado, resaltando el cabello rojizo de la creadora.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Asombro inicial, confianza en la experta, comprensión de la lógica y anticipación por el siguiente paso.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: CTA de retención: 'Mañana te enseñaré cómo convertir esto en una marca de un millón de dólares'.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: How I took this creator from 0 to 50,000 followers in just one week. This is the best kept secret for rapid growth and retaining an audience. If you're new here, my name is Madison. I'm a social media strategist with almost 2 million followers and for the next 30 days, I'm giving away all my best kept secrets. Let me introduce you to The Viral Series. A social media series is the best way to encourage people to come back to your page. Therefore, growing your following at a rapid rate. My client and I came up with the concept of building a Temu guitar from scratch. Before the viewer even finishes part two, part three, or part four, they've probably left a like and they've probably left a comment. So now, after just 7 days, my incredible client is sitting at 50k. What happens next? Are people going to unfollow him? Is he going to stop getting views? Tomorrow, I'm going to teach you how to take a series and turn it into a million dollar brand. Love you.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alto valor educativo percibido; los usuarios lo guardan o comparten como referencia estratégica.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía constante sin distraer del mensaje.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza un resultado extremo verificable como gancho, establece autoridad rápidamente y utiliza un 'open loop' (final abierto) para obligar al espectador a seguirla para ver el siguiente video.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Títulos grandes como 'The Viral Series', '50k', 'Hi I'm Madison'.
videos_analizados.tiempo_produccion: 2-4 horas (incluyendo guion, grabación y edición detallada).
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Creadores de contenido, emprendedores y dueños de negocios digitales.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Estrategia de Crecimiento Viral: El Poder de las Series en Redes Sociales
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Captura de pantalla del perfil de Instagram 'grahambransfordmusic' mostrando el crecimiento de seguidores de 2,000 a 50,000.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: Day 1 of Starting Over my Whole Entire Life
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - luz suave de ventana que crea una atmósfera cálida y hogareña
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una promesa de transformación personal radical y vulnerabilidad inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Storytelling de superación personal (The Comeback)
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos entre actividades de la rutina
videos_analizados.color_grading: Cálido, suave, con tonos tierra y pasteles
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la tristeza y pérdida inicial hacia la esperanza, determinación y renovación personal.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Let's start over, shall we? (Invitación implícita a seguir su viaje)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Day 1 of starting over my whole entire life. Hi I'm Raegan. And a year ago my happy healthy five year long relationship ended badly. Like really badly. And when I lost him I also lost my city, my apartment, all my friends and any belief I still had that my relationship actually was happy or healthy. Fun right? And so over the last year I've honestly spent way too much time crying in my shower or rotting in my bed trying to remember who I was before and trying to figure out how I'm gonna rebuild my life now that I'm in the after. Plus therapy, like so much therapy. And somehow I ended up here, 27 and back at home, absolutely terrified and completely unsure of everything except this: it's time to move on now. Time to let go, time to build a life that's even bigger and better than the one I had to leave behind. So here's to new beginnings. Let's start over, shall we?
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Identificación personal; los usuarios lo envían a amigos que están pasando por situaciones similares.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Acompañar el tono emocional del relato sin opacar la voz
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Conecta con una experiencia universal (ruptura) y utiliza el formato de 'vlog de estilo de vida' altamente estético que gusta en Instagram. La vulnerabilidad honesta genera alta retención.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y el título principal en la parte superior.

videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Reinicio de vida tras ruptura amorosa - Día 1

videos_analizados.escenario: Apartamento moderno de rascacielos en Toronto, minimalista y ordenado.
videos_analizados.hook_texto: day in the life of a selfish woman
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Natural - Muy luminosa, proveniente de grandes ventanales.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un adjetivo peyorativo (selfish) para generar curiosidad y polarización inmediata.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Reencuadre de etiquetas negativas (Reclaiming labels).
videos_analizados.color_grading: Tonos limpios, luminosos y ligeramente desaturados para un look 'aesthetic'.
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Inicia con una tensión provocativa, pasa por una fase de reflexión lógica y termina en una sensación de paz y empoderamiento personal.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (busca engagement orgánico por debate).
videos_analizados.guion_completo: day in the life of a selfish woman. I'm 32 years old with no desire to get married or have children and people often ask me if I met the right person would I change my mind? the answer is no I won't. not wanting to be a mother has less to do with finding the right person than it does living a life free from avoidable stress. although the thought of trying to co-parent with a man where I have to shoulder the majority of the emotional and physical labor sounds like a nightmare. no one is immune to life's challenges but at 32 I finally feel like I'm at a place where I can do what I want when I want without answering to anyone and it feels freaking awesome. having a child would completely change that. being a mother is really hard and I don't wanna work hard. the idea of hard work has been glamorized by the institutions and people that benefit from a woman's labour the most. so I'll gladly take the easy way out if it means I don't have to stress about raising a child in a broken system that only rewards the smallest subset of the population if they fit neatly into a mould. and even then what's really the reward? most people don't have the privilege of self actualization because they're forced to live paycheck to paycheck grinding away unfulfilling jobs for just a few weeks of freedom a year if they're lucky. if I ever do choose to partner again that person will share the sentiment that being a selfish woman isn't a flaw. it's a conscious rejection of the societal expectation that women must sacrifice themselves for others to be worthy or valuable. and being selfish is a wonderful thing a woman can be.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Alta identificación en mujeres millennials que cuestionan la maternidad tradicional.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Reforzar la sensación de tranquilidad y vida sin estrés.
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Toca un tema altamente polarizante (child-free by choice) con una estética aspiracional. Desafía el estigma de la palabra 'egoísta'.
videos_analizados.emocion_principal: Validación (para quienes piensan igual) / Indignación (para sectores tradicionales).
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen toda la narración de la voz en off.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Mujeres de 25 a 40 años, interesadas en feminismo, lifestyle y salud mental.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Day in the life of a selfish woman (Reivindicación de la vida sin hijos)
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Mujer de espaldas mirando por un ventanal de un rascacielos con vista a la CN Tower en Toronto.

videos_analizados.hook_template: Welcome back to the day in the life of two (insert label) trying to build the next (insert business).
videos_analizados.escenario: Cocina de producción a gran escala y oficina/comedor para pruebas
videos_analizados.hook_texto: Welcome back to another day in the life of two guys building the next big protein cookie business.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Artificial - Luz blanca de cocina industrial
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de una estructura de 'Day in the life' combinada con una meta ambiciosa para generar autoridad y empatía.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Build in Public / Problem-Solution
videos_analizados.transiciones: Cortes directos (Jump cuts) y transiciones de movimiento rápidas
videos_analizados.color_grading: Limpio, brillante, colores naturales saturados para que la comida se vea apetitosa
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Comienza con energía, baja hacia la frustración/conflicto por las críticas, sube con la perseverancia y termina en triunfo y gratitud.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No hay un CTA explícito de venta, es un CTA de fidelización basado en el agradecimiento a la comunidad.
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Welcome back to another day in the life of two guys building the next big protein cookie business. Today we pulled off an industry first and it wasn't easy. Over the last few months we've been grinding behind the scenes because y'all let us know how much you hated the fact that we used seed oils in our cookies. We get it, we heard you loud and clear. Here's the deal: the seed oils came from margarine, which is super common in baked goods and protein snacks you see on the shelves today. But we wanted to set a new standard. So back to the drawing board we went, testing different fat bases and let me tell you, we failed a lot. Like a lot a lot. But after months of trial and error and sacrificing way too many cookies, we nailed it. Our protein cookies are now made with avocado oil, making us the only protein cookie in the industry using this fat. This year we're here to push the limits, challenge ourselves and keep raising the bar for protein snacks. And it's all thanks to you for continuing to push us to be the best that we can be.
videos_analizados.estilo_escritura: Directo, transparente y orientado a la comunidad
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La validación de que las marcas escuchan al consumidor y el interés por un producto 'más saludable' (sin aceites de semillas).
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía alta y el ritmo de los cortes
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el sesgo de transparencia. Al admitir un 'error' o un ingrediente impopular y mostrar el proceso para cambiarlo, generan una confianza inmensa. Además, el nicho de 'anti-seed oils' es muy activo actualmente.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos palabra por palabra y capturas de pantalla de comentarios de TikTok/Instagram.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Personas fitness, biohackers, emprendedores y consumidores conscientes de los ingredientes.
nichos_compatibles: Cuidado de la piel (cambio de fórmulas)
Software (actualización basada en quejas de usuarios)
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Evolución de marca: Eliminando aceites de semillas en galletas de proteína
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Dos hombres musculosos posando en una cocina industrial mostrando bíceps, estableciendo el nicho de fitness de inmediato.

videos_analizados.hook_texto: Day in my life as a 10 year old model... heading to SF for a weekend shoot with Nike.
videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Natural en exteriores y luz de interiores de hoteles/restaurantes
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Contraste de edad y estatus (niño de 10 años trabajando con marca global)
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Vlog de estilo de vida aspiracional
videos_analizados.color_grading: Saturado y brillante, resaltando el rosa del cabello
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: De la anticipación emocionante al cansancio satisfactorio tras el éxito.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: No explícito (implícito en el seguimiento de la serie de videos)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Day in my life as a 10 year old model. LA models and I'm heading to SF for a weekend shoot with Nike. Bro this is my first time traveling for a shoot and I'm excited but I don't really know what to expect so yeah. I'm a book nerd. Don't judge. I read the whole flight. Then this cool bodyguard looking man picked us up in a black SUV. I mean it was pretty cool. He dropped us off at the Fairmont which is the nicest hotel ever. We checked in with the Nike team. Can't show you any of that. And we went to our hotel across the street. Got a goody bag that Mom stole everything from me. And then bro I went to this hilarious place called Mister Charlie which is like a rip off but Better McDonald. Everything is vegan and it went back to my fitting and went to every floor but the right floor. Got fitted. Can't show anything. Got my nails did and then dinner. Went to the best place on Earth. I'll make a different video for my foodie ratings. And then we saw this circus comedian man. Bro he was so funny. And then my mom made me wear these eye patches because you know my mom's a skincare... Woke up the next day Had to jaywalk slash run because my... I was mad at her. Got to set. Ate a plate of bacon for... Legit eight hours later. I played basketball and I ran all day with the Nike team. All the models were down to go for a crab house so I got to go again. Our moms made us take more photos. Woke up the next day at 5 AM. 5 AM people. That's freaking early. Got picked up by another... Started reading on the plane and then Bye bye.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: La naturaleza inusual de la vida del niño y el humor en su narración.
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener la energía y el estilo urbano del video
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Combina el formato 'Day in the Life' con un nicho aspiracional y poco común (niño modelo de élite). El uso de marcas grandes como Nike genera autoridad inmediata.
videos_analizados.texto_en_pantalla: Subtítulos dinámicos que siguen la narración y resaltan palabras clave como 'Nike', 'Bodyguard', 'Fairmont'.
videos_analizados.audiencia_objetivo: Padres, otros niños/adolescentes, interesados en moda y lifestyle.
videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Un día en la vida de un modelo infantil de 10 años para Nike
videos_analizados.hook_elemento_visual: Niño con cabello rosa en un coche con texto superpuesto y logos de agencias

videos_analizados.guion_completo: This is a day in the life of a baker, Valentine's Day cookie box edition. And I always like to put these in a heart-shaped box, but that kind of limits the number of cookies that I can do. So I ended up doing six different types of cookies, starting with these mini conversation hearts. Now last year I did mini conversation heart macarons which I thought were so cute and I wanted to basically do something similar but slightly different this year. So I ended up making a sugar cookie version and I just loved how these turned out. And then next I decided to make some chocolate linzers. I love linzer cookies because you can get so creative with the shapes and cookie cutters that you use and the filling. So for these I decided to use Nutella because clearly I'm a big fan. While I do think the Nutella filling was stunning, I filled some with the jam as well and I do think these actually taste better with jam. So I'm just giving you that disclaimer. Next up were my all-time favorites in these boxes and again it's one of the most simple recipes but they're chocolate covered peanut butter hearts. And they are truly so good. Now I like to make these super thick, kind of like the ratio of a mini Reese's. So they're literally two centimeters tall. And I like to add vanilla bean paste to the filling along with a little bit of extra salt. And then by using a good quality milk chocolate, I feel like these taste just like a Reese's but a little bit elevated. Because who doesn't love a nostalgic treat that tastes a little bit better than we remember? And somehow we're already running out of time, so check back in for part two.

videos_analizados.guion_completo: Come to work with me as a cake pop maker. Start off with a fuck ton of funfetti cake, straight from the oven into the mixer. Mix it up for about two to three minutes. These cake pops are going to be for a nine-year-old's birthday party, so they're going to be cute as fuck. Grab your sprinkles, add so many, you know we're going to just do it up. Mix those up and then get your dough into your molds. This is a cakesicle mold. Grab your sticks and then here is how I get them out of there super fast, you just gotta cut out that part. Here I am spilling sprinkles everywhere but we grab our vacuum and got back to business. Here she is. We're going to dip our cake pops brown, these are going to be ice cream cones. I used Colour Mill clay color. You're going to pipe on your lines. I'm not going to lie, this is a bitch, you have to practice but it's so worth it, they're so cute. And then we're going to pipe on our ice cream top. She loves pink so we chose a pink top. I love this color, I used so many colors to achieve it so please don't ask me what I used. And then we're going to add on our sprinkles that we spilled everywhere but here we are doing it right. And then here's the finished product and then we had to deliver them in town. Alright, bye friends!

videos_analizados.iluminacion: Mixta - Luz natural en el coche y dormitorio, luz fluorescente en el gimnasio.
videos_analizados.hook_tecnica: Uso de un patrón de 'viaje de transformación' interrumpido por un pensamiento honesto y perezoso con el que la audiencia se identifica.
videos_analizados.patron_viral: Documentación de proceso (Day X of...)
videos_analizados.color_grading: Natural con tonos cálidos
videos_analizados.arco_emocional: Resistencia -> Esfuerzo -> Sufrimiento Cómico -> Epifanía -> Determinación Silenciosa.
videos_analizados.call_to_action: Implícito (continuará en el Día 3)
videos_analizados.guion_completo: Day 2 of turning from fattie to baddie... and suddenly I don't wanna be a baddie anymore. Okay look, I've been somewhat fit before, I've had my gym era, I've tried to glow up at least 15 times, but this time I have to stick with it. The treadmill was loud but my knees were louder. At one point I genuinely forgot how to stretch. What even is a hamstring? And why does mine feel like it's 90 years old? Honestly, nothing about this gym experience was cute and every single part of me wanted to walk out. But then I remembered, this isn't just about the weight, it's about me showing up for myself. And listen, was I dying? Absolutely. But I showed up and that's more than I did yesterday. One thing I do know for sure: baddies don't quit, even when they wanna. Day 3, let's fix my eating.
videos_analizados.factor_compartir: Identificación personal ('Soy yo')
videos_analizados.musica_proposito: Mantener el ritmo y dar una sensación de 'película de mayoría de edad' (coming of age).
videos_analizados.por_que_es_viral: Utiliza el humor para desmitificar la perfección del fitness. La honestidad sobre el dolor físico y la pereza genera una conexión emocional fuerte con personas que se sienten intimidadas por el gimnasio.

videos_analizados.titulo_descriptivo: Transformación Fitness Realista: De Fattie a Baddie (Día 2)
